raw_text: ABERDEEN AND TEMAIR, 1ST MARQUESS OF (creat. 1915); 7TH EARL, creat. 1682; VISCT. FROMARTINE, BARON HADDO, METHLOC, TARVES, and KELLIE, 1682; Bart., 1642 (Scot.); VISCT. GORDON OF ABERDEEN, 1814 (U.K.), under which title he sits in the House of Lords; K.T. (1906); P.C. (1886); G.C.M.G. (1896); G.O.V.O.; JOHN CAMPBELL HAMILTON-GORDON, 3rd son of the 5th Earl.—B. 1847; succeeded his brother, 1870; lord-lieut. of Aberdeenshire, 1880; high commr. to gen.assem., Church of Scotland, 1861-6; lord-lieut. of Ireland, Feb. to Aug., 1886; gov-gen. of Canada, 1893 to 1896; representative of Canada on Pacific Cable Board, 1899 to 1900; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1906-1915.
ABBEYEWARDENE, HENRY WILKIN.—B. 1874; apptd. to cls. V. Ceylon civ. serv., Sept., 1923; extra off. asst., Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1923.

ABRAHAM, JOHN CONRAD, M.A. (Oxon.), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Keswick Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; asst. rec., Nyasaland, 1911; sp. serv., carrier transport, N.F.F., 1914-18; ment. in desps. three times; rec., 1920; admtn. off., 1st grade, 1923; ag. asst. ch. sec., Nov., 1926; examnr., Chiyao and Chinyanja languages.

ABRAHAMS, SIDNEY SOLOMON.—B. 1886; ed. Bedford Modern Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., L.L.B.; called to the bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909; Midland cirnrit; town mag., Zanzibar, June, 1915; crown counsel, Kenya, Nov., 1915, but did not proceed; seconded for serv. with Mesopotamia civil admtn., Jan., 1920; advoc.-gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, Apr., 1920; pres. ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, Jan., 1921; atty.-gen., Zanzibar, 30th June, 1922; atty.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1925; atty.-gen., Gold Coast, 1928; editor, English Reports, annotated, 1914-15; compiled (jointly), Zanzibar law rept., 1888-1918.

ABRAMS, ARTHUR BARROW.—Fellow of Cent. Assoc. of Accnts. (Incor.) England; asst. head storekeeper, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 9th Oct., 1905; head storekeeper, 26th May, 1906; offl. mem. town coun., Sept.-Oct., 1906; asst. ch. elk. and accnt., 12th May, 1907; ch. elk. and accnt., 18th Oct., 1908.

ABRAMSON, MAJOR ALBERT, C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in desps.; milly. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comm., and dir., land dept., 1920; ch. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. commr., northern dist., 1925.

ACHESON, A. B.—B. 1895; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in desps., Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte. on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte. on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-sec., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.
ACLAND, FREDERICK A.—B. 1861; journalist, England, U.S.A., and Canada; apptd. sec. dept. of lab., Canada, 1907; deputy min. of lab., 1908; King's printer, 15th June, 1921.

ACTON, ROGER DAVID.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; collr. of land rev., Perak, June, 1900; ast. sec. to rev., Perak, Jan., 1903; dep. regisr., sup. ot., Penang, May, 1907; ag. sheriff and deputy regisr., Penang, Dec., 1907; deputy regisr. and ast. offi. assignee, Apr., 1908; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Dec., 1910; promoted to Cls. III., Apr., 1911; 2nd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1911; ag. dist. judge, 1912; ag. regisr., sup. ot., Penang, Oct., 1912; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1913; ag. audr.-gen., Feb., 1914; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wesley, June, 1915; ag. censor, Penang, June, 1915; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1915; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Dec., 1916; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1920; dist. judge, Singapore, July, 1921; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, May, 1923; ag. police judge, Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Johore, June, 1924; ditto, Penang, Oct., 1925; judge, S.S., Jan., 1926; judge, Seremban, Mar., 1926.
ADAMS, HON. ALEXANDER SAMUEL.—B. 1861; ed., pub. schls. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; barrister and solr., 1883; crown solr., 1920; judge, N.Z. sup. ot., 1921.
ADAMS, C. H.—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. commr., Mar., 1917.

ADAMS, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1924), F.C.I.S. (Eng.) F.A.A. (Australia).—B. 1884; ed. Royal Orphanage, Wolverhampton; company sec., Sydney, N.S.W., 1908-1914; office of agt. gen. for N.S.W., London, 1915; liquidator of enemy firms, E.A. Prot., 1915; custodian of enemy property, occupied territory of German E. Africa, 1916 and Tanganyika territory, 1919; comptr. of cust., and custodian of enemy property, Tanganyika, 18th Jan., 9123; chmn., Tanganyika trade and infrn. local advisory committee.

ADAMS, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1878; ed. at Cranleigh Schl. and City of London Coll.; served articles, Quan. Survrs., London, 1891-1894; passed prelim. exam. Surv. Inst., 1896; Fellow of the Registd. Corp. Acctnts. (F.C.R.A.), 1915; tech. ast., accts. br., H.M. Office of Works, London, 1894-1895; accts. clk., civ.-engnr.-in-chf.'s dept., Admiralty, in charge, corresp. br., ag. on various occasions as ch. survr., 1896-1900; transf'd. to Simon's Bay Dkdy. Extension, Cape Colony, as senr. accts. clk., in charge accts. and corresp., S.C.E.'s Office, 1900-1905; served as agt. in Simon's Bay Dkdy. Post Rifles (Boer War), 1901; recd. comm. in Cape Gar. Artillery, Sept., 1902 prom. captain, Mar., 1905; ch. accts., Engnr., India, 1906-1910; ast. sec., Engnr., England, 1910-1912; accts. and oahr., Gold Coast Harbrs., Accra and Secondee, 1912-1918; rly. acctnt., in charge rly. and pub. wks. accts., Anglo-French occuptn., Togoland, 1918-1919; ast. rec.-gen., Gambia, 22nd May, 1919; senr. ast. recr. gen., 1st Jan., 1920; contrlr. food and fuel, July to Sept., 1920; J. P. and commr., et. of requets, 14th Sept., 1920; ag. rec.-gen., P.M.G., currency offr., and chmn., navigation and piloting by July to Oct., 1920, and July, 1921 to Jan., 1922; mem., exec. and leg. couns., July to Oct., 1921 and Sept., 1921, to Jan., 1922; ast. accts., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 9th Dec., 1922.

ADAMS, G. H., A.S.A.—B. 1891; C.A. office, Nov., 1911; temp. accts., Nyasaland, July, 1915; cashier, 1915; ast. commr., income tax, 1921; senr. ast. treas., Apr., 1925; ag. dep. treas. in 1921, 1925 and 1927.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL.—B. 1885; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; ag. supervisor of cts., Taiping, 1910-11; ast. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1914; supdt., land Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st ast. sec., 1919; ag. 1st ast. sec. to govt., 1920; ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, July, 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Sept., 1923; ag. regisr. titles, Perak, and chmn. sny. bd., Larut and Selama, Dec., 1923; chmn., cl. III., Nov., 1925; dist. offr., Larut and regisr. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925; ag.-dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1927.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc. 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Conferees of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offl. reps. on housing and town planning investigtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibts., Letchworth, 1905, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Govt., since 1914; hon. mem. Instn. of Mun. and Cnty. Engnr. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914, and mem. of coun., 1914-1918; mem. bd. of govs., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.
ADAMSON, ALFRED EDWARD.—B. 1871; ed. Godolphin schl.; survr., Kenya, 1911; ditto, Zanzibar, 1922.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed. in London; Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1905; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chmn., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925.
ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1901; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; B.A.(hons.), 1923; asst. dist. commr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924.

ADDISON, D'ARCY WENTWORTH, C.M.G. (1928), M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1st Dec., 1887; clk. of exec. coun., 1903; sec. to premier since 1903; ch. electoral offr., 1914; mag., 1918; admstr. of charitable funds, 1914-19; sec. for neglected children's dept., 1914-19; state organiser, visit of H.R.H. Prince of Wales, 1920 and the Duke and Duchess of York, 1927; under sec. for Tasmania.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTEN THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1869; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

ADERS, WALTER MANSFIELD.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; Marburg Univ., Prussia, Ph.D. (science honours) 1902; engaged on research work, Marine Biological Assoc., Plymouth, 1903; ast. to pathologist, Egyptian govt. hosp., Cairo, and curator of pathological museum, 1905; res. offr. of rinderpest serum station, Abassia, Cairo, 1906; priv. ast. and scientific sec. to dir. of quarantine bd., Alexandria, 1907; detailed to El Tor for research work re cause of dysentery amongst pilgrims; research work with Prof. Viguer Algers (marine biology); research work, Welcome Laboratory, Khartoum, 1909; London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1910; research work, Zanzibar, since 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for foreign affairs and the Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 2nd cls., for services rendered, 1912; ast. postal censor, 1914-1916; ch. censor, 1916-1918; lent to mil. authorities for work in Dar-es-Salaam and Kilwa, 1917; economic biologist, 1st June, 1917; at request of gov. of Tanganyika Territory proceeded to Dar-es-Salam to advise on anti-malarial work, 1921; author of numerous papers and reports on scientific subjects.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1918; ag.-capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, regar. of sup. ct., escheator gen., provost marshal, regar. gen., regar. of titles and legal adviser to gov't. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. commr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag.-atty-gen., 5th Dec., 1923 to 13th Apr., 1926; res. mag., Jamaica, 12th Oct., 1926.

AGASSIZ, EDWARD LEWIS.—B. 1879; office ast., Hong Kong, May, 1920; ag. crown solr. in addn., Aug., 1920; sec. to D.P.W., Jan., 1925; ag. offr. recr., Nov., 1925; offr. recr. and regar., trade marks and letters patent, Jan., 1927.

AHEARNE, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIO.—B. 1886; ed. Christian Schls., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S. Stiltns., 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; ast. supt., India, immigrants, Selangor, 1912; 1st ast. contrlr. of lab., Penang, 1913; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, 1914; dep. regar., sup. ot., Penang, 1916; dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1916; ast. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; ast. regar., impts. and expts. (war dept.), 1918; offr., ols. iv., Jan., 1919; ast. adviser to gov't of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1923; dep. contr. lab., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1923; ag. dep. contr. lab., Penang, Dec., 1923 and July, 1926; ag. contr. lab., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1926; ag. emigrn. commr. for Malaya, Madras, May, 1926; ag. dep. contr. lab., Penang, July, 1925; ag. contr. lab., Malaya in addn., Feb., 1926; ag. dep. contr. lab., Singapore, Nov., 1926.

AIKINS, ST JAMES A. M., KNT. BACH. (1914), K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Univ. of Toronto; graduated in arts; called to the bar, Ontario, 1878; Manitoba 1879; Q.C., 1884; counsel for the C.P.R.Co.at Winnipeg, 1881-1911; pres. Canadian Bar Assoc., 1914-1916; lieut.-gov., Manitoba, 1917; re-apptd. for a second term, 1921.

AINSWORTH, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Leeds Gram. Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1913; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. sec., sanitary bd., 1917; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; ag. dep. regar. and appraiser, sup. ot., 1919; ag. 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affr., 1919; sec. to the industrial employment of children commn. in addn., 1921; J.P., 1922; 2nd pol. mag. and 3rd ast. sec. for Chinese affr., 1922; ast. col. treas. and ast. assessor of rates, 1923; ch. ast., sec. for Chinese affr., 1925; ag. pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ast. sec., Chinese affr., 1927.

AITKEN, JAMES DAVIDSON, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. George Watson's Coll. Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; joined Ceylon pol. serv., Dec., 1912; ast. supt., pol., Jan. 1914; on mil'y. serv., 1917-19; supt., pol., Apr., 1927.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (1906).—B. 1885; ed. Giggleswick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; ast. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ot., Gambia, 1926.
AITKEN, JOHN BANKS.—B. 1903; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1925; ast. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1926.

AKBAR, MAAS THAJOON.—K.C., B.A., LL.B. Cantab., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924; ag. puisne just., Nov. to Dec., 1925; ag. atty.-gen., Dec., 1927; M.L.C.

AKTOYE, DANIEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Lagos Gram. Schl.; copyist to Queen's Advocate, Lagos, Aug., 1886; was fourteen years regar. of instruments affecting land; dep. regar. of births, deaths, marriages, and aliens, Jan., 1896; ag. regar. at intervals; clk. to commr. of lands (now dir. of surveys), Apl., 1901.

ALBUQUERQUE, JOHN PEDROSO D', M.A., (Cantab.), F.L.C., F.C.S. (Lond.).—B. 1865; ed. City of London Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; schol. nat. science tripes, part I, 1st cls., 1887, nat. science tripes, part II, 1st cls. (chem.), 1889; govt. prof. chem. and agrl. science, Barbados, 1890; consulting chem., Impl. dept. of agr. for W. Indies, and chem. in charge, sugar cane experiments, 1900; ag. commr., Impl. dept. of agr. for W. Indies on several occasions between 1900-1909; ag., dir., agr., Barbados, 1st Apr., 1928; dir., science and agr., Barbados, 1st Sept., 1925.

ALBURY, G. A.—B. 1865; entd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1898; Out Island commr. (1st div.), 1909; insp. instr., pro. tem., Jan., 1919; ag. stip. and circuit mag. for various periods from 1919-1923; ag. stip., circuit mag. and coroner in 1923, 1924, 1926, 1927 and 1928.
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island commr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 3rd div. commr., Jan., 1918.
ALBURY, STANLEY VICTOR STRATHMERE.—B. 1873; sidewalker, Bahamas, 1901; clk., post office, 1907; Out Island commr. (3rd div.), 1911; (2nd div.), 1912; supt. of hospital, 1917.

ALBURY, WILTON GRANT.—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apptd. 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., 8th June to 6th July and from 17th July, 1925.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl., Birmingham, and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1915; dist. commr., Savusavu, Kadavu, Colo East, Lautoka and Bua, Nov., 1915 to Jan., 1920; qual. in law, Oct., 1917; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920.
ALEXANDER, JOHN EDWARD—Ast. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct. 1912; lands offr., Nyasaland, 21st July, 1922.

ALLAN, KENNETH BRUCE, M.B., B.S. (Durham) Certif., London Schol. of Trop. Med. and Coll. of Med., Durham Univ.—Ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, May, 1911; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., 1916-17; transfd. as capt. on gen. list and attd. 1st K.A.R. Nyasaland Field Force, July, 1917 to Jan., 1919; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gambia, Mar., 1923; ag. senr. med. offr. on various occasions, 1923-26; senr. med. offr., Sept., 1926; J.P.; offr. mem., exec. and leg. couns.

ALLAN, PETER, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., Edin. M.D. (1920).—Res. med. offr., Royal Victoria Hosp. for consumption, Edinburgh, 1911-12; clin. astt. med. out-patient dept., Royal Infirmary, Edinburgh, 1912; astt. tuberculosis offr., boroughs of Paddington and Kensington, 1912-13; 1st med. spt., Ramsley Hill Sanatorium, Birmingham, 1913-1920; capt., R.A.M.C., 1917-18; med. inspr., dept. of pub. health, Union of South Africa, 1920; med. astt., tuberculosis sanatorium, Nelspoort, C.P., 1928; seconded to the S. African Inst. for Med. Research for duties in connection with tuberculosis research, 1st Apr., 1927; author of "An investigation into the increased mortality from pulmonary tuberculosis, 1914-1920."

ALLARD, JULES.—B. 1869; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; apptd. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1906; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotory of the superior ct., Montreal, 1919.

ALLARDYCE, SIR W. L., G.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1902). Knt. of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1916).—B. 1861; clk. and interp. Provincial Dept., Fiji, 1879; transfd. to Rotuma, 1882; stip. mag., July, 1882; astt. native commr. and stip. mag. and inspr. of native taxes, Kadavu, Jan., 1890; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1893; native lands commr. and mem. legis. coun., 1894; native commr., 1896; astt. col. sec. and receiv.-gen., Dec., 1898; deputy gov., 14-20 Feb., 1901; adminstrd. govt., 18th July, 1901, to 9th Sept., 1902; col. sec. and receiv.-gen., 18th July, 1902; gov., Falkland Is., 1904; gov., Bahamas, 28th Dec., 1914; assumed govt. 15th June, 1915; gov., Tasmania, 1920; gov., Newfoundland, 1st Sept., 1922; assumed govt., 1st Dec., 1922; ret., Apr., 1928; editor of native newspaper "Na Mata," 1890-99; author of a digest of Native Regulations, 1877-1900; attended first Fed. Conf. on Education, 1907.

ALLDER, WM. HOWELL.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd astt. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit offr., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comm., as astt. in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine comm., 1903, to West Indian quarantine confec., 1904, and to confer on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comm., 1920.

ALLEN, FREDERICK HAROLD, M.I.Mech.E., A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1890; engnr. astt., Port of London Authority, 1910-19; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1919; dist. engnr., Iorin, Nigeria, 1923; exec. engnr., 1924; native admistr. engnr., 1925; mech. engnr., Br. Guiana, 1926.
ALLEN, H.—Tempy. astt. dist. commr., R.A.P., Apl., 1919; astt. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920.

ALLEN, H. T.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; also clk. toconcessions and finance comm., Sept., 1910 to June, 1916; promoted, under order-in-council of 1910, 2nd astt. clk., C.O., 16th June, 1916; astt. sec. to trade relations comm., Aug., 1917; sec., Dependencies of Falkland Islands comm., Jan., 1918; sec., raw materials board, Dec., 1918; astt. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Lord Milner), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A. Camb.—B. 1855; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Div., New Zealand Garrison Art., 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educ., 1912-15; min. of finance and min. of external affairs, 1919-20; high commr. in England, 1920-26; M.L.C., 1927.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B. 1901; entered C.O., 1917; apptd., after exam., astt. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

ALLEN, LUCIEN ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. astt. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd astt. dist. offr., Tapah, Feb., 1915; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1915 to Sept., 1918; passed cadet, June, 1915; astt. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; astt. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. res., Brunei, Mar., 1921; astt. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; ag. ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1924; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; as. astt. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1925 and Sept., 1926; ag. offr. assignee and pub. trustee, F.M.S., Sept., 1926; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Sept., 1926; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Feb., 1929; ag. Br. adviser, Perlis, May, 1928.

ALLEN, MORRIS ANTHONY VERMONT.—B. 1875; ed. Westminster Schl.; jun. offr., Perak, June, 1895; astt. forest offr., Kinta, Jan., 1900; inspr., mines, Perak, Aug., 1901; astt. warden, mines, Perak, Mar., 1905; warden, mines, Selangor, July, 1910; ag. senr. warden, mines, F.M.S., Nov., 1927 to June, 1928.

ALLEN, PERCY TOHELL.—B. 1878; ed. at Highgate Schol., Christ's Coll., Camb., (hons. class. tripes, 1899); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1903; AG. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang Sept., 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Matang, June, 1906; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Nov., 1907 and Nov., 1909; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese and supt. of prisons, Penang, Feb., 1909; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Apr., 1909; ag. mag., Seremban, Apr., 1912; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese and munic. commnr., Penang, Oct., 1913; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan. and May, 1917; ag. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1917; ag. insp. of prisons, S.S. in addn., Jan., 1918; ag. rev. aud., Sel., N.S. and Pahang, April, 1919; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Sept., 1919; ag. state treas., Selangor and asst. treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; sec. sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and temp. off. mems., leg. coun., S.S., May, 1920; ag. regis., titles, Perak and supt., convict estab., Taiping, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, in add., May, 1923; ag. chmn., many bd., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1923 and July, 1924; asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; sec., res., Perak, Mar., 1925; regis., companies and offl. assignee, S.S. in addn., 1925; asst., Chinese sect., July, 1926; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., F.M.S., 1926; sec., ditto, Feb., 1927

ALLEN, COL. STEPHEN SHEPHERD, C.M.G. (1919), D.S.O. (1917), with bar (1918).—Volunteer serv., Cambridge Univ., 1901-04, and in N.Z., 1911-15; left N.Z. with 6th reinforcements N.Z. Exped. Force, with rank of major, 1915; lieut.-col., Jan., 1917; in comd. 2nd Batt. Auckland Regt., Dec., 1916 to June, 1919; commdr. Auckland Infantry Brigade (T.F.), Feb., 1920 to May, 1925; col., Feb., 1920; in comd., Haaraki Regt., Sept., 1926 to April, 1928; res. of offr., Apr., 1928; admstr., W. Samoa, May, 1928; four termsment, in despr.; hon. A.D.C. to gov.-genl. of N.Z., Jan. 1925 to May, 1928.

ALLEYNE, JAMES DOUGLAS, M.D. (Tufts Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIS, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. clk., Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv. to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.
ALLISON, ALFRED ERNEST.—B. 1869; ed. Christchurch Boy's High Schl.; ent. N.Z. govt. serv., 1886; commnr., govt. life insurance dept., 1926.

ALLISON, WALTER EDWARD, A.M.Inst.T.—Mech. engrnr., Br. Honduras rly., Aug., 1913; supt. and traffic man., 1914-18; seconded to German E. Africa (Tanganyika Territory), as asst. traffic man., Jan., 1919; traffic supt., Apr., 1928.

ALLISON, WILLIAM FRANCIS DUDLEY, A.M.I. Mech.E., M.I.Loco. E.M.Inst.T.—B. 1892; ed. Glasgow High Schl. and R. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; N.B.R. Co. and L. and S.W.R., 1906; inspgr. engr., crown agts., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nigerian rly., 1911; seconded as loco. supt., mil. rly., Togoland, 1915; asst. loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1918; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., 1922.
ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1885; German East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade admstve. offr., 1st Aug., 1922.

ALUWIHARK, RICHARD.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., Oct., 1920; office asst. to dir. of agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Ft. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924; 2nd landing survr., cust., 1926.

AMBLER, LLOYD EDWARD.—B. 1894; elk. postal dept., Fiji, 1st June, 1909; 4th cls. elk., lands dept., Aug., 1913; served with 1st Batt., K.R.C.R., France, 1916-17; Indian Army Res. (commd., Oct., 1917); Baluchistan, 1917-19; 3rd cls. elk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1916; attd., lands dept., Fiji, Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd cls. elk. and sec., mining bd., Oct., 1919-Oct., 1920; ag. sub-impr. constab. (during Indian strike), Feb., 1920; ag. 1st cls. elk. and elk., exec. and leg. couns., Jan. to Apr., 1921; ag. ch. elk., Sept., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. ch. elk. and asst., Oct., 1926 to Mar., 1927 and May to Sept., 1927.
AMEDROZ, CAPT. REGINALD THORNTON, R.N. (ret'd.)—Harbmr., Falkland Is., Apr., 1926.
AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926; office astt. govt. agt., E. Prov., Dec., 1927.

AMERY, Lieut.-Col., L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Souls, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1894-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Impl. War Coun. at Versailles, and as astt. sec. to the War Cabinet and Impl. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Mar., 1920; First Lord of the Admry., Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Cola., 7th Nov., 1924; also S. of S. for Dominion affrs., 9th July, 1925; capt. 14th Ist B'ham Batt., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMYOT, Lt.-Col. JOHN A., C.M.G., M.D., D.A., D.M.S.—Sanitation, second Canadian divn. area, 1914; O.C., man. sec., 1st Canadian divn., France, ch. adviser, man., with Canadian army in France, 1916; twice ment. in despr.; dep. min., dept. of health, Canada, 1919.
ANDERSON, C. R.—Ast. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Nov., 1916; A.P.O., cadet, 25th Aug., 1920; confirmed in appt. as A.P.O., 1st Jan., 1921.
ANDERSON, E. L. B.—Ast. dist. commnr., K.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1925.

ANDERSON, GEORGE BASTLIE.—B. 1880; ed. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; elk., acct.-gen'l's dept., G.P.O., London, 1896; elk., war office, Jan., 1900; 2nd cls. elk., G.P.O., Johannesburg, Dec., 1902; transf'd. to exec. and legis. councils dept.; 2nd cls. elk., June, 1903; 1st cls. elk., July, 1905; acct.'s elk. and cashier, Transvaal agt.-general's office, London, Oct., 1907; ast. asst. acct., Apl., 1908; elk., P.W.D., Nyasaland, June, 1909; chief elk., secretariat, Apl., 1914; ag. ast. chief sec., Aug., 1916; on re-organisation of secretariat appkd. 2nd ast. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. 1st ast. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June to

ANDERSON, HON. GEORGE JAMES.—B. 1860; ed. Lawrence Gram. Sch.; mem., N.Z. parl., since 1908; chmn. labour bills comtee., five years; chmn., library comtee., eight years; min. of internal affairs, 1920-21; min. of lab., 1921; min. of mines, 1921; min. of marine, 1922-26.

ANDERSON, MAXWELL HENDRY.—C.B.E. (mil.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.); entered Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; recd. thanks of Adm'y. for valuable series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; instructor, R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; employed on special services; recd. thanks of Adm'y. for services rendered to naval prize fund and at courts martial; specially promoted capt., Apr., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 9th May, 1919; ag. chief just., and col. sec. on several occasions.

ANDERSON, ROBERT MOWBRAY.—Entd. govt. printing dept., St. Vincent, 6th Feb., 1886; chief govt. printer, July, 1894; steward, col. hosp., Aug., 1901; mem. of comtee. to inquire into admn. of poor relief, Aug., 1904; clk. to regis., sup. ct., and registr. of B., M. and D. for dist. L., Nov., 1904; temp. asst. landing waiter for spec. excise duties, Nov., 1904; edited first "Illustrated Handbook of St. Vincent," 1907.
ANDERSON, R. T. H.—Asst.-supt. of police, E.A.P., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, 1920.

ANDERSON, THOMAS EDWARD.—B. 1877; excise offr., B. Honduras, Dec., 1902; 2nd grade customs and excise offr., July, 1905; ag. 1st grade customs and excise offr., from April, 1904; confirmed March, 1906, rlyw. acctnt., 1st Aug., 1909; on completion of rlyw. construction reverted to substantive appt., 7th Sept., 1910; ag. chief clk., etc., treasy. and customs Jept., 20th May to 7th Sept., 1911.
ANDERSON, THOMAS JOHN, M.A., B.Sc., (Hons. Edin.) F.Z.S., F.S.E.—Entomologist, agric. dept., Kenya, May, 1908.
ANDRE, WILFRID JOSEPH.—B. 1890; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1912; mag., St. Andrew, &c., Trinidad, Nov., 1923; mag., Caroni, Dec., 1924.

ANDREW, ROBERT CHARLES.—Ed. Liverpool Inst., Harper-Adams Agric. Coll., Newport, and Liverpool Univ.; B.Sc. (agric., London); National Diploma in agric.; mem. (by exam.) of Surv.'s Institn.; lecturer under the East Sussex county council, 1905 to 1910; foreign experience, 1911; asst. supt. of agric., N. Nigeria, 24th April, 1912.

ANDREWS, NORMAN FREDERICK STEWART.—B. 1902; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and Oxford; schol., Brasenose Coll., 1919; Bridgeman Prize, 1923, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1924, B.A., 1924; cadet, adminstr., Uganda, 1925; asst. dist. offr., 1927; temp. serving in C.O., 1927-28.

ANGLIN, RT. HON. FRANCIS ALEXANDER, P.C. (1925).—B. 1865; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Univ. of Ottawa; called to the bar, 1888; K.C. 1902; apptd. sen. puisne judge, exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1904; puisne judge, supreme ct. of Canada, 1909; ch. just., sup. ct., Canada, 1924; dep. of gov.gen.

ANNECKE, D. H. SIEGFRIED.—B. 1895; ed. Grey Univ. Coll.; Bloemfontein; B.A. (lit. and phil.), 1914; Univ. C.T., M.A. (physiology), 1918; Guy's Hosp., Lond., 1919-23, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1921; all house appts., Guy's, 1921; M.B., B.S., Univ. Durham, 1924; D.P.H.; and B. Hy., Univ. Durham, 1925; research schl. and asst. dept. protozoology, Lond. Schl. Hyg. Trop. Med., 1925-28; D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), 1927; joint educ. grant, govt. S.A. and Health Section L. of N. for study of anti-malarial methods in Italy, 1927; senr. govt. pathologist, Union health dept., Durban, 1928.

ANNETTS, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1888; Ed. Magdalen Coll., schl., Oxford, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1910; asst. master, preparatory schl., Repton, 1910-11; served in Oxfordshire educon. comtee.'s office, and inspr. of evening schl., Oxfordshire county council, 1911-1912; apptd. junr. supt., educon. dept., Northern Nigeria, 12th Sept., 1912; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; ag. dir. educon., 4th Apr., 1920; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1919; dep. dir., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. dir., 1920 and 1922.

ANTHONY, EDWIN AUGUSTUS.—Clk., ch. mag's office, Tembuland and Transkei, 2nd May, 1894; clk. and dept. audr. of rev. atty.-gen's. office, 28th Mar., 1897; examr. of scots., July 1897; ag. ch. clk., pol. branch, 1st Mar. to 30th Apr., 1902; ag. bookkeeper, guardian's fund, master's office, from 16th Jan., 1903; ag. 1st clk., Simon Town, 5th to 29th Sept., 1904; clk., civ. comnscr., Cape, 30th Sept., 1904; ch. paymr's. office, treasy., 1st July, 1908; regiar. of friendly societies and treasy. rep., Cape Town, Sept., 1918; civ. comnscr., Cape, Mar., 1921.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.L.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schl.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engr., F.M.S. Rly., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust., Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rlya., 1925; ag. ch. engrnr. (design), C.A., Nov., 1925 to Dec., 1926.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E., B.A.—B. 1893; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhib., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps., Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes, pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd., under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; asst. prte. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1925; seconded as prte. sec. to gov., Ceylon, July, 1927.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1898).—B. 1853; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; 2nd class in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1878; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May 3rd, 1877; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 5th Dec., 1880, to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1885; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, to 6th Feb., 1896; and to Earl Granville, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1896; administrator the govt. of St. Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, 1st Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; princ. clk., 1896; asst. under-secr. of state, Oct., 1898; crown agent for the cols., 1909; retired, 1918. AOTEA-ROA (N.Z.) 1st Bishop of (founded 1928, covering Maoris of N.Z.), RIGHT REV. FREDERICK AUGUSTUS BENNETT, L.Th. (1897).—Many years' service among Maoris; consecrated bishop, with addnl. title of suffragan bishop to bishop of Waipu, 1928.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1916; junr. asst. col. sec., 13th May to 30th Sept., 1915; travelling comsnr., Nasawam, 1st Oct., 1915 to 31st May, 1916, and from 12th Apr., 1917; ag. dist. comsnr., Kwahu, 1919.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1920), C.M.G. (1913).—Asst. collr., East Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, June, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. comsnr., Ist Apr., 1907; spec. sec., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1909; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cuthbert Paul Grant by the R.G.S., 1918, for surveys in E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinian triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa; transf. to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admst. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. comsnr., 31st Mar., 1913; comsnr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 16th May, 1914; assumed govt., 22nd May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Shimber Berri, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Medal and clasp); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st class); govt., Somaliland Prot., 4th Nov., 1919; directed operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting in complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; govt. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., 6th Sept., 1922; govt., Sudan, 1924.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1899; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st cls. certifs.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, specialist course, as torpedo lieut., 1915; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1915-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd cls.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. admv., H.M.S. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; apptd. under reconstruction scheme asst. prin., min. of lab., Sept., 1920; temp. asst. to C.O. as asst. prin., Dec., 1921; seconded for serv. as sec. to business mission to Australia, 12th July, 1928.
ARCHER, W. J.—Supt., govt. printing office, Cyprus, 27th May, 1902.
ARCHER-BETHAM, A., F.R.I.B.A.—Asst. consultg. archt., govt. Madras, India, 1920-21; archt., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, May, 1925.
ARCHIBALD, CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1869; entd. Trinidad civ. ser., Jan., 1892; cashier, Trinidad govt. rly., 1906; asstnt., 1919.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1885; recd. pmry. educn. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1905; also grad. from the Nova Scotia hort. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scien. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and experimt. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Exptn. Farm, Ottawa; ag. dir. of Exptn. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.

ARCHIBALD, MUNGO TENNENT, M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow (M.A.), and Peaub. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., May, 1910; ditto, Batticaloa, May, 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1912; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1913; spec. comsnr. in connection with riots, June, 1916; asst. land settm. offr., Aug., 1916; on military duty, 1916; asst. land settm. offr., Aug., 1919; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Dec., 1922; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1923; asst. sttlm. offr., July, 1923; ag. sttlm. offr. for various periods, 1924, 1925 and 1927.

ARMITAGE, CAPT. SIR CECIL HAMILTON, K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1911), D.S.O. (1901).—Asst. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1894; served in Ashanti exped., 1896-6 (star); Northern Territories (Neutral Zone), 1897 (medal and clasp); inspr., 1898; priv. sec. to gov. of G. Coast and clk. of leg. and exec. couns., 1899-1900; Ashanti campaign, 1900 (ag. res. during siege of Coomassie, D.S.O., medal and clasp); trav. comsnr., 1901; comsnr. of S. Province of Ashanti, July, 1901; comdt. of Ashanti Mines volunteers with rank of major, Nov., 1905; ag. chief comsnr. of Ashanti, Oct., 1901, to end of year, Apr. to Oct., 1906, Dec., 1907, June 1908, and from June to Dec., 1909; chief comsnr. of Northern Territories, 28th Jan., 1910; gov., Gambia, 20th Nov., 1920; assumed govt., 3rd Jan., 1921.

ARMSTRONG, ROBERT.—B. 1879; ed. Friends' Schl., Great Ayton, Yorks and Durham Univ.; diploma in agric., Armstrong Coll., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, in Univ. of Durham; lecturer, schl. of agric., Egyptian govt. 1906-1910; lecturer and organiser in agric. for the Hampshire County Council; ast. dir. of agric., Zanzibar, 6th Feb., 1913; ag. dir. of agric., 28th Oct., 1915 to 13th Aug., 1916; ag. chief supply offr., 16th Sept. to 15th Nov., 1918, and 30th Mar., 1919; ag. admstr.-gen., 10th Nov., 1919 to 22nd Jan., 1920; ag. dir., agr., 30th Mar., 1919 to 13th Jan., 1920, and 30th Nov., 1923 to 10th May, 1924; ag. dir. of agr. and ch. supply offr., 7th Feb. to 16th Nov., 1922; ag. dir., agr. and offr. mem., leg. coun., 23rd Jan. to 17th July, 1926.

ARNDT, CARL EVAN.—B. 1896; on milly. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Anuradhapure Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1921; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Aug., 1922; extra office asst., Kandy kachcheri, Nov., 1923; off. asst., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1925; dep. rubber contr., Apr., 1926; ast. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Sept., 1928.

ARNETT, EDWARD JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), B.A. (1898).—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's schl. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; classical tripes; called to the bar, 1912; C.A. office, 1902; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 1903; 3rd cls. res., 1906; 2nd cls. res., 1909; 1st cls. res., 1912; ag. heut.-govt., N. Prov., Nigeria, Oct., 1920.

ARNOLD, 1ST BARON (creat. 1924), RT. HON. SYDNEY ARNOLD.—B. 1878; was partly, priv. sec. to the pres. of the bd. of educn. and to the finan. sec. to the treas., 1914; lib. M.P. for Holmfirth and later for Penistone, 1912-21; partly, under sec. for the coll., Jan., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1924.

ARTHUR, JAMES STARTIN WILLS.—B. 1881; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1904); cadet, S. Stittms., Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1907; ag. asst. treas., Singapore, June, 1909; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1910; attached to col. audit office, Feb. Mar., 1911; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Aug., 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1912; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Mar., 1915; offr., cls. IV, May, 1916; seconded as asst. adviser, Kedah, May, 1916; ag. temp. as adviser in addn., Feb., Mar., 1917; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, May, 1922; dist. judge, Penang, Oct., 1922; dep. pres., mun. comms., Penang, May, 1923; dist. judge, Malacca, Jan., 1924.
ARUMUGUM, JOSEPH NALLAH, B.Sc.(Lond.)—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura knch., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. Prov., May, 1924; pol. mag., Chilaw, Aug., 1927.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expdn., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.
ASHTON, HUGH.—B. 1878; served in S. African War; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1906; inspr., 1914; served in European War with native labour contingent; asst. comantr., Basutoland, 1923.

ASHWORTH, HENRY TWEDDLE, M.M.—B. 1836; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1902; on milly. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary clk. exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to C.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept., 1922.

ASPINALL, SIR ALGERNON EDWARD, KT. BACH. (1928), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comtese since 1908 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; hon. treas. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comtese on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comtese., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agr. Coll. comtese., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comtese., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comtese., 1923.

ASSER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH JOHN, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), C.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1867; served with Nile expdn., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expdn., Soudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commdt., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922; ret., 1927.

ASTON, ARTHUR VINCENT.—B. 1896; ed. King’s Schl., Chester, and Queen’s Coll., Oxford (open maths. scholar); R.F.A., 1915-18, M.C., 1917; apptd., cadet, M.C.S., 1919; ag. asst. to senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, June, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, Sept., 1920; ag. harmbr., Malacca, Mar., 1922; ag. collr., income tax, Malacca, Apr., 1922; ag. 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Dec., 1924.

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUCHLAND, JUNR.—B. 1886; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; elk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry commsnr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regiar., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. “A” and “B,” Antigua, 6th Oct., 1917; mag. dist. B., Antigua, 1st July, 1924; city commsnr., St. John’s, Antigua, Nov., 1925.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, K.G. (1928), G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), G.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commodr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923; Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.

ATKINSON, HON. LLANELYN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1867; ed. Church Grammar Schl., Launceston, Tas., and Melbourne Univ.; called to the bar, 1893; el. mem. of H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Willmot (Tas.), Dec., 1906; vice-pres. of exec. coun., C. of A., 1923-26.
ATKINSON, VIVIAN BUCHANAN.—B. 1886; N. Staffs. Rly. Co., 1907; A.M.I.C.E., 1916; ast. engnr., Uganda Rly., Jan., 1912; dist. engnr., Tanganyika rly., Oct., 1920.
ATTERBURY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921.

AUBER, EGERTON LANCELOT.—B. 1889; ed. Wesleyan High Schl., Freetown, Sierra Leone; supernumy. clk., cust. (indoor), Gambia, Feb., 1908; 3rd clk., treasy., 19th Nov., 1908; 2nd clk., 19th Nov., 1911; regraded 4th grade clk., 1915; 3rd grade clk., 1st July, 1916; 2nd grade clk., 1st Dec., 1918; oh. clk., treasy., 26th Oct., 1920; seconded for duty as marine acctnt., 3rd Feb. to 31st Dec., 1927.

AUCHINLECK, GILBERT GRAHAME.—B.Sc. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hons. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-06; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl., 1908-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; ast. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric. and regiar., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Confe. in London, 1920; divnl. agric. offr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of agric. development, 1921-22; dep. dir., agric., G. Coast, Aug., 1926; sent to F.M.S., Java and Sumatra to rept. on palm oil industry, Apr. 1926 to Jan., 1927; del. to W. African agric. confe., Nigeria, Mar., 1927; author of papers on land stintm. and various trop. agric. subjects; dir., agric., Dec., 1928.

AUCKLAND, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ALFRED WALTER AVENILL, D.D.—Ed. St. John’s Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. Th. sch.), 1887; M.A., 1891; Ely Coll., 1888; deacon, 1888; priest, 1889, Lon.; formerly curate of St. George’s, Hanover Square, 1888-91; Holy Trinity, Dalston, Middlesex, 1891-94; vicar of St. Michael’s, Christchurch, N. Zealand, 1894-1909; chaplain of hosps., Christchurch, 1895-1909; hon. can. of Christchurch Cathedral, 1902-1909; archdeacon of Akaroa, 1903-1909; bishop of Waiapu, 1910-1913; bishop of Auckland, 1913; primatc and archbishop of N.Z., 1926.

AUDEN, FRANCIS THOMAS.—B. 1869; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1903; M.B., 1891; civil surg., S. African war, 1900-1901 (medal and clasp); dist. commr. and govt. med. offr., Turks and Caicos Islands, Dec., 1914; med. offr., Jamaica, 1917; Kenya, 1919; Bahamas, 1924; Cayman Is., 1925; St. Vincent, 1926; Seychelles, 1927.
AUDETTE, HON. LOUIS ARTHUR, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1866; apptd. regis. of exchequer et. of Canada, 1887; ast. judge, exchequer et., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.
AUNE, EDNA.—B. 1874; survr., grade I, rev. survr., branch, F.M.S., June, 1909; ast. supt., F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ast. senr. ast. supt., Kinta, Oct., 1926; senr. ast. supt., Kinta, Oct., 1926.

AVICE, TRISTAN, O.B.E. (1929).—Ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; matric., London Univ., 1888; ent. civ. serv. Mauritius, 1893; 6th cls. clk., 1894; 6th ditto, Apr., 1899; 4th ditto, May, 1899; 2nd ditto, 1903; oh. clk., 1905; ast. regis.-gen., 1912; regis.-gen., rec. of registration, dues, regis. of companies, 1920; nom. mem., coun. of govt., May, 1926.

AYLESWORTH, HON. SIR ALLEN BRISTOL, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), M.A. (1875).—B. 1854; educ. at Newburgh high school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prizeman; bencher of law soc. of Upper Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s commrs. for settlmt. of Alaska boundary, 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North York, 1904; postm.-gen. of Canada, 1905; min. of just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbitn. at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfolio and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.

AYTON, A. L.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899; clk. in charge of acct., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 29th Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in acct. dept., 29th Jan., 1920; ag. astt. prin., 16th Aug., 1920; sec. to "Discovery" comttee., 1925; ag. prin., 9th Jan., 1926.

BABER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; copyist, survey dept., B. Honduras, 1905; apprentice, ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. soc.'s dept., Nov., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. clk. ch. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of schls. and clk. to bd. of educn., Nov., 1916; senr. clk., col. soc.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. clk., etc., for various periods, 1917-21; ag. astt. col. sec., Nov., 1921; ag. priv. sec. to Sir Eyre Hutson, May-Aug., 1922; oh. clk., Apr., 1921; astt. col. sec., June, 1923.

BACKWELL, H. F.—B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. scholarships), and at King's Coll., Camb., exhib.; ast. mast., Portsmouth gram. schl., 1907-8; ast. nat., N. Nigeria, 19th Dec., 1908; ast. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., 29th Aug., 1917.

BADDELEY, SIR FRANK MORRISH, KT. B.A. (1928), C.M.G. (1925).—B.A., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. tripes, 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; passed final-exam. in cantonese, Dec., 1900; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1918; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, May, 1901; ditto, Serendah, Nov., 1901; read. thanks of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd astt. prot. of chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Xmas Is., Oct., 1906; ag. offr. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apptd. a currency commr., May, 1907; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1908; supt., govt. monopolies dept., S. Stiltns., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Stiltns., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; under-sec., S. Stiltns., July, 1921; ag. col. sec., S. Stiltns., Apr.-Sept., 1922, May-Oct., 1923 and from Sept.-Nov., 1924; oh. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, in 1925 and 1927; govt. dep. for various periods in 1925, 1926 and 1927.

BAGENAL, CHARLES JAMES, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. St. Mark's Sch., Windsor, Camborne Sch., of Mines; served Indl. Yeomanry, Boer War, 1899-1901; Union of S. Africa dept. of mines and industries, 1901; served German S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; ment. in desps.; German E. Africa campaign, 1915-18; ment. in desps.; dist. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1917; senr. commr., 1920; prov. commr., 1926.

BAGSHAWE, ARTHUR WILLIAM GARRARD.—C.M.G. (1915), B.A., M.B., B.C., D.P.H., Camb., M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P., Lond.; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Caius Coll., Camb., served in resident hosp. appts., 1896-1899; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Oct., 1900; served as med. offr. in Lango expeditn., 1901; served with Anglo-German bndry. commr., 1902-4; employed in sleeping sickness extended investigations in Uganda, Apr., 1906, to May, 1907; director of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908.
BALBY, JOHN F.—Dir. of botanic gardens, Adelaide, S. Australia, 1917; formerly dir. of Brisbane gardens.
BALWARD, A. N.—Astt. dist. commr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec., native affrs. dept., 1926; pvt. sec. to ag. gov., 1927.

BAIN, NORMAN KERR, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, S. Stiltns., Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. astt. to res., Malacca, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., 31st Mar., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 29th Mar., 1914; ag. astt. to res. and harbrmr., Malacca, Mar., 1918; ag. agent, food controller in addition, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Port Dickson, Negri-Sembilan, Jan., 1921; ag. 2nd mag., Kinta, July, 1921; mag., Ipoh, Aug., 1921; oh. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Aug., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1923; pol. mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; ag. dist. judge, Penang and Singapore, Sept., 1924; offr. 2nd mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; offr. registr., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1925; offr. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1925; ag. contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., and ag. valr-gen., F.M.S. and town planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1928.

BAINES, DENNIS LYNCH, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Wellington and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; astt. collr., Uganda prot., May, 1904; transf. to German E. Africa, 1st July, 1916; senr. commr., 1st Apr., 1920.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE.—B.A., Oxon.; cadet, S. Stiltns., Dec., 1908; attched. to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 1912; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, Apr. and Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., B. Pulau, 1913; joined offr. cadet batt., July, 1916; demob. June, 1919; officiating astt. supt., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating astt. offr. assignee and dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Aug. 1919; ag. 2nd mag., Aug., 1919; capt., Penang volunteers, Nov., 1919; supernmy. offr., cls. III, Oct., 1920; ag. astt. offr. assignee, Penang, Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1921; collr., land rev. and astt. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923; commr., trade and cust., Johore, June, 1927; ag. asst. adviser, Muar, July, 1927; offr. cls. Ib, Deo., 1927; comsnr., trade and cust., Johore, 1928.
BAKER, CHARLES MATTHEW, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Felsted Sch., Essex; supt. of educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

BAKER, ERNEST LAIDLAW.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (joint appnt.), 1903; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (joint appnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 1913; dist. comsnr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, Ra., 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918; ag. asst. col. sec., 1920; dist. comsnr., Lautoka, 1920; ditto, Macaua, 1923; ditto, Rawa, 1928.

BAKER, FRANCIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Schl.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; comsnr., R. Artillery, 1915; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; pres., Jerusalem dist. ct., Aug., 1919; also pres., Beersheba dist. ct.

BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.So., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Day Training Coll., Univ. of London, 1905-08; B.S. hons.; B. of E. teacher's certif., 1908; M.Sc., London, 1910; D.So., London, 1920; dipl. of membership, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology (D.I.C.), 1920; R.F.C., France, 1915-16; warrant offr., R.N., 1916-19; govt. geologist, Falkland Is., 1920-22; headmaster, Grenada boys secondary schl., 1924.

BAKER, ROBERT, M. Inst. C.E., M.I. Struct. E., F.P.W.I.—B. 1874; F.M.S. Rlys., 1899; Athens water supply, 1905; Kowloon-Canton rly., Hong Kong, 1906; port engrnr., 1926; man. and ch. engrnr., Kowloon-Canton rly., 1927.

BAKER, MAJOR RUPERT HARRY WALPOLE, R. of O.—B. 1885; ed. Malvern Coll.; 2nd lieut. The Buffs, 1904; seconded as asst. comsnr., Gold Coast pol., 1914; served with the W.A.F.F., Togoland, 1914; The Buffs, B.E.F., France; major and 2nd in commd., 10th B.W.I., Regt., B.E.F., Italy, 1917; A.P.M., Italy, 1917-19; retnd. to Gold Coast, 1919; local commdt., Cyprus mily. pol., Mar., 1927; ag. A.D.C. and prte. sec. to gov., June-July, 1927; ag. comsnr., Sept.-Oct., 1928.

BAKER, WILLIAM ANTHONY, F.R.G.S., F.S.I.—B. 1868; foreman of wks., P.W.D., Jamaica, 1890; asst. supt., road constrn., 1892; survr., 1894; 2nd staff survr., lands dept., 1898; 1st staff survr., lands dept., 1899; asst. survr. gen. (ag. survr. gen. on several occasions) 1920; survr. gen., 1922.

BAKEWELL, FREDERICK WILLIAM, M.I.C.E.—B. 1874; res. engrnr., Kodugannawa rly. deviation, Ceylon, 14th Sept., 1903; ch. asst. constrn. engrnr., Jan., 1908; ag. ch. constrn. engrnr., rly. extensions, Apr., 1927.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MASON, M.A., D.Sc.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, 1st July, 1919.
BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Sch. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 19th Oct., 1921.

BALDWIN, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1882; 2nd div. elk., War Office, July, 1901; army audit off., Cape Town, 1904; army cashier, army scota., dept., O.F.S. and Cape Col., 1908; army cashier, Pretoria (attached to army pay dept.), for S. Africa, 1911; asst. auditor, col. audit dept., S. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1911; auditor, Seychelles, 12th Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, Nyassaland, Sept., 1914; attached for one month to audit off., Mauritius, 1915; resumed post of auditor, Seychelles, Mar., 1915; transf'd. to Brit. E. Africa for military service in connection with accns., Apr., 1916.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-asst., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inptr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19; inptr., 1927.

BALFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 16th Jan., 1911; attd. to Jaffna Kach., Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag. P. Pedro, May, 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1911; attd. to Trincomalee Kach., May, 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; asst. comsnr. excise, June, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1928; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1926; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, July, 1927.

BALL, ARTHUR DYRE.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law; ed., Mill Hill Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhib.), B.A. 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ag. sec., san. bd., Kuala Lumpur, 1912; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Mar., 1914; ag. asst. contr. of labour, Perak, Apr., 1914; passed final exam. in law, June, 1914; 1st al. mag., Perak, 1914; transf'd. Hong Kong, Mar., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Taipo, 1917; passed final exam. in Hakka, 1917; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1917; attd. to imp. and exp. off., and to col. secs'. off., 1918; ag. asst. col. sec., olk. of couns. and clk. to gen. mil. serv. tribunal, 1918-19; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1920; 2nd pol. mag., 1920; asst. crown solr., 1920; asst. atty.-gen., 1920; 2nd assist. col. sec. in addn., 1921; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1922; priv. sec. to O.A.G. in addn., 1922; asst. atty.-gen., 1922 and 1925; ag. pujaane judge, 1924; offr. rear., 1925; draftsman, atty. gen's. dept., Ceylon, Aug., 1927; editor, "Ordoes. of Hong Kong, 1844-1923"; joint edtr., "Regulations of Hong Kong, 1844-1925."

BALLANTINE, CAPT. JAMES ALEXANDER—B. 1878; 2nd lieut., Lance Fusiliers, 1901; served in S. African War (Queen's and King's med. with clasp); seconded to W. African Regt., 1906-09; asst. adjutant, 1907-09; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 30th Mar., 1910; dist. comsnr., 13th Mar., 1914; seconded for serv., Field Force, Togoland, 7th Aug., 1914; staff offr. to O.C., Br. Forces, Togoland, 21st Nov., 1915 to 21st Aug., 1916; Br. and Victory medals and "1914-15" Star; dep. prov. comsnr., Ashanti, 2nd Aug., 1921; ag. ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 23rd Nov., 1923 to 30th Mar., 1924; prov. comsnr., Ashanti, 3rd Jan., 1924; ag. ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 7th Dec., 1924 to 18th Mar., 1925.
BALMER, A. B.—Asst. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1918.
BALMER, F. E.—Dep. chief acctnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ch. acctnt., G.P.O., Kenya, 1st Oct., 1919.

BAMFORD, Lieut.-Colonel H.W.M., O.B.E. (1918); M.C. (1916).—B. 1883; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1900-1902; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff, N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F. Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde.-major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defense of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre avec palme; comsnr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, 29th July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, 28th July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol., G. Coast, 27th Aug., 1924.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE.—B. 1883; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; admitted bar, Barbados, 1910; barrister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A, Barbados, 1925; dep. regis., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926; crown coun., N. Rhodesia, 1928.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advoc., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct., G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag. since 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925 and 1928.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824), RIGHT REV. ALFRED PAKENHAM BERKELEY.—Foundation scholar, Codrington Coll., Durham; B.A., 1883; M.A., 1908; D.D., 1917; consecrated Lord Bishop of Barbados, 1917; vicar of All Saint's, Antigua, 1886-88; vicar of the Holy Innocent's, Barbados, 1890-1900; rector of St. Philip, 1901-06; dean and rector of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1906-17.
BARGERY, REV. GEORGE PEBOY.—B. 1876; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 26th Aug., 1913.
BARKER, H. P., D.S.C.—B. 1883; master mariner, 1900; ast. marine supt., mercantile marine; ch. instr., Dublin Sch. of Navig. and Seamanship, 1918; port off., Tanganyika, Oct., 1919.

BARLAS, WILLIAM.—B. 1888; ed. Pitlochry High Sch. and Thomson's Acdy., Dundee; travelling teacher, Falkland Is., Feb., 1908; ag. govt. schoolm., June, 1913; ast. teacher, govt. schl., July, 1914; mag., &c., S. Shetlands, Nov., 1914; jnr. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1915; mag., &c., New Is., Mar., 1916; mag., &c., S. Georgia, Apr., 1918; dep. mag., S. Georgia, Jan., 1920; ag. mag. S. Georgia, on various occasions, 1921-27, ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and ast. of couns., May, 1922; Feb., 1923; ag. col. postmr. and local audr., June, 1926-Feb., 1927; mag., S. Georgia, Sept., 1928.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A., 2nd cls. hon.; cadet, W. Pacific civ. ser., 15th Dec., 1911; attached to res. comsnr.'s off., Apl., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, 1st Nov., 1912; ag. treasr. and collr. of customs, 16th Apl., 1913; passed cadet, 14th Apl., 1914; deputy comsnr., 29th July, 1914; dist. offr., 1914; ag. res. comsnr., 19th Apl., 1917; dist. comsnr., Fiji, Feb., 1922.

BARNARD, BARIL HENRY FRANCIS.—B. 1874; ed. St. Mark's Schl., Windsor, and Keble Coll., Oxford; audit elk., Perak State Rlys., Apr., 1896; demarcation offr., Perak, Aug., 1896; ast. forest offr., Perak, Jan., 1898; forest offr., Selangor, Jan., 1900; dep. conserv. forests, S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1903; ag. conserv., forests, S.S. and F.M.S., in 1911, 1914, 1920 and 1924; personal ast. to conservator, forests, S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1926.

BARNARD, SIR FRANK STILLMAN, K.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1856; astd. ser. of B. Columbia Express Co. in 1879; subsequently became gen. man. and pres. of the company; promoter, and dir. for many years of the B. Columbia Electric Rly.; identified with many other important interests; mem. of H. of C., Canada, 1879-1896; alderman of Victoria, B.C., 1886 and 1887; mayor, 1896; ret. from active business life in 1906; lieut.-govr. of B. Columbia, 1914-1919.

BARNES, ARTHUR CHAPMAN, B.Sc. (Hons.), F.I.C., A.M.I.Ch.E.—B. 1891; ed. Descon's Schl., Peterborough Munic. Coll. of Tech. and Victoria Univ., Manchester (schol.); computer, survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; ast. analyst, Apr., 1916; chem. offr., Kenya, 1919; ag. dir., chem. research, May to Aug., 1920; agril. chem., Nigeria, Apr., 1923; ast. dir., agr., Zanzibar, June, 1927; ag. dir., agr., Jan.-Sept., 1928; prov. offl. mem., leg. coun.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; elk. and pay and qrtmtr., 1900; sub-impr., Fiji constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; impr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grade, 1914; ast. adjutant to local forces, 1914; capt., Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. impr. gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1916; J.P. for the col., 1916; press censor, 1917; ag. impr. gen. of constab. and sheriff, 1917 and Sept., 1925; chief impr., 1918; ag. impr. gen. of prisons, 1918; dist. comsnr., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr. and prov. comsnr., Ba. May, 1921-June, 1922; dep. impr. gen., constab., 1st Jan., 1922; prov. mem., leg. coun., Nov., 1926.
BARNHILL, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A. Hons. (Cantab.).—B. 1903; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 28th Apr., 1926; ag. ast. col. sec., 8th May, 1926.
BARRETT, UNWIN SANKEY, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1874; ed. Bedford Schl., elk., master of sup. ct.'s office, Sept., 1902; ch. clk., insolveney, July, 1906; master, sup. ct., Pretoria, 1912.

BARRETT-LENNARD, SIR FIRNIES CECIL ARTHUR, KT. BACH, (1926)—Served in Anglo-Boer War, but invalided through wounds recd. in action, 1899-1900; judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, and ex officio of sup. ct., Nigeria, Sept., 1913; served with local milty. forces and as judge, ct. of appeal for eastern Africa and the high ct. of Uganda, Feb., 1915 to Nov., 1916; judge, sup. cts., S. Sttlms. and F.M.S., Mar., 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Johore, June, 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Kedah, Dec., 1921; ch. just. and keeper of records, Jamaica, Nov., 1925.

BARRON, JACOB MAURICE.—B. 1891; ed.; High Schl., Dublin, 1899-1910; sen. modr., nat. sol., 1914, T.C.D.; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; labr. dept., 1915-17; passed in Tamil, 1917; dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 1917; labr. dept., 1917; offr., cls. V., Dec., 1917; supervisor of cust., Port Swettenham, 1917-20; labr. dept., Apr., 1921; supern. offr., cls IV., Nov., 1921; ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; dep. contr., rubber expts., Jan., 1924; town planning admstr. and valuer-gen., Kuala Lumpur Jan., 1926 and in Mar., 1927 (in addn.); ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1926; ag. mag., ditto, May, 1927; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Feb., 1928; ag. valr.-gen., F.M.S. and town planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1928; ag. contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., June, 1928; ag. dep. contr. lab., F.M.S., Aug., 1928.

BARRON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.A., 1909, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, July, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Dec., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kroh, Perak, Apr., 1913; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Selangor, Dec., 1916; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Temerloh, Pahang, Jan., 1921; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Nov., 1922; offg. asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Jan., 1925.
BARROW, JOHN—B. 1903; ed. Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge; cadet, Hong Kong, 7th Jan., 1927.

BARRY, EDWARD.—B. 1896; ed. Mount St. Mary's; served in Army, Apr., 1915-Oct., 1927; expdy. forces, European War, Aug., 1915-Nov., 1918; 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1922 to Oct., 1927; commr., 3rd gde., Somaliland, Mar., 1928.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1858; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mem. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's Bench division, 1913; ch. just., King's Bench divn., 1924.

BARRY, RICHARD JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., C.S.O., Cape, 1895; C.S. Law, 1899; clk., mag. dept., 1901; asst. mag., various places; relief staff, 1904; ch. clk., master's office, Cape (insolv. brch.), 1906; Natal, 1912; Transvaal, 1912; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1915; ditto, Cape, 1923.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACH.—B. 1880; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford, 2nd cls. class. moda., 2nd cls. final honour, schl. of jurisprudence; B.A., 1903; M.A., 1906; solr., 2nd cls. final honours, 1906; asst. dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1909; dist. comanr., 1913; ag. prov. comanr., Dec., 1915 to June, 1916; and from 23rd June, 1919; ag. comanr., Western Prov., 1st Jan. to 25th Feb., 1920; dep. prov. comanr., 1921; ag. comanr., C. Prov., June to Dec., 1921, and from Sept., 1922 to Jan., 1923; ag. comanr., W. Prov., Feb., 1923; prov. comanr., 6th May, 1923.

BARTLETT, HUMPHREY EDWARD GIBSON.—B. 1880; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford, 2nd cls. class. moda., 2nd cls. final honour, schl. of jurisprudence; B.A., 1903; M.A., 1906; solr., 2nd cls. final honours, 1906; asst. dist. comanr., G. Coast, 1909; dist. comanr., 1913; ag. prov. comanr., Dec., 1915 to June, 1916; and from 23rd June, 1919; ag. comanr., Western Prov., 1st Jan. to 25th Feb., 1920; dep. prov. comanr., 1921; ag. comanr., C. Prov., June to Dec., 1921, and from Sept., 1922 to Jan., 1923; ag. comanr., W. Prov., Feb., 1923; prov. comanr., 6th May, 1923.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1920; adminstr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; asst. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; ag. asst. admstr. gen. and asst. regiar. of companies, June, 1924.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B.A., Dublin; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, Nov., 1909; ag. asst. to atty.-gen., S. Sttlmts., Apl., 1910; passed in Malay, Jan., 1910; passed in law, July, 1910; offr. of sla. V., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, 2nd Jan., 1912; ag. inspr. of prisons, 30th Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, 21st Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, 25th May, 1912; ag. asst. supt., Netherlands Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts., 11th June, 1912; ag. asst. controller of labour, F.M.S., 10th Dec., 1912; ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Singapore, 21st Oct., 1914 to 19th Mar., 1915; asst. regiar. of imports and exports, S. Settktns.; sec., food control comttee., June, 1917; mem., shipping comttee., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrlr.'s comttee., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services recog. by sec. of state, Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering comttee., 1920; ag. collr.-gen., income tax, Sept., 1920; supernmy. offr., ols. III, Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comttee. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; seconded for serv. under Kelantan Govt. as asst. adviser, Apr., 1922; ag. Br. adviser Kelantan in admn., 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st asst. sec. (B), S.S., and clk. of couns., Jan., 1924; ag. under sec., S.S., Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Klung, Apr., 1926; sec. to rea., Selangor, Aug., 1926; ag. comanr., lands, S.S., Sept., 1926; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1927.

BARTOLO, PROF. SIR AUGUSTO, KT. BACH.—(1929), LL.D., B. Lit., F.R.H.S.—B. 1883; matric., Malta Univ., 1901; B.Lit., 1904; called to bar, 1907; edr. for many years of "Daily Malta Chronicle"; prof., hist. of legislation and examr. in law and lit., Malta Univ.; chmn., Malta branch Empire Parly. Assocn.; Malta Univ. del. at congress of Univ. of Empire in Gt. Britain, 1921; el. for 2nd divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for educn. and migrn., Aug., 1927; author of several works on Maltese hist. and Imp. affrs.
BARTON, A. E.V.—Imp. cust. serv., 1912; cust., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923; comptlr., cust., B. Guiana; collr., gen., Jamaica, 1927.

BARTON, C. J. J. T.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law triposes; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., 1917, 1919, 1921; asst. for native affrs., 1923; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1924; ag. asst. col. sec., 1927; ag. priv. asst. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., 1928, author various papers on Kenya tribes and local history.

BARTON, CECIL MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons., classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, 1906; asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1913; res. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; comanr. to inquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of coman. to inquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vetv. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup., on various occasions, 1923-28; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; circuit judge, Gold Coast, 1929.

BARTON, MAJOR P. F., V.D.—Assoc., London Assn. of Acctnts.; asst. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; addl. col. sec.'s office, Oct.-Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nov., 1908; ag. ch. asst. treas., Apr., July, 1914 and June, 1916; ch. asst. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, 6th Aug., 1923.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.), M.P., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. senator, 1925; agt. gen. for S. Australia, 1928.

BASSETT, JOHN HAROLD.—B. 1896; ed. Wallasey and Glasgow Univ.; warserv., 1914-19; priv. sec. to dep. contr. (organization) Min. of Lab., 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., 1921; attd. to dist. office, Jelebu, July, 1921; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Klang, Oct., 1922; ag. ast. ditto, May, 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; town planning admstr., Ipoh, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IV., Oct., 1927.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; attd. to Ratnapura Kach., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1922; astt. stntlt. offr., July, 1925.
BATEMAN, G. S., L.D.S., R.S.C., Eng.—Serv. with Forces in E. Africa, 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATEMAN, WALTER SLADE, O.B.E. (1920).—Served in convict and prisons branch, Cape Colony, 1894-1901; sec., bd. of management, Somerset hosp., Cape Town, 1897-1901; clk., prisons dept., Transvaal, Jan., 1901; chief clk., July, 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, June, 1903; astt. dir. of prisons, July, 1905; ag. dir., Nov., 1906; mem. of tender bd. at various times during 1902-1906; ag. gov. of central prison and local gaol, Pretoria, June, 1907; gov. of Johannesburg gaol and Diepkloof prison, Dec., 1907; ag. astt. dir. of prisons, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1911; dir. and under-secr. for justice, 1918.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity bar, 1909-13; oh. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., with jurisdiction as a judl. comsrr. for the Western Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. comsrr. in addn. during 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag., Fiji, May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., on various occasions 1926-27; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1927.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and audit office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transfld. Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; prin. clk., July, 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; astt. dir. prisons, Apr., 1926; is also comsrr. of oaths for Union.

BATHURST, HENRY CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Camb. Univ.; maths. schol., 1906-09; 22nd wrangler, 1909; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; passed in law and Tamil, 1912; ag. 2nd ast. supt. of imgrts., Penang, Apr., 1912; ag. astt. supt. of imgrts., Klang, Aug., 1912; ag. astt. contr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1912; astt. contr. of lab., Klang, 1913; ag. emigr. agt., Madras, Apr., 1914 and Mar., 1916; temp. employed in C.O., July, 1915; ag. sec., Madras govt. lab. bureau in addn., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1920; passed in Telugu, 1920; 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1921; astt. offr. assignee and dep. regiar., sup. ct., Penang, Oct., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, June, 1922; offr. chief astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1923 and Dec., 1924; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Mar., 1924; offr. ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Dec., 1924; ag. dep. contr. lab., Penang, May, 1926; fedl. examr., Telugu, Aug., 1926; ag. dep. contr. lab., Malaya, July, 1927, ag. contr., ditto., Aug., 1927; offr. cls. II., Nov., 1927; offr. cls.II. sec., high comsrr., July, 1927.

BATHURST, WALTER DUNDAS.—B. 1869; ed. Lancing Coll.; agt. of Congo Free State, 1883-6; appd. to col. audit branch exchequer and audit dep., Jan., 1889; local audr., Gibraltar, under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891; ch. examr., E. and A. dept., col. audit branch, Dec., 1906.

BATTERBEE, SIR HARRY F., K.C.V.O. (1927), C.M.G. (1918); C.V.O. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Schl., Faversham, and at Oxford; classical scholar, Hertford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 1st cls. math. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls.clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., May, 1906; ag. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands comsrs., 1912; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Ilington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr. 1920; ag. astt. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Adml. commdg. sp. serv. squadron during Empire cruise, 1923-24; astt. sec., 12th Dec., 1924.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Colombo cust., May, 1920; attd. to Badulla kachcheri, Jan., 1921; office astt.
