(1910).—B. 1851; ed. at public schls.; arrived in New Zealand, 1875; mem. of house of representatives for Timaru, New Zealand, 1890-1908; min. of pub. wks. and marine, 1896; premier col. treas., min. for educn. and labour, June, 1906; resig. premiership, and assumed portfolios of pub. wks. and rlys. in Ward ministry, Aug., 1906; high comsrr. in Lond., 1908-1912; M.L.C. since 1913.
HALLOCK, Ananda Rajah.—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1927; attd. Puttalam ksch., Dec., 1927.

HAM, Gordon Lupton.—Cadet, F.M.S., 7th Nov., 1903; lent to Kelantan, 13th, Oct., 1913; cls. IV., S. Stltmts., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., 14th Apr., 1919; clk. of couns., 2nd Sept., 1919; 1st asst. col. sec., S. Stltmts. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; asst. offl. assignee, Dec., 1920; ag. sec. to high comsrr., Feb., 1921; ag. asst. adviser to govt. of Kelantan, Apr., 1921; ag. 1st asst. sec., B. S. Stltmts., Apr., 1922.

HAM, Henry Somerset McKenzie.—Ck., recr. of rev., O.F.S., 1900; clk., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1901; ch. clk. and examr., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1904; asst. regar., deeds, Bloemfontein, 1912; ditto, Pretoria, 1928; ditto, Bloemfontein, Oct., 1928.

HAM, Laurence Peter Van Zyl.—Ck., rly. and cent. tel. office, 1898; clk., res. mag., Hoopstad, June, 1902; A.R.M., Hoopstad, Aug., 1902; Jacobsdal, Nov., 1903; Thaba 'Nchu, Feb., 1907; Winburg, Feb., 1910; D.A.R.M., Fouriesburg, Aug., 1911; Trompsburg, May, 1915; mag., Phillipolos, Jan., 1918; Winburg, 1920; Wakkerstroom, May, 1920; ag. mag., Harrismith, Apr., 1925; inspr., pub. serv. comm., Feb., 1926; sec., ditto, Jan., 1928; sec., dept. of mines and industries, Sept., 1928.

HAMBLIN, R. A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1925.

HAMILTON, Andrew Todd.—B. 1879; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1910; on milry. serv., 14th Dec., 1917 to 11th Feb., 1919; asst. mast., cls. II., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. mast., 1921; cls. I., Mar., 1926.
HAMILTON, Archibald Johnston.—B. 1892; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1924; ent. service of govt. of Trinidad, Apr., 1911; mag., county of St. Patrick, Jan., 1927.

HAMILTON, Arthur Wedderburn Hartwig.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; pol. probationer, 1908; proceeded to Amoy to study Chinese (Hokkien), Apr., 1909; passed final exam. in Hokkien, Jan., 1911; ag. asst. supt., pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1912; asst. supt., pol., Penang, Mar., 1913; ag. ch. pol. offr., Kelantan, May, 1915; asst. supt., pol., Singapore, June, 1915; asst. supt., pol., Penang, Oct., 1916; asst. supt., pol., Singapore, Apr., 1920 and Apr., 1923; ag. dir. criminal intell., May, 1920; asst. comsrr., pol., Kedah, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Dec., 1922; dep. comsrr., F.M.S. and ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Feb., 1923; attd. rubber restriction dept., Singapore, May, 1923; dep. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1923; dir. crim. investn. dept., F.M.S., May, 1924; ag. dir., polit. intell. bureau, Singapore, Sept., 1924.

HAMILTON, Eric William, B.A.—B. 1883; ed. Blundell's; exhibtr., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb., 1906; scholar, 1907; B.A., 1909; cadet, Hong Kong civ. serv., 1911; ag. sec., sanitary bd., 1914; J.P., 1914; returned to Canton, 1914; attached to sanitary dept., 1915; passed final exam., 25th June, 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., South, 1915; ag. 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, and dep. regist. of marriages, 1915; attached to imports and exports office in addition, 1916; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1916; ag. asst. dist. offr., 1917; attached to sany. dept. for sp. serv., 1918; ag. asst. dist. offr., 1918; dist. offr., 1918; dist. offr., South, 1920; 2nd pol. mag., 1923; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1924; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1926; dep. clk. of couns. in addn., 1927.
HAMILTON, Kenneth.—B. 1884; ed. Edin. Acad. and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; 3rd cls. economics tripso; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 6th Apr., 1910.

HAMILTON, Percival John Alexander.—Col. audit dept., 3rd Aug., 1915; asst. audr., Nyassaland, 18th Sept., 1915; audr., Br. Honduras, 28th Aug., 1922; audr., Leeward Is., 17th Dec., 1923; audr., Fiji, May, 1928.

HAMILTON, Sir Robert William, Kt. Bach. (1918).—Ed. at St. Paul's schl. and Trin. Hall, Camb.; class. scholar, B.A., 1889 (honours); M.A., 1892; student, Inner Temple, 1893; called to the bar, 1895; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1895 to 1897; regist., E. Africa Prot., 1897; town mag., 1899; asst. judge and admstr.-gen., 1900; judge of high court of E. Africa, 11th Aug., 1902; apptd. to serve on land coman., 31st Oct., 1904; prin. judge, E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1906; chief justice, 1911; ret., 1920.
HAMMOND, Stanley Alfred.—B. 1898; ed. Bancroft's schl. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 5th Apr., 1922; dir., educn., Jamaica, 1928.
HANCOCK, Cyril George.—B. 1902; ed. Kingsland Grange, Shrewsbury, Bromsgrove Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, 7th Jan., 1925.

HANCOCK, Capt. Otoh Lewis, O.B.E. (1925)—B. 1893; ed. King's Coll., Taunton and Exeter Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Aug., 1914; lieut., 1914; capt., 1915; wounded, 1915; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Leeward Is., 1916; staff offr., local forces and adjutant, volunteers, Barbados, 1917; gov., H.M. prison, Barbados, 1920; ag. senr. pol. mag., Aug. to Sept., 1923; comsrr., Virgin Is., 1923; dir., prisons, Gold Coast, Aug., 1926.

HANCOCK, WILLIAM JOHN, M.I.C.E., M.I.E.E.—B. 1865; ed. Dublin and Glasgow Univ.; G.S. & W. Rly. (Ireland workshops, Inchicore; Electric Lighting and Telephone Co. (Ireland); supt. of telephones, W. Australia, 1885; supt. of telegraphs, 1890; govt. electrical engnr., 1894; mem. of senate, Univ. of W. Aust.; pres., W. Aust. Inst. of Engineers, 1917-18; pres., Royal Soc. of W. Australia, 1918-19; hon. local sec., Inst. Elec. Engrs. (Lond.); lieut., A.A.M.C.; D.S., Univ. of W. Aust., 1924; Kelvin gold med., R. Socy. of W. Aust., 1924; hon. life mem., Inst. of Engrs. of Aust.

HAND, JOHN ST. VINCENT.—1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Oct., 1898; ch. clk., gen. man.'s off., Lagos govt. rlyw., Apr., 1901; asst. sec., sec.'s off., S. Nigeria, Feb., 1904; priv. sec. to J. J. Thoburn, Esq., gov. of Lagos in 1905, 1906, 1907, 1908, 1909 and 1910; and clk. to exec. coun.; asst. col. sec., S. Nigeria, 31st July, 1907.

HANDS, ARTHUR L. C., M.B.E.—B. 1880; clk. in P.O., St. Helena, Sept., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Apr., 1898; regiar. of births and deaths, 1904-07; ch. clk. and clk. to exec. coun., Jan., 1907; J.P., 1910; ag. crown prosecutor, clk. of the peace and pol. mag. during 1923.

HANKEY, Lieut.-Col. Sir, M. P. A., G.C.B. (1919); K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1912).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby; joined R.M.A., 1896; served in H.M.S. "Ramillies," flagship and 2nd flagship, Mediterranean, 1899-1901; naval intell. dept., 1902-6; granted temporary rank of Major whilst serving on a special comtee., 1906; intell. offr., Mediterranean, 1907; asst. sec., comtee. of Imp. defence, 1906; apptd. sec., 1912; sec., war cabinet, from 1916; British sec., Peace Conf., 1919; comdr., Legion of Honour, 1917; commendatore, Crown of Italy, 1918.

HANKINSON, WALTER CROSSFIELD, M.C. (1918).—B. 1894; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); private, Royal Fusiliers, Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Yorks and Lancs. Regt., 9th Jan., 1915; lieut., 1st Apr., 1915; capt., 13th May, 1915; served in Egypt and France; demobilised, 28th Feb., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., O.C., Feb., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1st Mar., 1923; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Jan., 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 20th Nov., 1924; ag. prin., 2nd Nov., 1925.

HANN, HUGH.—B. 1862; joined R.A. 1881; served in India, 1882 to 1890; ret. from serv., 1890; joined home prison serv., 1891; clk. and schoolmtr., 1896; supt. of pris., S. Leone, 1891; sheriff and ag. supt. of pol., S. Leone, July to Sept., 1902; J.P. for col., 1902; formed S. Leone fire brig. and first supt., 1893; 1st asst. supt. of pris., Ceylon, 1906; supt. of pris., Kandy, Nov., 1906 to Sept., 1908; asst. supt., convict estab., Colombo, Oct., 1908 to Dec., 1909; ag. insp.-gen. of prisons, Ceylon, and supt. of convict estab., Sept. to Dec., 1909; supt., Mutwal and Hulfta-dorp prisons, Jan. to Aug., 1910; supt., Mahara prison, Sept., 1910; ag. insp.-gen. of prisons, Sept. to Dec., 1911; supt., Fremantle prison, W. Aust., Jan., 1912.

HANNAN, GERALD HUGH CRAWFORD.—B.1879; ed. Mercers' Schl., London; served in S. African War, 1900-02 (2 med., 5 clasps); 2nd cls. clk., exec. and leg. couns., Transvaal, Oct., 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1903; gentleman usher of black rod, leg. coun., Transvaal, 1907; clk., asst. prov. coun., Transvaal, 1910; clk. of prov. coun. and exec. comtee., 1917.

HANNIGAN, CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. Ranelagh Sch., Athlone, Piper's Coll. and Connell's Acad., Belfast; serv. R. Irish constab., Mar., 1896 to Apr., 1902; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1902; ag. asst. comnrn. pol., Sept., 1906; asst. comnrn., pol., Mar., 1907; ag. supt., pol., S. Stiltns., Aug., 1916; supt., pol., S. Stiltns., Sept., 1916; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., S. Stiltns., Jan. to Mar., 1919, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1921 and Dec., 1922 to Mar., 1923; comnrn., pol., F.M.S., Nov., 1924.

HARCOURT, HAROLD WALLACE.—B. 1885; examng. clk., audit dept., Fiji, Nov., 1909; book-kpr., treasy., May, 1910; chief audr., Tonga, under three years agreement with native govt., June, 1911; audr.-gen. and govt. sec., Sept., 1911; ag. premier, Jan. to Feb., 1914; 2nd asst. audr., Fiji, Oct., 1914; ag. ch. clk., treasy., Oct., 1918; ag. asst. audr., July, 1920; ag. ch. clk., treas. and sec., comnrn. of currency, Oct., 1920; ag. comnrn., inland rev., Apr., 1921; cust., enemy property and contr., clearing office, Fiji and W. Pacific (conjoint), May, 1921; provisionally M.L.C., Aug., 1921; ag. comnrn. of stamps (conjoint), Jan., 1922; ag. audr. (conjoint), Apr. to June, 1922; dep. treas. and comnrn. of inland rev. and stamps, Jan., 1923; ag. col. treas., Apr. to Aug., 1924; ag. prov. mem., exec. coun., Apr., 1924; ditto, leg. coun., July, 1924; ag. col. treas. etc., (conjoint) and prov. mem., exec. and leg. coun., Feb. to Sept., 1925, July, 1927 and Apr., 1928; mem., leg. coun. in addn., July, 1927.

HARDING, ALFRED J., C.B.E. (1927), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon and St. John's, Camb.; foundtn. schl., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip. part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comtee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal coman. on Mauritius, 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from 1st Apr., 1912; 1st cls. clk., 12th Oct., 1914; sec., colonial blue-books comtee., 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. African currency bd., 1926-1928; mem., Palestine currency bd., 1926-1928; dir., col. audit, 1st Oct., 1928.

HARDING, SIR EDWARD JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1928), C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comtee., 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Royal Coman., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 10th Apr., 1916; 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; on military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th Apr., 1916; sec. to comtee. for attlt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ag. prin. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Dec., 1921; asst. under-secr. of state for dominions affairs, 30th July, 1925.

HARDY, THOMAS JAMES, A.C.R.A. (1924).—B. 1900; passed exams. of London Ascn. of Accts. and Corporation of Accts., 1923; diploma in book-keeping and accountancy, executorship and trust law and accts., mercantile law, auditing and economics, 1923; ent. C.O., 3rd Mar., 1916; apptd. asst. clk., C.O., after compet. exam., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES, C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1853; clk. treasy., W. Australia, Apl., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dists., June, 1886; ag. gov't. res., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Roebourne, June, 1889; sec., W. Australian gov't. agency, London, May, 1891; ag. agent-gen., 1909.
HARKNESS, EDWARD BURNS, C.M.G. (1922).—Entered treasy., New S. Wales, 1891; sec. to Premier's office, 1901-16; under sec., dept. of ch. sec. and public health, since 1916.
HARLEY, JOHN A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif. Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), July, 1909; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, 16th Apl., 1910; ag. protectorate med. offr., 2nd Jan. to 11th May, 1911.

HARMAN, H. A. A. F., D.S.O., B.S. (Lond.).—B. 1881; capt., 4th (S.R.) Batt., S. Staff. Regt.; prin., govt. training inst. for teachers, Accra, Gold Coast, 17th Apl., 1912; attd., Gold Coast Regt., for serv. in E. Africa, 14th May, 1916 to 31st Dec., 1918; slightly wounded, ment. in desps.; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, Dec., 1919; asst. dir., educn., G. Coast, 14th Oct., 1926.

HARMAN, WM. HENRY.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apl., 1900; to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf'd. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.

HARNETTY, LIEUT. EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed., Brompton Oratory schl.; capt., Lincolnshire Yeomanry; lieut., Sierra Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., 1918-21; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi and Mandingo; asst. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, 11th Jan., 1922.

HARPER, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 5th May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, 24th Jan., 1900; attached to Ashanti field force, 1st June to 1st Sept., 1900; asst. dist. comanr., 6th Sept., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec., 26th Oct., 1904; asst. col. sec., 8th Feb., 1906; ag. chief asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 14th Apl. to 25th June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, 16th Oct., 1908; prov. comanr., 23rd July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. chief asst. and clk. of couns., 15th May to 29th June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912, and from 3rd May to 27th Aug., 1914; attached to Togoland field force, 29th Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, 30th Sept. to 23rd Dec., 1914; chief asst. col. sec., 14th Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec., 25th June to 17th Nov., 1915, 27th Nov., 1915, to 27th June, 1916, and from 2nd Dec., 1916, to 23rd Apl., 1917; ch. comanr., Ashanti, May, 1920; gov't., St. Helena, 26th Dec., 1924; assumed gov't., 2nd Feb., 1925.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary clk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900; conf. clk., Nov., 1912; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1922.

HARRAGIN, WALTER.—B. 1890; barrister-at-law; clk. of the peace and sub-regar., San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, 9th Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary captain; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1919; crown counsel, 1924; ag. solf.-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from 16th Jan., 1925 to 13th Aug., 1926.

HARRAGIN, WILLIAM THORNTON.—B. 1887; clk., sup. ct., Trinidad, 1910; asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., 1911; held comanr. in Gordon Highlanders, 1915-16; served in France, May to Oct., 1915; asst. mast., Queen Victoria Schl., Fiji, 1916; cadet, Solomon Is., 1917; supervisor of customs, 2nd cls., G. Coast, 1st June, 1918; ag. asst. dist. comanr., Jan.-Mar., 1919; asst. dist. comanr., 28th Apr., 1919; ag. dist. comanr. in 1919 and 1920; asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921.

HARREL, MELVILLE DAVID, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1872; attchd. Royal Irish constab. depot, 1895; certif. drill and police duties; sub-inspr. Jamaica constab., Jan., 1897; 3rd cls. inspr., Nov., 1901; 2nd cls., Feb., 1907; 1st cls., June, 1913; deputy inspr.-gen., Oct., 1913; ag. inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, July to Sep., 1914; inspr.-gen. of police and comdt. local forces, Barbados, Aug., 1916; local rank of lieut.-col., 1919; inspr. gen., pol., Jamaica, 1925.

HARRIGAN, SAMUEL SIMONS.—Head teacher, Wesleyan schls., Virgin Is., 1878; resig., 1903; clerical asst., govt. offices, Road Town, 1903; ag. govt. offr., West End, Nov., 1904, Feb., 1906, and Feb. to Apr., 1907; govt. offr., Road Town, 1906.
HARRINGTON, WILFRED FRANCIS, M.O.—B. 1886; asst. polit. offr., 6th Dec., 1917; adminis. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st Aug., 1923.

HARRIS, SIR CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1917); C.B. (1904); C.M.G. (1900); C.V.O. (1917); M.V.O. (1911), B.A.—B. 1858; scholar, prizeman, and Porteus medallist of Christ's Coll., Camb.; Tanned student in com. law, Lin's Inn, 1877; 12th classic, 1878; apptd. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., after open competn., 1st July, 1879; sec. to W. Indies Finance Comms., Dec., 1882; asst. sec. Sugar Bounties Comfco., 1889; priv. sec. to Mr. Buxton, M.P., Jan., 1894, to June, 1896; and to the Earl of Selborne, June, 1895; 1st class clk., 1896; specially engaged in work connected with Venezuelan bdny. arbitn., 1896-8; prin. clk., Nov., 1898; sent to Paris, 1899, in connection with the Venez. bdny. arbitn.; conducted British case in Brazilian-B. Guiana boundy. arbitn., 1901-4; mem. of comte. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; ch. clk., 1st Feb., 1908; represented C.O. on advisory comte. of comm'l. intell., B. of T., 1900-17; mem. of managing comte. of Imp. Inst. 1909-16; gov. and o. in c., Newfoundland, 1st Nov., 1917; assumed govt., 17th Dec., 1917; ret., 1922; author of numerous articles in Dict. of Pol. Econ. on economic and statistical subjects, and of lives of various colonial govs. and statesmen in Dict. of Nat. Biog.

HARRIS, DAVID JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1897; apptd. after compet. exam., by clk., G.P.O., 1912; on mily. serv. (R.E. and R.A.F.), 8th Oct., 1915 to 27th Feb., 1919; temp'y. employment clk., Miny. of Labour, 1st Oct., 1919 to 20th June, 1920; permanent ditto, 21st June, 1920 to 30th Sept., 1922; cler. offr., C.O., 1st Oct., 1922.

HARRIS, HENRY GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. Brentwood Grammar Schl.; L.N.W. Ely.; minor appts., Gold Coast and Nigeria, 1901-1906 and 1909-1913; asst. loco. supt., Gold Coast, 1913; dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika, 1920.

HARRIS, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1884; entd. serv. of Imperial Post Office, 1st Jan., 1900; transf'd. to dept. of posts and telegrapha, Cape of Good Hope, 13th Nov., 1902; resig., Dec., 1907, and entd. S. Rhodesia telegraph serv., 1st Jan., 1908; seconded for serv. with African Trans-Continental Telegraph Co., 12th Apr., 1908; resig., 31st Jan., 1910; provincial postmaster, S. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; asst. supt. of posts and telegrapha, Fed. Malay States, 14th June, 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., Indian army res. of offs., unattached, Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., 91st Punjab Jt., July, 1918; lieut., Mar., 1919; released from army serv., Oct., 1919; resumed duty, Kuala Lumpur, July, 1920; relinquished coman., I.A.R.O., Sept., 1920; contr., posts and tels., F.M.S., Oct., 1926; addt. off., cf sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1927.
HARRIS, PHILIP CAPEN, A.R.I.B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Parkfield Schi., Liverpool, Liverpool Univ. and the Royal Acad. Schls. of Art; on war serv., 1914-19; architect, Zanzibar, Jan., 1922.

HARRIS, REGINALD ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Queen Mary's Schi., Basingstoke; clk., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., 13th June, 1913; editor, govt. gaz., since 1914; clk., 1st grade, Oct., 1920; ag. ch. clk., secretariat, Nov. 1920 to June, 1921; asst. treas., Apr., 1925.

HARRIS, HON. ROBERT EDWARD, D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. High School, Annapolis Royal; pri. tuition; D.C.L., King's Coll. Univ., Windsor, N.S.; LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S.; called to the N.S. bar, 1882; K.C., 1883; practised at Yarmouth, 1882-92; Halifax, 1892-1918; justice, sup. ct., N.S., 1915; ch. just., sup. ct., N.S., 7th Feb., 1918; selected as third arbitr. to determine value of Canadian Northern Rly. stock, on its acquisition by the Dom. Govt.; mem. of the coun. of the N.S. Barrister's Soc. and for some years pres.; chancellor, diocese of N.S., 1906; dir., schl. for blind, Halifax; gov. of King's Coll., Windsor, N.S.

HARRIS, WILLIAM VICTOR, B.Sc. (Agr.), A.I.C.T.A., F.E.S.—B. 1904; ed. Secondary Schi., Stockton-on-Tees; B.Sc., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1925; Cambridge, 1925-26; assoc., Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., Trinidad, 1927; asst. entomologist, dept. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1927.

HARRISON, ALBERT SEDDON, M.Sc. Manchester, 1908.—B. 1887; on staff of Manchester Univ., and asst. mast., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1908-10; senr. mast., Halesowen comml. schl., 1910-12; on staff Manchester Univ., educ. dept., and headmst., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1912-14; inspr. of schls., Ceylon, Apr., 1914; on active serv., 1918-19; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Ceylon, May, 1919, to Apr., 1920; ag. dir. of educn., Apr. to Oct., 1920; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920; prin., training coll., Jan., 1921.

HARRISON, ARTHUR ERNEST.—B. 1884; London Schi. of Law, 1911-12; clk., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1913; ag. registr., high ct., sherifff, off., recrt., regisr., deeds, etc., July, 1915; custodian, enemy property, 1917; regisr., etc., 1918; contr., clearing office, peace treaty, 1920; regisr. companies and patents, 1921; admstr.-gen., June, 1925.

HARRISON, CAPT. HORACE DURNFORD.—B. 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1915-18; twice ment. in depts.; headmast., govt. primary schls., Gold Coast, 6th June, 1920; mast., training coll., 1st Jan., 1921; inspr., schls., 1st Apr., 1926.

HARRISON, ERNEST, M.S.A. (Ivwa), B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A. (Hon.).—Lecturer in agr., schl. of agr., Grootfontein, Cape, 1904; prin., schl. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1913; dep. dir., agr., Kenya, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Nov., 1922 and Mar.-Oct., 1924.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1909; asst. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; ag. asst. gov. agt., Hambantota, Apr., 1912; pol. mag., Matare, May, 1912; ag. asst. gov. agt., Mannar, Feb. 1913; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; ag. asst. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1920; ditto, N'Ellya, Nov., 1924; ag. govt. agt., Eastern prov., May, 1927.

HART, H. W.—B. 1887; on milly. service in France and Flandert, Mar., 1915 to Jan., 1919; service in corps of milly. acctns., 1919-21; temp. clk., C.O., 1st Mar., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July 1926.

HART, RUPERT LANCELOT LEON, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1888; ed. Reading Schi. and R. Veterinary Coll., London; govt. vety. surg., N. Rhodesia, 1910-14; vety. offr., Uganda, 1914; served with E. African Forces in Great War, 1914-17 and on Western Front, 1917-19; senr. vety. offr., Uganda, 1920; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Aug., 1927.

HART, WILLIAM HUME, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Dub.)—B. 1885; ed. Sedbergh, Yorks and Dub. Univ. (Trin. Coll.); med. offr., grade III, F.M.S., May, 1912; grade II, Nov., 1913; seconded for mil. serv., Dec., 1915; temp. 2nd lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1916; capt., Feb., 1917; discharged, Sept., 1919; ag. medi. supdt., cent. mental hosp., Tanjong Rambutan, June, 1920; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, May, 1922; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1923; ditto, Perak, Nov., 1925; ditto, Pahang, Dec., 1925; ditto, Selangor, Apr., 1926; senr. med. offr., Pahang, but continues ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, Aug., 1926.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th I.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Haynes Smith, Cyprus, 1901; asst. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dis. oomsnr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comanr. for the Western Pacific, 1910; on milly. serv., 1915-19; ch. asst. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1919; ag. ch. inspr. of schls., Aug. to Oct., 1919; ag. ch. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. registr.-gen., Dec. to May, 1920; ag. comanr., July to Oct., 1920; ag. ch. sec., 27th Oct., 1920 to 3rd Feb., 1921, 17th to 23rd Mar., 11th May to 3rd Oct., 1921 and 14th July to 13th Oct., 1923; comanr., 1st July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Jan., 1924.

HARTER, JEAN DENIS EDGARD.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; asst. mast., R. Coll., 1901; asst. mast., Victoria Schi., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; asst. mast., King's Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schi., July, 1920; ag. dir. educn. and inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; inspr. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to bd. of educn.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. pvtc. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to couns. from 16th Dec., 1924.
HARTNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918, and from Apr.-June, 1922; comanr., July, 1922; ag. registr.-gen., July-Sept., 1922.
HARTWELL, CHARLES HERBERT, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1926; addt., Kandy kach., Jan., 1927; addt., secretariat, Oct., 1927; office asst., N. Eliya kach., May, 1928.

HARVEY, CHRIS, B.Sc. (Agr.), A.I.C.T.A.—B. 1902; ed. Woodford Coll., Essex, Transvaal Univ. Coll. Pretoria and Colonial Office course, Oxford, 1925; Imp. Coll. Trop. Agr., 1926; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct. 1927.
HARVEY, DAVID HAROLD.—B. 1874; entd. Natal civ. serv., Apr., 1898; transf'd. to Swaziland

HAWKINS, BRIAN CHARLES KEITH.—B. 1900; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.) hons. mod. hist. 1923; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924; 2nd ast. collr., treas., 1927; ast. supt., impts. and expts., 1927.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A. London Univ.; pte., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 8th Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 7th Feb., 1915; B.E.F., 29th Mar., 1915; ment. in desp., 20th Dec., 1917; wounded, and discharged with rank of capt., 5th May, 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; ast. to land office, Penang, 21st Dec., 1920; dep. collr. of land rev., Penang, 14th Jan., 1921; ast. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 21st Dec., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV, May, 1923; ast. ast. sec., res., Selangor, Dec., 1923; sec. rea., Pahang, Apr., 1926.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1924), M.V.O.—Under sec., and perm. head, premier's dept., New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, MORTIMER CECIL.—B. 1891; ed. The Schol., Bishop Stortford and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge (exhib.); B.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; ag. ast. contrlr. of lab., Penang, Mar., 1915; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Mar., 1916; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; dep. contrlr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; ag. ast. adviser, Endau, Johore, May, 1921; ag. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Mar., 1922; ag. prot. of Chinese and contrlr. of lab., Johore, Oct., 1922; ag. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Sept., 1923; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925; ag. ast. adviser, Kemaman, Trengganu, June, 1926.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE, K.T. BACH. (1919).—B.A., St. John's Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. cham. of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1889; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of "Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder."

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (dual gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; called to Irish bar, 1926; registr., high ct., Uganda, 21st Jan., 1921; also addnl. dist. mag., lat. cls. admstr.-gen., rev. authority, regist. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house; mag., Uganda, Mar., 1927.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1924; capt., B. G. Militia (Art.), 1922-25; ag. 2nd cls. clk., July, 1928; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., Oct. to Nov., 1928.

HAYES, ROBERT EDWARD, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1889; ed. New Zealand Univ.; entd. N.Z. postal dept., 1886; ast., tourists and industries and commerce dept., 1903; registr., friendly societies, 1906; also supt., national provident fund, 1910; ast. sec. and acct. to treas., 1922; sec. treas., paymr.-gen. and registr. of inscribed stock, 1925; again supt., national prov. fund, 1926.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxon; served in France with R. Engrns., (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; ast. dist. comsrr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1926.

HAYNES, ALWYN SIDNEY.—B. 1878; ed. at Halleybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. finan. astt., Krian, Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; passed final exam. in law, Oct., 1902; mag., Perak, 1902; passed final exam. in Tamil, Aug., 1903; ag. finan. astt., Krian, Sept., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Nov., 1905; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1906; ag. inspr., schls., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906; 1st cls. mag., Negri Sembilan, 1906; ag. astt. supt., Indian immigrts., Penang, Nov., 1906; astt. supt., ditto, Nov., 1906; examr., Tamil, Perak and Penang; ag. supt., Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1910; ag. astt. supt., immigrts., Klang, Jan., 1911; passed final exam. in Siamese, Aug., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1914; mem., bd. of examrs. in Malay for Perak, Apr., 1915; dist. offr., Tampin, 1916; sec., high comnrr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., and dir., food production ditto, Feb., 1922; chmn., small holdings comtce. (rubber restrictions), Nov., 1923; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1924; ag. Br. res., Perak, Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1926; resumed as sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1927; ag. comnrr., lands, F.M.S. in addn., June to Aug., 1927.

HAZELTON, ERIC.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., Monmouthshire Brig., R.F.A., June, 1915; joined B.E.F., France, Nov., 1915; lieut., R.H. and R.F.A., June, 1916; served with E.E.F., Suez Canal, Sinai, Palestine, 1916-19; cadet, F.M.S., Johore, Dec., 1919; 2nd astt. adviser to govt. of Johore, Jan., 1921; temp. ag. astt. adviser, Endau, Feb., 1921; ag. 2nd astt. adviser, Batu Pahat, May, 1921; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; ag. 4th astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; 4th astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; ag. pvt. sec. to ch. sec., Nov., 1923; offl. sec., res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1924; ag. astt. prin., C.O., Oct., 1928; offr., cls. IV, Nov., 1926; ag. astt. sec., res., Perak, Dec., 1927; ag. 2nd astt. sec. (A), S.S., Mar., 1928.

HAZKN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Schl., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1883; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis. for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERIGG, GARY, O.B.E. (1920).—Ed. St. John's Coll., Camb., B.A., LL.B. (class tripso, 1900, law tripso, 1905); dist. comnrr., G. Coast, Dec., 1908; regist., sup. o., Ceylon, Oct., 1910; called to Ceylon bar, 1914; sec., Ceylon Coun. of legal educn., 1914; served in Belgium and France with Rifle Brig., 1915; served in C.O. from May, 1916; sec. to gov. gen., Union of S. Africa, 1919-21; employed in C.O. since 1924.

HAZLERIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Admitted solr., sup. o., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; astt. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. astt. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924; treasy. solr. in addn., 1926; ag. dep. regis. in addn., 1928.
HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., Oct., 1910; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925.

HEAD-EVANS, ELLIS JAMES, M.I.M. & C.E., A.M.S.E.—Astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Nyasa land, Jan., 1912 to Aug., 1916; ag. astt. D.P.W., Nyasa land, Jan. to Sept., 1913; served with Nyasa land Vol. Res., 1914-15 (A.G.S. Med.); attd. as civil offr. to Nyasa land Field Force as ch. engrnr., road constr. in operns. in Ger. E.Africa, Mar., 1916 to Sept., 1917; seconded as astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1916; exec. engrnr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920.
HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; ed. Rugby and Sandhurst; astt. somaliland, Nov. 1919.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint comn. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transf., O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.

HEATH, HUBERT HAROLD.—B. 1865; ent. pub. serv., Barbados, 1883; junr. clk., P.O., 1885; 1st clk., 1902; acctnt., 1908; ag. col. postmr. on several occasions, 1907-18; ch. clk., treasy., 1918; ag. col. treas., Mar. to July, 1919; ch. offr., cust., 1919; ag. comptr., cust., on several occasions, 1919-22; man., govt. savings bank, 1922; col. postmr., 1925.

HEATH, W. G. (M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A," 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1905, 1912, and since 1916; prea. defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May, 1915; med. offr.-in-chg. of Montserrat recruits for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut., defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. comnrr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prin. clk., East London, 1910; survr., Durban, 1916; ch. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; dep. comnrr., cust., and excise, 1926; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Cust. Confe., 1921; cust. adviser to delegn. to Australian govt. (flour shipments), 1921; mem., delegn. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923; advisory mem., Imp. Confe., 1926.

HEDGES, WALTER FREDERICK, O.B.E., F.R.I.B.A.—B. 1886; served with L.N.L. Regt., Franco, Salonica, Egypt, Palestine, 1915-19; architect, Gold Coast hosp., 1920; Prince of Wales' Coll., Achimota, 1923; ch. arch., pub. works dept., Gold Coast, 1926; arch.-in-charge, archi. brchs., pub. works dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1928.
HEENAN, PETER.—Elec. to Ontario legis., 1919-25; House of Commons, Canada, 1925; min. of lab., 1926.

HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. at King's Schl., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1897; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of depot, 8th Aug., 1904; in command of Kyrena div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 15th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as commr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek, lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high commr., Sir C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ch. sec.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in commd. of pol., Kyrena div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; ast. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in commd. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907; inspr. of police, Cyprus, Apl., 1910; ch. of pol., Grenada, 22nd July, 1914; inspr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 1925.

HELLINGS, GEOFFREY STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp. (foundation schol.) and St. John's Coll., Camb., Camb., Stewart of Rannoch Univ. schol., Camb., 1909; B.A., 1910 (cl. trip. ii, I); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; seconded for mil. ser., Jan., 1915; 2nd lieut., 8th (Service) Batt. Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., May, 1916; lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., Sept., 1916; ag. capt., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., June, 1918; demob., Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Nov., 1919; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1920; ag. ast. prot., Chinese, Selangor, Sept., 1921; contr., lab. and prot. of Chinese, Johore, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; ag. prot., chinese, Selangor and Pahang, July, 1928; ag. sec., Chinese affra., F.M.S., in addn., Aug., 1928.

HELPS, EDMUND ARTHUR PLUNKETT.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge; history trip., 2nd cls., (1909); B.A. (1909); cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1912; ag. 2nd ast. supt. of imigrts., Klang, Sept., 1912; ag. ast. contrlr., lab., Aug., 1913; ag. ast. sec. to res., Selangor, May, 1914; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V., Mar., 1915; lent for service under the admstr. of German E. Africa, July, 1917; offr., cls. IV., Jan., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Raub, Apr., 1920; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; ast. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1921; attd. to sec., rubber restriction comtce., Kulim, Nov., 1923; ag. ast. adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1924; offr., cls. III., ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Nov., 1925; ast. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925; ag. regier., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Mar., 1928.

HEMMANT, GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Tonbridge and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; ag. ast. dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, June-Dec., 1904 and Mar.-June, 1905; passed cadet (Malay and Law), May, 1905; ag. rev. audr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1905; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Aug., 1905-Mar., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar.-July, 1908 and Jan.-Feb., 1909; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, July-Sept., 1908; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, Oct., 1908-Jan., 1909 and from Mar., 1909-Oct., 1910; ast. collr., land rev., Seremban, Dec., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1911-Jan., 1912; seconded, Kedah, Oct., 1914; ag. legal adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1914-Jan., 1916; seconded, Johore, Jan., 1916; ag. ast. adviser, Muar, Jan., 1916-June, 1918; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur and regier. of titles, Selangor, Nov., 1919-Nov., 1920; sec. to res., Selangor, Dec., 1920-Mar., 1922; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Jan.-Apr., 1923; ag. under-sec., S. Stltlms., May, 1923-Mar., 1924 and from Apr.-July, 1924; ag. col. sec., S. Stltlms., Mar. and Aug., 1924; gov.'s dep., Mar., 1924; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1924.

HEMSTED, RUPERT WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1919).—2nd treasy. ast., E.A.P., 19th Sept., 1899; ch. acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901, to 1st Dec., 1903; ast. collr., E.A.P., 1st Dec., 1904; collr., 17th Dec., 1906; offr.-in-charge, Massai reserve, 1912; senr. commr. (1st cls.), Jan., 1924.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prota., 18th Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., 1st Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., 7th Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; serv. in Northd. Cyclists' Batt., Northd. Fus. and Machine Gun Corps; M.C. and Bar.

HENDRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in desp., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920; M.L.C., 1926.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, 1st July, 1888; ast. to conservator, 1st Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, 1st June, 1893; Stutterheim, 1st Aug., 1898; ast. conserv. forests, E. Conservancy, 1st Oct., 1905; ast. conserv., W. Conservancy, 1st July, 1907; prim. lecturer, forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, Oct., 1906; resig. lecturship, Feb., 1908; chmn., bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, May, 1907; ast. conserv., forests, Midland Conservancy, 1st Feb., 1909; conserv. forests, ditto, 2nd grade, 1st Apr., 1912; conserv., forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's med.; tranfd., Rhodesian forest serv., 1st Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Feb., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

HENNESSEY, PATRICK H.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.); house surg., S. Stltlms., Feb., 1907; supernumerary med. offr., Penang, Feb., 1908; med. offr. gen. hosp., Singapore, May, 1908; ag. med. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1908; med. offr., grade II, Kuala Pilah, and med. offr., rly. construction, Apr., 1909; ag. med. offr., grade I, May, 1914; ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, in 1918 and 1919; ophthalmologist physician in addition; med. offr., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1920; med. offr. and opthal. surg., Ipoh, 20th Sept., 1923.

HENNIKER HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G. (1928).—Ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1906; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regier.-gen.'s off., Mar.-May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., ch. Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. elk., native dept., and ag. astt. native comnr., July, 1906; apptd. astt. native comnr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and off. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; comnr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; nom. mem. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law comnr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comd., coy., Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925.

HENRY, HON. GEORGE STEWART, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. pub. schs., Upper Can. Coll., Univ. of Toronto, and Ont. Agric. Coll.; farmer; al. to Ont. legis. by el., 1913; re-el., 1914, 1919 and 1923; min. of pub. wks. and highways in Ferguson admnstr., 1923.

HERBERT, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E. (1919).—Cllk., Cape census off., May, 1891; Cararnovar, Dec., 1891; Willowmore, May, 1896; atty.-genl.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1895; held several ag. appmts.; astt. mag., Gordonia, Apr., 1897; relvg. offr., Apr., 1899; ag. astt. mag., Rietfontein, Nov., 1905; confirmed Jan., 1908; ag. civ. comnr. and mag., Somerset, E., May, 1908; mag., St. Marks, July, 1908; detached for special service in B.B. Prot. in connection with inroads of Hottentots ex German S.W. Africa, under Simon Kooper; received thanks of the Cape govt. and Imp. Germ. govt. upon successful accomplishments of mission; inspecting mag., S.W. Africa Prot. (with rank of Major in Union Defence Forces), Sept., 1915; dep. secy., S.W. Africa Protectorate, Mar., 1916; secy. for Protectorate, Dec., 1916; soctg. offr. in addition, Apr., 1920; ag. admistr., S.W. Africa, 2nd to 17th Oct., 1920; sec. for S.W. Africa, civ. admistr., 1921; sec. for native affrs., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1923.

HERCHENRODER, SIR FURCY ALFRED, KT. BACH. (1914), K.C. (Mauritius), 1905)—B. 1865; ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid. Term., June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge, Seychelles, 1900; ch. just., 1903; proc. and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1906; ag. chief judge, 3rd Sept., 1912; ch. judge, 1st Jan., 1913.

HERDMAN, HON. ALEXANDER LAWRENCE.—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915; resig. 1918, and apptd. judge of N.Z. sup. ct.
HERMON-HODGE, H. B.—B. 1886; ed. Winchester coll. (scholar) and Magdalen coll., Oxford; B.A., hons. in mods. and hist.; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 26th Dec., 1908.

HEROLD, THOMAS BARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. clk. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; astt. master, June, 1902; comnr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn.), 1907-8 and 1908-9, land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chrmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1924.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.L.A.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comdt. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Vereeniging; atty.-gen., O.R.C., min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., 30th June, 1924.

HESLIP, MAJOR ISAAC.—B. 1876; 3rd batt., R. Irish Rifles; passed course of instrn., R.I.C., Dublin; Hythe certif.; astt. comnr., pol., Sierra Leone, 8th Feb., 1911; comnr., pol. and sheriff, 8th July, 1914; dist. comnr., 22nd Sept., 1920.

HEWINS, WILLIAM ALBERT SAMUEL, M.P.—B. 1865; ed. at Wolverhampton Grammar schl. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; M.A.; dir. of London schl. of economics, 1896-1903; teacher of modern economic history, Univ. of London, 1902-3; Tooke professor of economic science and statistics, King's Coll., 1897-1903; mem. of senate of Univ. of London, 1900-1903; late sec. of Tariff comnr.; M.P. (U) for Hereford, 1912-1918; parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 26th Sept., 1917, to Dec., 1918; author of numerous works and articles on economics and political econ.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B.1890; astt. auditor, G. Coast, 25th Feb., 1914; ag. senr. astt. auditor, 28th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1919; ag. astt. col. sec., 19th Aug. to 14th Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Col. Audit Dept., 1st July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, 3rd Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, 18th Sept., 1926.

HEWLETT-COOPER, C. T.—Ent. "Britannia," 1889; lieut., 1898; ocmdr., 1909; capt. (ret.), 1921; S. African Med., 1900; commanded H.M.S. Aquarius, Egian and Adriatic operations, 1914-17; commanded H.M.S. Audes, escort to convoya, 1918; commanded H.M.S. Marshal, 1919-20; joined col. serv. as dir. of marine, Dar-es-Salam, 2nd Oct., 1920.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., 25th July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Mar., 1921; ag. astt. contr., lab., 1st May, 1921; ditto, Seremban, Jan., 1923; passed cadet, Aug., 1923; astt. contr., lab., Seremban, Mar., 1924; astt. contr. lab., Klang, Aug., 1924; fedl. examr. in Tamil, Sept., 1924 and July, 1926; astt. contr., lab., Penang, Jan., 1926; ag. dep. contr., lab., Mar., 1926; fedl. examr., Tamil, July, 1926; astt. emigrn. comnr., Malaya, Negapatam, Mar., 1928.

HIAM, ROBERT WOOD, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1874; ed. Claremont Grammar Schl.; pupil, G.W.Rly., 1890-1893; engrg. staff, G.W.Rly., 1893-1910; engrg. staff, F.M.S.R., 1910-19; engrg. for ways and wks., F.M.S.R., 1919.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, 3rd Jan., 1903 to 19th Mar., 1917; astt. polit. offr., Tanganiky Territory, 20th Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramagate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., 22nd May, 1905; astt. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, Hon. Henry Bournes.—B. 1861; M.L.A., Victoria, 1894-1900; chrmn. of royal comm. on legal procedure; mem. of Austn. Fed. Convention, 1897-98, and of the first Federal parlmt.; justice of High Court, C. of A., since Oct., 1906.

HIGGINS, Major Robert Henry Constable, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in despatches; vetry. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1923.

HIGGINSON, Robert Cyril Gerard Durant.—B. 1884; ed. Brighton Coll.; cadet, Fiji, 1907; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1908; admitted barrister and solr., sup. ct., Fiji, 1909; stip. mag., 1910; passed cadet, 1911; 2nd grade dist. commr., 1914; dist. commr., and prov., commr., Colo East, Apr., 1914; prov. commr., Mamisi and Serua and dist. commr., Navua, 1915; ag. res. commr., Rotuma, Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1921; prov. commr., and dist. commr., Lomaiviti, Apr., to Dec., 1921; ag. ch. pol. mag., ag. reg. sup. ct., ag. reg. of titles, ag. curator, intest. and vacant estates, ag. pub. trustee, ag. offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922; ch. pol. mag., reg. gen. and reg. titles, July, 1922; nom. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1922; commr., cust., Sept., 1925; mem. of comte. to rep. on constitution and laws of Rotuma, Nov., 1925; seconded for sp. serv. with the W. Pacific high commr. and apptd. a judl. commr., to preside over Malaita murder trials, Solomon Is., Mar. to June, 1928.
HIGHAM, Reginald.—B. 1879; traffic canvasser, Gold Coast rly., Feb., 1904; ag. senr. traffic asst., May to Oct., 1910; ch. traffic offr., Aug., 1911.

HIGHET, David John, M.I.C.E.—First asst. engrnr., Ulu Selangor extension, Oct., 1890; ch. asst. engrnr., Jan., 1894; ag. res. engrnr. for rlyws., Mar., 1894; dist. rlyw. engrnr., F.M.S.R., Jan., 1903; ag. divisional engrnr., Selangor and N. Sembilan, June, 1904; divisional engrnr., South, Apr., 1906; ag. gen. man., F.M.S.R., May, 1909.

HIGNETT, Charles Francis.—Ed. Woking Coll., Surrey; served with Imp. Army, Apr., 1884, to Aug., 1889; 3rd clk., mag. office, Durban, 1st Aug., 1889; sub-inspr., Zululand pol., 27th Jan., 1891 to 31st Jan., 1896 (three years as adjutant); asst. mag., Umlalazi sub-dist. of Eshowe, 1st Feb., 1896; mag., Umlalazi dist., 30th Dec., 1897; Ngutu dist., 10th Aug., 1899; ag. mag., Eshowe dist., Apr. to Aug., 1903; Dundee dist., Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909; mag., Alexandra div., 31st Aug., 1909; Umgeni div., Pietermaritzburg 6th June, 1911; Eshowe, Zululand, 6th May, 1912; Verulam, 1st Nov., 1915; senr. mag., Durban, 1st Oct., 1920; judge, native high ct., Natal, 1st Oct., 1922; war service, rebellion in Zululand, 1888-9; Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1902; native nation rebellion, 1906.

HILL, Arthur Heylinger.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and New Coll., Eastbourne; clk., treasry., Br. Guiana, 1893; 3rd grade commr. of taxation, 1902; 2nd grade, 1904; sec. excise bd. and supervising commr. treasry., in rev. branch, 1905; passed exam. in Hindi, 1905; 1st cls. clk., imigrn. dept., 1907; seconded as asst. emigrn. agt., Calcutta, Mar., 1913; ag. emigrn. agt. at Calcutta for Br. Guiana, Trinidad, Fiji and Jamaica, 14th Apr. to 7th Aug., 1914, and from 20th Nov., 1915 to 6th Nov., 1926.
HILL, Frank Macley.—Graduated, Lond., 1891; gov't. vet. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1919; J.P., 1920.
HILL, Gilbert.—B. 1889; wireless engnr. operator, Gambia, Feb., 1923; tel. asst. dir., Somaliland, Sept., 1926.

HILL, James Reginald.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana; 8th clk., admtr.-gen.'s off., Br. Guiana, May, 1896; 5th cls. clk., police dep., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr. of police, Dec., 1900; dist. inspr. of pol., Apl., 1903; ag. adjut. and musketry instr., July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of pol., Nov., 1909 to Apl., 1910, and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qtrmr., from Sept., 1911; pay and qtrmr., 1st Jan., 1912; commd. paymr. and staff offr. for admstv. duties with local forces, 1914-18; 1st cls. clk., audit dept., Apr., 1923; ag. dir., co-op. socs., July to Dec., 1923; ch. clk., audit dept., Jan., 1925; ag. audr. on various occasions, 1926-27.

HILL, Reginald Dykers Richardson.—B. 1902; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; called to bar (Gray's Inn), 1924; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 20th Jan., 1926; ag. prin. clk. and clk. to the ct. of policy and combined ct., Sept. to Apr., 1928; ag. prin. clk., Aug. to Dec., 1928.

HILL, Samuel Ramsay.—B. 1881; S. African constab., 1901; Queen's S.A. med., five clasps; prison serv., E.A. Prot., June, 1905; ag. dep. supt., Sept., 1928; asst. supt., Apr., 1910; mily. serv., 1914-18; ment. in despatches; M.C.; supt., Apr., 1917; asst. commr. of prisons, Kenya, Feb., 1923; ditto, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1928.
HILL, Wilfrid James, C.D.A.—B. 1895; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh; mily. serv., 1916-19; C.D.A., Edin. Agr. Coll., 1921; dist. agr. offr., Tanganyika, 1925.
HILLIER, Sidney Charles.—B. 1888; London Schl. of Economics (Brunel med.); G.W.R., 1902; mily. serv., 1915-1921; ment. in despatches (twice); G.W.R., 1921; asst. acct., Tanganyika rly., 1922.

HILLYER, Herbert Keys, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900; secretariat ass't., col. sec.'s off., Ceylon, Jan., 1908; sec., Colombo Port Coman., July, 1913; on war service, 1918-1919; resumed duties, 1919.

HIME, Charles Frederick William.—B. 1869; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal; sec. to late clk. just. Sir Henry Connor, 1st Jan., 1888; clk., atty-gen.'s office and ag. regist., sup. ct., 1st Mar., 1889 to 30th June, 1904; sec. to min. of just., 1st July, 1904; mag. for colony of Natal, 1st July, 1908, holding post, asst. mag., Pietermaritzburg; ag. ch. mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1st Apr., 1915; mag., Bergville, 1st Dec., 1915; addnl. mag., Durban, 1st Dec., 1918; mag., Dundee, 1st May, 1920; senr. mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1st Aug., 1921.

HINCKS, Maurice Thorp.—B. 1885; ed. Stonegate Schl., Leicester, and Blairlodge Schl., Polmont, N.B.; solr., sup. ct., 1908; commr. for oaths, 1913; on mily. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. reg., Gold Coast, on various occasions in 1921 and 1923-27; ag. solr.-gen., Jan. to May, 1922; regist., sup. ct., Nigeria, Aug., 1927.

HISLOP, Capt. Francis Daniel, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Stilts., Oct., 1919; attd., treas., Singapore, Deo., 1919; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resign. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1880; asst. engr., water wks., Trinidad, 24th Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, 24th Sept., 1906; divisional engr., 1st Apr., 1926.

HOAL, WILLIAM GARNER.—B. 1886; ed. Normal Coll. Schl. and S. African Coll., Cape Town; B.A., 1904; LL.B., 1908; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1909; clk. to mag., Woodstock, 1909; sup. ct., Cape Town, 1908; asst. regis., prize off., 1914; asst. regis. and taxing offr., sup. ct., Kimberley, 1917; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Pretoria, 1920; govt. atty., 1924.
HOAR, HENRY SAMUEL, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1928; attd., Kurunegala kach., Feb., 1928; attd. Anuradhapura kach., April, 1928.
HOARE, ALBERT RAWLINSON.—B. 1884; govt. schlmr., Falkland Is., Aug., 1915; local audr., Apr., 1928; supt., educ. and headmr., govt. schl., Stanley, Sept., 1928.

HOBDAW, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo Kacheheri, April, 1913; Trincomalee Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. off. asst. to the govt. agt., Ratnapura, May, 1914; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing survr., Colombo customs, May, 1919; 4th asst. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1922; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1925; ditto, Kegalle, Feb., 1927; ditto, N'Elia, Sept., 1928.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1865; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the commrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1886; transf'd to the C.O., April, 1891; asst. regis., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff-clk., first grade, 1911; supt., printing dept., Dec., 1924.
HOCART, ARTHUR MAURICE.—Archeological commr., Ceylon, May, 1919.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926.

HODGES, ARTHUR HARRIS.—B. 1884; entd. Impl. civ. ser., and apptd. to acnt.-gen.'s office, G.P.O., London, 1900; ch. acnt.'s office, G.P.O. O.R.C., 1903; Australia, 1908-9; junr. asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 1910; senr. asst. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924.

HODGINS, HON. FRANK EGERTON.—B. 1854; educ. Upper Canada Coll. and Trin. Univ.; B.C.L.; called to the bar, 1879; K.C., 1902; prominent legal practitioner in Toronto for several years; pres., Ontario bar assoc., 1908-9; legal agt. for Dominion govt. at Toronto, 1890-1896; counsel for Ontario govt. on license investigation, 1907, and on other occasions; judge of high ct. of Ontario, 1912; also local judge in Admiralty, Exchequer Ct.; now a just of appeal.

HODGKINS, GILBERT GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. N.Z. pub. schls. and Otago High Sch.; entd. N.Z. regisr.-gen.'s dept., 1901; regisr., births, etc., 1907; dep. ch. electoral off., 1913; ch. electoral off., 1928.
HODGSON, HARRY COURTENAY.—B. 1887; ed. Rocklington Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 30th Nov., 1910; dist. commr., 1st May, 1915.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, Fellow R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1885; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educ., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; attd. to gen. staff; educ. offr., 66th divn., 1918-19; instr., schl. of educ. for offrs., Catterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instr., W.O. schl. of educ., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educn. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldermot, 1922; A.E.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922; prin., Arab schl., Mombasa, Kenya, 1927.

HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, C.M.G. (1922).—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damaras war (med. and clasp); mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; ag. ast. commr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. commr. and 1st cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912; H.B.M. consul. for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditt., S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attcd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasp); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Falkland Is. and their dependencies, 22nd Dec., 1926; joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadhams Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditt., Tangalla, May, 1909; ditt., Matara, Dec., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; ast. settlm. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Ordn., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addnl. ast. col. sec., Apr., 1919; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. ast. dir., educn., Mar., 1922; ag. ast. dir., educn., Apr., 1922; ast. govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1923; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926.
HOFMEYR, HON. JAN HENDRIK, M.A.—B. 1894; prof. of classics, Univ. of Witwatersrand, 1917; prin., 1919-24; admtr., Transvaal Prov., 1924.
HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 24th Aug., 1921.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science; Whitworth Schol., 1906; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; ast. engr., 1919; dep. head, engnr. inptn. dept., 1920.

HOHENKERK, LUDOVIC SMITH, M.B.E. (1925).—B., 1874; ed. in B. Guiana; ag. 6th cls. olk., govt. land dept., B. Guiana, 1890; ag. survr., 1896; 5th cls. elk., 1897; 3rd grade survr., 1897; 2nd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., 1904; ag. 1st cls. offr., 1901 and 1908; J.P. for the col., 1908; ag. warden, No. 3 mining dist., 1906, 1907 and 1909; 1st cls. offr., 21st Sept., 1908; ag. forestry offr., 1914-15; performed duties of prin. elk. in head offr., 1916; represented Col. at Imp. Forestry Conf., Canada, 1923; seconded for duty with B. Guiana exhibit, Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924; supt., forest surveys, forestry dept., 1926.

HOLDBROOK, HUBERT GEORGE, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., Trinity Coll., Dublin, L.M., Rotunda Hosp.—B., 1887; med. offr., S. Stltms., 1912; med. offr., F.M.S., 1913; certif., London Sch. of Trop. Med., 1921; health offr., F.M.S., 1921; health offr., Perak North, Feb., 1922.

HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stltms., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908; ditto, Malacca, May, 1909; exec. engr., Malacca, Dec., 1909; ag. exec. engr., Singapore, Jan., 1910; ag. exec. engr., Prov. Wellesley, Oct., 1910; ag. exec. engr., Singapore, June, 1913; ag. exec. engr., Malacca, Mar., 1915; ag. exec. engr., Singapore, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. coll. engr., Mar., 1920; exec. engr., Malacca, 29th Dec., 1920.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B., 1898; on mily. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, COMMDR. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B., 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Petersol," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; asst. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commdr. 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbmr., Hong Kong, 1924; commdr., 1927.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B., 1884; atty. and notary, sup. ct. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, 1st Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Mimoho, 12th Oct., 1917; dist. polit. offr., Lome, 1918; ag. dist. commr., Bole, N. Territories, Gold. Coast, Oct., 1918; asst. admstr.-gen., Zanzibar, 19th Jan., 1925; ag. admstr.-gen., pub. trustee, regsr. of doc. and land offr. on various occasions, 1926-28.

HOLLINGSWORTH, LAWRENCE WILLIAM, H.A. (Lond.), F.R. Hist. S.—B., 1896; ed. Sir Walter St. John's Sch., London Univ., and London Day Training Coll.; on active serv., E. Africa, 1916-19; normal master, Zanzibar educn. dept., 1922; author "Milangoya History" (Vols. I and II); part author "A School History of Zanzibar."

HOLLINS, NOEL CLINTON.—B., 1886; ed. Rossall Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab), 2nd cls. hist., tripes; qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; asst. coll. sec., Sierra Leone, 31st Mar., 1910; ag. sen. asst. coll. sec., 1911 and 1912; asst. dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1913; dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1915; ag. prov. commr., C. Prov., Nov., 1922.

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B., 1874; asst. coll., E.A.P., 12th Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admstr. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admstr., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expdn., 1900-1; and Nandi expdn., 1906-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., 13th June, 1907; on special service to Uganda, Oct.-Nov., 1909; ag. chief sec. to gov't., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 26th Jan., 1913; ag. gov'r., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914; June to Oct., 1915, and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admstr., German East Africa (Tanganyika Territory), 4th Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; chief sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. gov'r., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; author of works on the Massai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B., 1890; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C. of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Econ.) Lond. (Schl. of Economics), 1917; Hutchison research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-25; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1925.

HOLM, ALEXANDER, C.B.E. (1928), M.R.A.S.R. (Gold Medallist), F.H.A.S., S.E.A.C. (Dip. Hons.).—Gen. manager, govt. experimental farm, Potchefstroom, Transvaal, 1903; gen. manager and principal, schl. of agric., Potchefstroom, 1909; under sec. for agric. (educltn.), Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., 1918; mem., exec. leg. and inter-coll. couns.; pres., agrl. and horticl. society of Kenya; Br. Empire Exhibn. commr. for Kenya, 1924.

HOLMES, HAROLD KENNARD, C.B.E. (1928).—Ed. All Saints, Bloxham; admitted solr., sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1900; served with K.R.R.C., Somme, 1916; wounded, Guilemont; with 20th London Regt. at Salonika, Palestine, France and Rhine, 1917-18, 2nd lieut., 1918; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, 1919; J.P., 1919; ag. land offr., 1921 to 1922; ag. crown solr., 1922; crown solr., 1922.

HOLMES, ROBERT BLAKE WOLESLEY, O.B.E., M.Inst. C.E., M.I.M.E., F.R.G.S.—B., 1878; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. engr., Lagos rly. (constrn.), Nigeria, 1900; asst. loco. supt., Nigeria rly., wks. man., dist. loco. supt., dep.ch.mech. engr., Nigerian Eastern Rly., 1905-16; formed mech. and shipyard formations, inland waterways and docks br., R.E., Rimbrough, 1916; major, 14th Sept., 1914; lieut.-col., 1st Mar., 1917; ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E., 1918; col., 1st Mar., 1918; demob., Apr., 1919; recalled to colours, June, 1919, and proceeded to Damascus as asst. dir., rly. traffic, in ch. of Hejaz rly., Nov., 1919; in ch., evacua- tion of troops by rail from Syria, Dec., 1919; col., grade T., Mar., 1920; gen. man., Palestine mily. rlys., Mar., 1920; transf. to civ. govt. as gen. man., Palestine rlys., 1st Oct., 1920; col., R. of O., 1st Nov., 1921.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B., 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Art'y., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; prte. sec. to commr. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926; ag. prin., D.O., 10th Dec., 1928.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Dec., 1926; asst. pvt. sec. to gov., Mar., 1927; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1927; office asst., Kurunegala kach., Sept., 1927.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney), Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Sch. of Mines (London) in Mining, Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Sch. of Mines, 1901; mining engr., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr., mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expeditn. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admin., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 9th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs and acting offr., 20th Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admin., 22nd Mar., 1907; res. commr., Swaziland, 1st Jan., 1917; gov., Seychelles, 1923.
HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot's Sch. and R. Vety Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France, 1916-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922.

HOODLESS, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. hon., Lond., 1910; Drew gold medal, mathematics, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; asst. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., Lau Schl., 1915; J.P. for the colony, 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. supt., schl., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924; asst. dir., educ., 1927.

HOOGENHOUT, NICOLAAS MARAIS.—B. 1876; ed. Boys' High Schl., Wellington and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; M.A. (mod. lang.), Univ. of Cape of Good Hope; Ph.D., Kaiser-Wilhelms-Universitat, Strasbourg (Alsace); prin., Krendrecht High Schl., Pretoria, 1904; vice-prin., Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1906; prin., ditto, 1921; rector, Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria, 1925; dir., educ., Transvaal, 1928.

HOOKER, RICHARD SYMONDS.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; passed lower standard exam. in Mende; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, July, 1910; dist. commr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. commr., N. Prov., Nov., 1920 to Aug., 1921 and from Sept., 1922; prov. commr., 1st Jan., 1926.

HOOPS, ALBERT LAUNCLOT.—B. 1876; ed. at King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stip. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M., Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Sch. of Trop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offr., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (severely wounded, ment. in desps., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. capt., Penang vol.; ag. col. surg. req., Penang, June, 1906; seconded to organise medical and prison depts. in Kedah, Mar., 1906; state surg. and supt. of prisons in Kedah to Nov., 1920; ag. adviser to govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1906, to Apr., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of Indian immigra., Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910; prot. of lab., Kedah, in addition, Nov., 1917; prin. civil med. offr., S. Stltmte., 23rd Nov., 1920; pres. coun., coll. of med., S'pore.; pres., S.S. and F.M.S. med. council; mem., leg. coun., S. Stltmte., 1st Jan., 1923; chmn., tuberculosi comittee, June to Aug., 1923; pres., 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Singapore, Sept., 1923.

HOPE, JAMES.—B. 1899; ed. Potchefstroom Coll., Transvaal and R.M.U., Sandhurst; gaz. 2nd lieut., K.O.S.B., 1918; Br.-Gen. Serv. and Victory medals; joined Swaziland serv., Sept., 1919; gaz. clk. to A.C. Hlatikulu; ag. mast., regir. and sheriff, 1923; con. clk. to treas., N. Rhodesia, June, 1925; clk., Bech. Prot., May, 1927; passed 3rd grade Swazi language exam., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv., lower law exam., 1922.
HOPKINS, J. G.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, CAPT. EMILIUS, C.M.G. (1922), D.S.O. (1900), M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; late ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical asst., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., I.Y. (ment. in desps., Queen's Med., 4 clasps, D.S.O.); late surg.-lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903; travelling commr., South Bank prov., Gambia, 1912; travelling commr., North Bank Prov., Gambia, 1921; author of "Vocabulary of the Mandingo Language."

HOPKINSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1894; cler. asst., post office, Br. Guiana, 1911; 6th cls. offr., cust., 1912; 5th cls. ditto, 1914; 4th cls. clk., treasy., 1920; 3rd cls. ditto, 1923; 3rd cls. clk., col. secretariat, 1925; 2nd cls. clk., ditto, 1926.
HOPPS, HERBERT DAVIDSON.—B. 1876; asst. foreman, Indian State Rly., 1903; foreman, 1919; E. African Forces, 1914-19; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika Rlys., Oct., 1919.

HOFTON, REGINALD EDWARD, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.).—B. 1887; surg. probationer, R.N.V.R. (on active serv.), 1916-19; house surg., R. Infirmary, Edin., 1921; clin. asst., Grovesnor Hosp. for women, London, 1923; clin.-asst., London Lock Hosp., 1925; specialist in venereal diseases, Cyprus, July, 1927.

HORE-RUTHVEN, MAJOR-GEN. HON. SIR ALEXANDER GORE ARKWRIGHT, K.C.M.G. (1928), V.C. (1899), C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—B. 1872; ed. Rton; joined 3rd batt. Highland Light Infy., 1891; attd. Egyptian Army, Soudan, 1896; commanded Camel Corps detachment at battle of Gedaref and subsequent operns. (V.C., 4th cls. Osmanieh, English and Egyptian medics with clasp, three times ment. in desps.); sp. serv. offr., Somaliland, 1903-04; mily. sec., Viceroys of Ireland, 1905-06; mily. sec., gov.-gen., C'wealth of Australia, 1906; served European war, France and Gallipoli, 1914-18 (severely wounded, D.S.O. and bar, C.B., C.M.G., ment. in desps. five times); commanded Welsh Guards, 1920-24; commanded 1st Infy. Brig. (Guards); 1924-28; gov., S. Australia, 1928.

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1922), M.D., B.S.(Lond.); M.R.C.S.(Eng.); L.R.C.P.(Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Sch. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; apptd. to W. African medical staff, 1904 and has served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; recd. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910-12; personal ass't to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr. ad mem., exec. and leg. couns., Gambia, 1914-16; prim. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; att'd. Rockefeller oomsn. for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir. med. and sany. services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and Sany. Comtee. at C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; mem., exec. comtee. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y. of Med.; R. Soc'y. Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M.—B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agr. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schl. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922.

HORNE, EDWARD BUTLER, O.B.E. (1928).—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schl.; ast. coll'r., E. Africa Prot., 21st Apr., 1904; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1909; offr.-in-charge, Masai res., May, 1923; senr. comsnr. (2nd class), Kenya, Jan., 1924; senr. comsnr., 1st grade, 1926.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schl.; C.A., Jan., 1908; ast. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engnr. contracts, 1920; tech. ast., "Discovery Comte," 1923.
HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., desps.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, 1922.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schl., St. Helens; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 13th Aug., 1919; instr. of metalwks., Accra tech. schl., 10th Apr., 1920; ag. prin., 1st Dec., 1920 to 22nd Mar., 1921; house mast., 6th Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schl., 28th Apr., 1926.

HORWOOD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1896; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec. 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1895, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun., and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comsn. on Sealing Industry, 1918; pres., Newfild. centre of St. John Ambulance Assoctn.; chrmn. of comtee. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newfild. fund for hospital cots for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comsn. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.
