Bristol and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Hulme exhibr., Bridgeman prizeman, B.A.); ast. dist. comnr., East Africa, Prot., Mar., 1913; sec., cent. comtes. of supplies, 1914-15; hon. lieut., unattached list, 1915-17; ag. dist. comnr., 1918 and Dec. to July, 1921; ag. senr. ast. sec., Sept. to Nov., 1920 and July, 1921 to June, 1924; 1st grade admistr. offr., June, 1924; sen. ast. sec., Dec., 1924 to Mar., 1925; ag. ast. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1925 and in 1926; sec., local govt. comnr., 1926; ag. ast. col. sec., 1927.

LOGIE, HON. WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.B. (1918).—B. 1866; ed. Queen's Univ., Kingston, Canada; B.A. (lst cls. hon. in classics), 1887; M.A., 1888; LL.B., 1892; barrister, Canada, 1890; bencher, Upper Canada, 1811; entered Vol. Militia, 1883, and is maj.-gen. comdg. 2nd Div., Toronto; puisne judge of high ct., div. of Ontario, and a judge of Appellate div., 1918.
LOITGEN, W. G.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., 18th May, 1916 to 18th Feb., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Mar. 1920 to 27th June, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 27th June, 1922.
LONDON, GEORGE ERNEST.—B. 1889; ed. at Warwick, and Downing Coll., Camb. (schol.); lst in nat. sci. tripes, 1910; hist. tripes, 1911; B.A., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; offr., cl. V, 1914; ag.

LONG, CAPT. HERBERT EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Kendrick Schl., Reading; B.E.F., Egypt, Gallipoli, Salonika, Mar., 1915 to Feb., 1918; India and Aden, Mar., 1918 to April, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1921; H.B.M.'s vice-consul, Jigiga, Sept., 1927.

LONSDALE, CAPT. PHILIP.—Ed. at Sandhurst; comsn. in regular forces, 18th July, 1893; served on the N.W. Frontier (Tirah), 1898 (medal and clasp); with Ashanti Field Force, 1900 (medal and clasp, "Kumasi"); ag. dist. comsnr., Northern Territories, G. Coast, 1903-4; comdt., schl. of signalling, Dublin, 1905; asst. res., N. Nigeria, Mar., 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Oct., 1906.

LORAM, CHAS. TEMPLEMAN.—B. 1879; M.A., LL.B., Ph.D.; asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll. schl., 1896; asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Natal, seconded, May, 1901; asst. inspr. of nat. schl., Feb., 1906; asst. inspr. of schl., July, 1906; inspr. of schools, 1910; chief inspr., native educn., May, 1918; native affrs. comsnr., 1920; ch. inspr., educn., 1922.

LORD, GERALD FRANCIS.—Clt., P.M.G.'s dept., Natal, 1901; inspr. of educn., Transvaal, 1901-2; civil serv. examn., Natal, 1902; clt., lands and works dept., and rlys. and harbs. dept., 1903; senr. clt., 1906; prin. clt., 1910; ag. sec., rlys. and harbs. dept., in 1908 and 1909; prin. clt., admstr.'s dept., Natal Prov., 1910; chf. clt., Feb., 1918; ag. prov. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1925.

LORD, LIEUT.-COL J E C., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D.—Ent. Tasmanian civ. ser., 1886; commanded 40th Batt. Tas. A.I.F. in European War from Feb., 1915 to Aug., 1919; commands 12th Inf. Brig., Australian Mil. Forces; A.D.C. to the gov.-gen.; comsnr. of pol., Tasmania.

LORNE, M.A., B.Sc. Edin.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1901; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. asst. regist., sup. ct., and mag. and sheriff, Malacca, Apr., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1908; ag. temp. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1910; ag. dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1910; offr., cls. IV., May, 1911; ag. collr., land rev. and recvr. of deeds, Singapore, Aug., 1911; offr., cls. III., Feb., 1916; asst. dist. judge (civil) in addition, Jan.-Feb., 1917, July-Aug., 1920 and Nov.-Dec., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; offr. mem., leg. coun., May, 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., and regvr. of deeds, Singapore, 2nd Nov., 1921; ag. under sec., S. Stitltns., 28th Apr., 1922; comantr., lands, S.S., Sept., 1922 and June, 1926; dep. pres., munic. commsnr., Singapore, Mar., 1925; ag. Br. rep., Selangor, Sept., 1926; Br. res., Dec., 1926.
LOUDOUN, WILSON ANDERSON.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schl., Chatham, New Brunswick; asst. audr.-gen., 1902; audr.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1907; now comptroller-general.

LOUW, ERIC H.—B. 1891; ed. Beaufort West High Schl. and Univ. of Stellenbosch, S. Africa; B.A. and LL.B. (Cape); called to bar, 1916 and practised at sup. ct., Grahamstown; sl. M.P. for Beaufort West, 1924; first S. African commr. to U.S.A. and Canada, 1925.

LOUWRENS, HILLIGARD MULLER.—B. 1868; ed. Normal Coll., Cape Town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch and law classes, S.A. Coll., Cape Town; admitted as atty. and notary, sup. ct., Cape Town, 1892; pract. as atty., 1892-1903; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1906; pract. at sup. ct. bar, Cape Town, 1906-23; K.C., 1922; ag. water ot. jge., 1923; jge., sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope local divn., 1924.

LOVAT, 14TH BARON (CR. BEFORE 1440) MAJOR-GEN. SIMON JOSEPH FRASER, K.T. (1914), K.C.M.G. (1919), K.C.V.O. (1908), C.B. (1902), C.V.O. (1903), D.S.O. (1910).—B. 1871; ed. Fort Augustus Abbey and Oxford; late major 1st Vol. Batt., Queen's Own Cameron Highlanders and lieut., 1st Life Guards; served S. Africa as hon. maj. commdg. Lovat's Scouts (depts., D.S.O., C.B.).; served European war, Gallipoli, France and Flanders (K.C.M.G., depts.); vice-chmn. and chmn., comtee on agrl. research and adminstr. in the non self-governing dependencies, 1924-27; perly. under sec., D.O. and chmn., Overseas Settlement Comtee, 1927-1928; chmn., Overseas Settlement Comtee, 1st Jan., 1929.

LOVETT, HERBERT J.—A.C.A.; ed. at Marlborough coll.; chartered acctnt., England and Wales, 1900; asst. acctnt., G.P.O., Ceylon, 1901; ag. acctnt. on several occasions; on military duty, Sept., 1914; acctnt., G.P.O., Oct., 1916.

LOWE, ALEXANDER FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1924).—B. 1862; ed. Nelson Coll., New Zealand, and at priv. schl., England; filled various positions as offr. of New Zealand parl., 1885-1918; olk. of H. of R., 1915; elk. of parl. and elk. of legis. coun., 1919.

LOWE, CAPT. HERBERT JOHN, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1882; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; lieut., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1914; capt., R.A.V.C. (S.R.), 1916; war serv., France, 1914-18; vetry. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1918; senr. vetry. offr., Apr., 1923; ag. dep. ch. vetry. offr., May to Dec., 1926.

LOWINGER, VICTOR ALEXANDER.—B. 1879; ed. at Pietermaritzburg and S.A. Coll., Cape Town; Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1895-1904; licensed surv., Cape, 1901, Transvaal, 1902; sec., confee. of surveyors-gen., S. Africa, 1904; geodetic surv., Transvaal and O.F.S., 1904-06; trig. surv., F.M.S., 1906; sup't., trig. surveys, 1913; ag. asst. surv.-gen., 1918-19; ag. asst. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S. Stitltns. in addition, July, 1921 to Feb., 1922; ag. survr.-gen., F.M.S. and S. Stitltns., May, 1922; survr.-gen., July, 1922.

LUCAS, SIR CHARLES PRESTWOOD, K.C.B., (1912), K.C.M.G. (1907), C.B. (1901), B.A.—B. 1863; ed. at Winchester Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner; 1st cls. in mods., 1873, and 1st class in final classical schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1886; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1877; priv. sec. to Sir R. Herbert, Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1886, and again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1886; also chairman of comtee. of emigrants' information officer till 31st Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., Feb., 1892; priv. elk., Sept., 1896; asst. under-sec. of state, June, 1897; head of the Dominions div., 1907, in which capacity he visited Aust. and N. Zealand, 1909; registr. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1909-1911; ret., 1911; author of "Historical Geography of the British Colonies," "The Canadian War of 1812," "A History of Canada, 1763-1812," "Greater Rome and Greater Britain," etc.
LUCAS, HON. SIR EDWARD, K.T. BACH. (1921).—M.L.C., S. Australia 1900-1918; agt. gen. for S. Australia in London, 1918-24.

LUCETTE, EDWARD HENRY, B.A. (Oxon.), M.C.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Apr., 1915; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd. to Anuradhapura Kaan., June, 1921; attd., Trincomalee Kachcheri, Mar., 1922; attd. col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1922; prte. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to the gov., July, 1924 to Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1926; ditto, Kandy, Dec., 1926; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1928.

LUCIE-SMITH, SIR ALFRED VAN W., K.T. BACH. (1911).—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limassol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900; ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 1908; pres., W. Indian ct. of appeal, 31st May, 1920; ret., 1924.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN ALFRED, O.B.E., V.D.—B. 1888; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; col. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, 1905-06; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; advoc. and solr., S. Stltlts., 1910-14; on mily. serv., 1914; twice ment. in despatches; ag. pres., dist. ct., Trinidad, 1920; ass't. mag., Port of Spain, 1920-23; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Dec., 1926; ag. puisne judge for various periods, 1926-27; ag. crown pros., Nov., 1926; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.

LUCIE-SMITH, JOHN DUDLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Berkhamsted schl.; entd. civ. serv. of Jamaica, 1909; 2nd cls. clk., 1914; 1st cls. clk., 1920; sec., Westmoreland hurricane relief comm., 1912; sec. foot and mouth disease comm., 1922; ag. priv. sec. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Sydney Olivier) 1912; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of Jamaica (Sir Leslie Probyn), 1920-23; war serv., R.F.A., (France and Flanders) 1915-18; A.D.C. to C.R.A., 68th Div., 1918; W.O. staff, 1918; edr. of "The School Garden in Jamaica," and "Elementary Hygiene."

LUCKHAM, HAROLD ARTHUR LEE, B.A.—B. 1904; ed. Blundell's Schl., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 2nd cls. hon. sch. philosophy, politics and economics; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1928; attd., dist. off., Tampin, Feb., 1928.

LUDDINGTON, NORMAN JOHN, M. Sc., Durham Univ.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to the Jaffna Kachcheri, Dec., 1911; ag. pol. mag., Trincomalee, July, 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Mar., 1913; ag. office asst., Kurunegala Kachcheri, Aug., 1913; ag. asst. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Apl., 1914; resumed duties as office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1914; pol. mag., Negombo, May, 1915; Panadura, June, 1915; Negombo, Aug., 1915; Galle, Apl., 1916; Colombo, July, 1921; asst. comsnr., excise, July, 1923; asst. attlit. offr., Nov., 1926.

LUGARD, 1ST BARON (GR. 1928), RT. HON. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEATRHY LUGARD, P.C. (1920), G.C.M.G. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st comsnr., 9th foot, May, 1878; captain, Aug., 1880; major, Aug., 1896; lieut.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1880, medal; Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasp, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; Burmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, Feb., 1888; commanding expdn. against slavetraders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1892; exploration of Sabakhi; admnstr. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apl., 1895, Feb., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Brad Charterland Co.; app'td. comsnr., and comdt., West African frontier forces, Aug., 1897; high comntr. for Northern Nigeria, 29th Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, 1st May, 1907; gov., N. and S. Nigeria, 26th Mar., 1912; gov. gen., Nigeria, 1st Dec., 1913; assumed govt., 1st Jan., 1914; retired, 1919.

LUKE, HARRY CHARLES, C.M.G. (1926), Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1884; ed. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. med. hist., B.A., 1906; M.A., 1910; B. Litt., 1919; priv. sec. to the gov'r. of Sierra Leone (Sir L. Probyn, K.C.M.G.), 1908, and A.D.C., 1909; ag. asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, Mar. to July, 1909; priv. sec. to the gov'r. of Barbados, Feb., 1911; attached to C.O., May to July, 1911 (Coronation medal); priv. sec. to the high comntr. of Cyprus (Sir H. Goold-Adams, G.C.M.G.), and clk. of execo. coun., Oct., 1911; asst. sec. to gov'r., Cyprus, 1912; ag. comntr., Paphos, 1917; comntr., Famagusta, 1918; sec., Cyprus defence comtee., 4th Aug., 1914; served in H.M.S. Doris on Syrian Coast, 1914-1915; political offr. to Admirals Lord Wester Wemyss and Christian and gov't. sec., Mudros, Feb., 1915, to June, 1916; (lieut.-comdr., and ag. comdr., R.N.V.R.); recd. thanks of adm'y.; Italian med. for mily. valour; political offr. to Admiral of the Fleet Sir J. de Robeck, Constantinople and Black Sea, Aug., 1919; Br. ch. comntr. in Georgia, Armenia and Azerbaijan, Apr. to Sept., 1920; asst. gov. of Jerusalem, Oct., 1920; mem. of comnrs. on the Orthodox Patriarchate of Jerusalem, and on the Jaffe disturbances, 1921, and finan. comntr. of the Patriarchate, 1921-24; col. sec., Sierra Leone, May, 1924; ag. gov. on four occasions; ch. sec., Palestine, July, 1928; passed in Turkish and modern Greek (hona.); author of "A Bibliography of Sierra Leone," 1910 and 1925; "Cyprus under the Turks," 1921; joint editor, "Handbook of Cyprus," 1913 and 1920; "Handbook of Palestine," 1922.

LUKE, SIR JOHN PEARCE, K.T. BACH. (1921), C.M.G. (1917), M.P., A.M.I.M.E., J.P.—Ed. Penzance, Cornwall, and privately Wellington, N.Z.; M.P., 1908-11, and since 1917; Mayor of Wellington, 1913-21; el., life mem., N.Z. Returned Soldiers' Association, and holds Medaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise (3rd cls.) for war services.
LUKIN, LIONEL OSCAR.—B. 1868; puisne judge (central), supreme ct., Queensland, Dec., 1910-26; judge, fed. conciliation ct., 1926.

LUMLEY, JAMES MADDY.—B. 1878; ed. Royal Gram. Schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne; served R.H.A., 1895-1901; S. African war, 1899-1902; wounded; Queen's medal, four clasps; King's medal, two clasps; S. African constab., 1901-08; pub. proc. and just. of the peace, Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1904-08; asst. supt. of pol., R.A.P., 1909; served in E. Africa, 1914-18; King's pol. medal, 1919; supt., pol., Kenya, 1919; ag. asst. comntr., Kenya pol., various periods, 1920-24; asst. comntr., pol., 1925; dep. comntr., pol., 1926; ag. comntr., pol., 1927.

LUNDIE, CORNELIUS RONALD, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1878; asst. supt., surveys, Ceylon, Feb., 1908; supt., survey, Oct., 1910; ag. ast. surr.-gen. in 1919, 1920, 1923; ast. surr.-gen., Dec., 1923; ag. dep. surr.-gen. in 1924, 1926, 1927; dep. surr.-gen., Apr., 1927; ag. surr.-gen., Feb., 1928.
LUNNON, GEORGE JOSEPH.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LUSCOMBE, LAURENCE GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Heles schl., Exeter; served in S. African war, 1901-02; clk., Swaziland admin., Sept., 1902; ast. acctnt., Dec., 1909; acctnt., Apr., 1910; finan. secy., 1918; finan. sec., Basutoland, Sept., 1927.

LYALL-GRANT, R. W., M.A. (Aberdeen), L.L.B. (Edin.)—Vans Dunlop schol. in law, Edin. Univ.; called to Scottish bar, 1903; practised till 1909; atty.-gen., Nyasaland, Apr., 1909; ag. judge, high ct., Sept., 1909 to June, 1910, and from Aug., 1912 to May, 1913; judge of high ct., Aug., 1914, and mem., H.B.M. Ct. of Appeal for Eastern Africa; chmn., native rising comm., 1915; chmn. of claims bd. for Nyasaland, under Br. prots. defence o. in, 1916-19; judge advce. to Nyasaland-Rhodesia Field Force, 1917-18; atty.-gen., Kenya, July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. councils; puisne just., Ceylon, Feb., 1926.
LYDDERER, C. J.—Ast. dist. comsnr., R.A.P., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1926.

LYNCH, FRANCIS CHRISTOPHER CHISHOLM.—B. 1884; ed. at pub. schls., priv. tuition, McGill Univ.; assoc., Eng. Inst., Canada; assoc. mem., Town Planning Inst. of Canada; gen. engrg., 1902-05; apptd. to interior dept., 1906; ast. supt., rly. lands br., 1911; sup., nat. resources intell. br., dept. of int. Can., Dec., 1911.
LYNDE, WALTER MICHELLS, F.S.I. (Eng.)—B. 1883; ed. Cranleigh Schl.; quantity surr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1910; in charge sp. wk., Kabete, 1918; contracts offr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1923; ag. D.P.W., 1926.

LYON, WILLIAM ROY.—B. 1889; ed. at Mountjoy Schl., Dublin; 2nd lieut., 6th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.), Feb., 1909; Hythe certif., musketry and mach. gun, 1910; lieut., 1910; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, Nov., 1912; ag. dist. comsnr. for various periods, 1914-16; capt., 1914; major, 1916; served with Nigeria Carrier Corps in E. Africa, 1917; attached, 4th batt., Nigeria Regt., as O.C. details, Lagos, 1st Apr., 1918 to 20th May, 1918; distr. comsnr., 2nd ol., 29th Nov., 1917; passed lower standard native language (Temne).

MAARTENSZ, LEWIS MATTHEW.—B. 1876; barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple; addtnl. crown counsel, Ceylon, Apr., 1903; crown counsel, July, 1906; addtnl. dist. judge, Kandy, Oct., 1910; ditto, Colombo, Apr., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Apr. to Oct., 1916, Apr.-Dec., 1920, Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924; comsnr. of assize, Apr. to June, 1925; ag. puisne just. July to Sept. and from 30th Oct., 1925; dist. judge, Colombo, Jan., 1927; ag. puisne just., June, 1927.

MAASDORP, HON. SIR ANDRIES FERDINAND STOCKENSTROM, KT. BACH (1904).—B.A., Lond., 1869; barrister-at-law, Inn. Temp., Nov., 1871; mem. of the house of assem., Cape Colony, 1874 to 1878; solr.-gen., Cape Colony, Aug., 1878 to May, 1897; mem. of special (treason) court, Cape Colony, Oct., 1900, to June, 1902; ch. just. of the O.R.C., June, 1902; ret., Jan., 1920.

MACALISTER, GEORGE HUGH.—B. 1879; ed. Charterhouse, Cambridge and Guy's Hosp.; B.A., 1901; M.A. 1906; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., 1905; M.B., B.Ch.(Cant.), 1906; M.D., D.P.H.(Cant.), 1909; M.R.C.P., 1927; house phys. and astt. bacteriologist, Guy's Hosp.; astt. bacteriologist, Lister Inst., 1910; pathologist, Impl. Bact. Lab., Muktesar, U.P., India, 1914; capt., I.M.S., 1916; prin. and prof. of clin. med., Singapore Coll. of Med., since 1918; mem. and sec., S.S. & F.M.S. Med. Coun. and Dental Bd.; mem., coun. and senate, Raffles Coll.; edr.-in-ch., Malayan Med. Jnl.; rep. Malaya govt.s at Internat. Rabies Conf., Paris, 1927; on sp. serv., Gold Coast and Sierra Leone, 1927; reed. thanks of S. of S. for services in W. Africa; author various papers on med. and pathological subjects.

McARTHUR, ROBERT TAYLOR, D.S.O., A.M.I.E.E., M.(S.A.)I.E.E.—B. 1877; Imp. tels., 1890-97; dist. engr., Natal tel. dept., 1910; engr., dept. of posts and tels., Union of S. Africa, 1914; divn. engr., ditto, 1920; ch. engr., 1923.

MCCALL, LIEUT.-COL., FREDERICK JOHNSON, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., E.A.P., Mar., 1910; served in Great War, Oct., 1914 to June, 1918; M.C., 1917; 1914-15 Star, British War Med., twice ment. in desps.; major, A.D.V.S., 2nd divn., E. Africa Force, 5th May, 1916; A.D.V.S., G.H.Q., E.A. Force, 8th Aug., 1917; ch. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 26th Feb., 1919; dir., vety. services, 1st Apr., 1927.

MACCALL, THOMAS VERNON.—B. 1884; ed. Vickers' schl., Portsmouth, and Edin. Univ.; joined London Metro. Police, 6th Feb., 1905; served in C.I.D., New Scotland Yard; resig. 27th Mar., 1911; served in B. of T. labour exchanges, June, 1911, to Mar., 1914; astt. sup. of police, B. Honduras, 12th Apr., 1914; J.P., 23rd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July-Aug., 1914; visiting justice of dist. prisons, 1914-19; deputy atty.-gen., Corosal sessions, supreme ct., Sep., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, Feb.-Mar., 1915; comsnr. of supreme ct., 1st Apr., 1915; 2nd lieut., B. H. Volra., 7th Dec., 1915, attached to Corosal; lieut., Jan., 1917; served in Cayo Exped. Force, on Guatemalan frontier, Apr. to July, 1916; capt., B.H.T.F., 5th May, 1917; deputy atty.-gen., Corosal sessions, supreme ct., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Corosal, from 13th Nov., 1918.
MCCALLAN, E.A.—Clk., post office, Bermuda, 1903; clk., works dept., 1904; rev. offr., 1907; agric. astt., 1913; dir. of agric., Bermudas 1920.

MCCALLUM, ANGUS, M.C.—Kt. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin. and Aberdeen Univ.; capt., B.E.F., France, Aug., 1915 to Aug., 1918, M.C. and bar. ment. in desps.; lieut., 3rd K.A.R., Nov., 1918 to June, 1923; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1923.

MCCARTER, HARRY BERTRAM ADAIR.—B. 1883; entd. civil serv., Cape Colony, 1899; served in S. African war in Ingolond native levies, 1901-02; served in law dept., Transvaal civil serv., 1902 to 1907; clk., Swaziland admin., June, 1907; clk. and dep. astt.-comsnr., Apr., 1911; passed Cape Univ. div. ser. lower law exam., 1911.

MACDONALD, W. G. MACLEOD.—Imp. post office, 1899; transf'd. to combined post and tel. serv. of E. Africa and Uganda, 1908; dep. postmnr., 1910; oh. clk. and storekeeper, 1913; postmnr., 1913; in charge radio tels., Lake Victoria area, 1914; 1st cls. postmnr., 1918; asst. postmnr.-gen., 1922; ag. P.M.G., 1926.

MCDONALD, W. M.; O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (England), L.R.C.P. (London).—B. 1870; ed. Liverpool coll., and St. Bart.'s Hosp., London; house physician, St. Bart.'s, 1896; ag. med. offr., Montserrat, 1897; ditto, Antigua, 1899; med. offr., Montserrat 1901; med. offr., burgher refugee camp, Klarksdorp, Transvaal, 1902; med. offr., Antigua, 1904; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts, 1906; med. supt., hosp., etc., St. Johns, 1913; med. offr. of health, St. Johns, 1914; surgeon-lieut., Antigua defense force, 1914.

MACDONELL, SIR PHILIP JAMES, K.T.B.A.C.H. (1926), B.C.L., M.A.—B. 1873; ed. Clifton Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (schol.; 1st cls. hist. schls., 1894); of Gray's Inn, utter barrister; Bacon schol.; I. of C. prizeman, constitutional law; called to the bar, 26th Jan., 1900; pub. pros., Transvaal, Apr., 1906; asst. crown pros., Transvaal, Feb., 1907; legal adviser and pub. pros., N.W. Rhodesia, Oct., 1908; legal adviser and pub. pros., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1911; ag. judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, Apr. to Oct., 1914, Apr. to July, 1916, June to Nov., 1917; judge, high ct., N. Rhodesia, 13th Sept., 1918; chmn., Native Reserves Comsna., 1924-26 (East Luangwa) and 1926-7 (Railway Dists.); ch. just., Trinidad, 1927.

MCDONNELL, MICHAEL FRANCIS JOSEPH.—B. 1882; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and St. John's Coll., Camb. (scholar), B.A., 1904; pros., Cambridge Union, 1904; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1908; asst. dist. comsna., Gold Coast, Jan., 1911; pol. mag. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Apr., 1913; legal adviser, Gambia, July, 1918; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Aug., 1918; atty.-gen., Apr., 1920; ag. ch. just., circuit judge and judge of ct. of appeal on various occasions, 1919-23; ch. just., Palestine, 1927.

McDOUGALL, ARCH.—B. 1889; ed. West of Scot. Tech. Coll., Glasgow; civ. engr., P. and W. Anderson, rly. contractors, 1906-11; asst. engr., Caledonian Rly., 1911-12; M.I.E.S., 1914; engr., way and works dept., Demerara Rly. Co., Br. Guiana, 1912-21; engr., way and works dept., col. transport dept., 1922.

McDOUGALL, JOHN HENRY GORDON.—B. 1889; ed. Marlborough Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; 3rd cls. hons., schol. of jurisprudence, 1911; B.A., 1911; asst. dist. comsna., Uganda, Nov., 1911; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921; mag., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1921; senr. mag., 1925.

McELDERBY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD.—B. 1886; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schlr., B.A., sen. mod. in maths., sen. mod. in exp. science; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. deputy regsr., supreme ct., 1912; J.P., 1912; ag. 1st astt. sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. deputy regsr., supreme ct., 1913; ag. astt. dist. offr., Southern Dist., New Territories, 1913; apptd. as an offr. of 3rd grade, 1915; temporarily in C.O. 1915-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. astt. sec. for Chinese affairs, 1919 to 1920; attached, col. sec. office, 1920; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of comsna., Nov., 1920 to 1921; add. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Col. on opium question at internat. red cross confce., Bangkok, 1922; astt. col. sec. and clk. of comsna., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; astt. col. sec. and clk. of comsna., 1926; dep. oh. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1929.

McELWAIN, PERCY ALEXANDER, B.A. (Hons.), LL.B. (1907).—Ed., Campbell Coll., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to Irish bar (King's Inn) 1908; Alberta bar, 1913; temp. lieut., Royal Irish Rifles, B.E.F., 1916-19; stip. mag. and coroner, 2nd and 3rd dists., St. Lucia, 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Apr., 1923; senr. crown coun., Jan., 1926; ag. solr.-gen., Kenya and M.L.C., Oct., 1925 to Mar., 1926; atty.-gen., Fiji, 1927.
MACFADYEN, QUENTIN ALLISON ASHBY.—B. 1904; ed. Berkhampsted schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford cls. (3rd hons. hist.); cadet, Hong Kong, 1927.

MOFFALL, JOHN LYND, Barrister-at-Law, Middle Temple (1922).—B. 1888; educ. Coleraine Acad. Inst. and Queen's Coll., Galway; B.A., Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1909; cadet, Straits Stlitms., Nov., 1910; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Aug., 1913 to July, 1915; ag. astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, May to Dec., 1916; ditto, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1917; ag. astt. supt. Chandu monopoly, Negri Sembilan, Feb. to July, 1917; ag. prot., Chinese, Penang (cls IV., F.M.S.), Aug., 1920; add. to cust. dep., F.M.S., Aug., 1922; supermny. offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; dep. pub. prosecutor, Perak, Apr., 1923; ag. dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, Dec., 1924; astt. regir., sup. ct., Ipoh, Apr., 1925; astt. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Oct., 1925; dep. pub. pros., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Oct., 1925; ag. comsna., landa, F.M.S., Oct., 1927; offr. Cla. II., Nov., 1927; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., S'pore, Mar., 1928.
MACFARLANE, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.D., C.M. (EDIN.).—B. 1871; entered med. serv., Trinidad, 26th Jan., 1895; dist. med. offr., 1st Aug., 1915.

MACGILLIVRAY, JOHN WALKER, F.S.I.—B. 1884; 4th astt., trig. survey, Trinidad, 1901; 3rd astt., 1903; 2nd astt., 1904; 1st astt., 1906; qual. by exam. for diploma or sworn land survr., 1905; survr., Trinidad rly. extensions, 1909; survr., roads and traces, P.W.D., 1912; topographical astt., survey dept., 1917; crown survr., 1920.

MACGREGOR, ALASDAIR DUNGAN ARTHOLL.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., M.A. (Edin.), class. hons.; B.A. (Oxon) class. hons.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1909; dist. comsna., S. Nigeria; polit. dept., 20th Mar., 1912; astt. sec. col. sec.'s office, S. Nigeria, 1st Mar., 1913; statn. mag., Nigeria, 1st Apr., 1914; pol. mag., 1st Jan., 1917; crown coun., 17th Mar., 1921; solr.-gen., 16th Sept., 1923; atty.-gen., Trinidad, 31st July, 1926.

MACGREGOR, ALEXANDER JOHN, B.A. (Cape).—B. 1884; ed., Oriel Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. hons., mod. hist.; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1889; admitted to Cape sup. ct. bar, 1892; puisne judge, Eastern dist. local divn., Grahamstown, 1st Aug., 1913; puisne judge, O.F.S. prov. divn., 15th July, 1915.
MACGREGOR, CECIL JOHN.—B. 1888; ed. S.A. Coll. Schl., Capetown; mil. serv., 1916-19; dip. agr., Eisenberg Agr. Coll., Cape Prov., S.A., 1921; dist. agric. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1928.
MACGREGOR, ERNEST S.—B. 1867; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1890; Out Island comsna. (4th div.) and teacher, 1917.

MACGREGOR, Sir James Comyn., Kt. Bach. (1923), C.M.G. (1920).—2nd lieut. 7th battn. K.R.R., 1881; lieut., 1881; sub-inspr. Basuto-land mtd. pol., 1884; asst. commr., 1893; govt. sec. and master, Bech. Prot., Nov., 1912; ag. res. commr., May to Sept., 1913; special commr., Ngamiland, 1914; res. commr., Bech. Prot., 15th Dec., 1917; ret., 11th Nov., 1923.
MACGREGOR, John Grant, M.B., B.Ch. (Aberdeen).—Med. offr., S. Stirling, Feb., 1908.

MACGREGOR, Hon. William Cunningham, K.C. (1914).—B. 1862; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Otago Univ. (N.Z.); gold medallist, N.Z. Law Soc'y, 1883; solr.-gen., N.Z., July, 1920; judge, sup. ct., N.Z., 1923.
MACHARY, W.—Asst. local auditor, E.A.P., 1909; asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., 1914; deputy traffic man., 1917; supt. (admtr.), Jan., 1924.

MACHTIG, E. G. S., O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1889; educ. at St. Paul's and Trin. Coll., Cambridge (scholar) also at the Sorbonne, Paris; 1st cls. 2nd div., class tripas, Pt. I., 1911; B.A. (Cantab.), 1911; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 15th Oct., 1912; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Nov., 1917; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

MOILVENNEY, William Bernard, M.V.O. (1927).—B. 1867; ed. privately; N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1885-87; N.Z. defense dept., 1892-93; pol. constable, 1893; detective, 1896; ch. detective, 1907; sub-inspr., 1916; inspr., 1919; supt., 1924; commr. of pol., 1926.

MACKAY, Alexander Howard, B.A., B.Sc., LL.D., F.R.S.C.—B. 1849; ed. at T'ictow Academy, Provl. Normal Coll., and Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, 1873; principal, Annapolis Roy., 1873, Pictou, 1873-1889, and Halifax, 1889-1891, academies; lect. in biology, Dalhousie Univ., 1889-1910; pres., teachers' assoc. of Nova Scotia, 1874-1876, of Canada, 1898, of N. Scotia inst. of science, 1900, 1901, and 1902, of summer schl. of science, Atlantic provs., 1887 and 1888, of Victoria schl. of art and design, of biological sect. of roy. soc. of Canada; vice-pres. of religious educn. assoc. (America), of simplified spelling board (America); mem. of geographic bd. of Canada, of marine bd. biological laboratories of Canada, of American and British assoc. for advancement of science, etc.; gov. of Dalhousie univ., of Halifax ladies' coll., etc.; memb. of exec., Strathcona trust for Canada; editor of Dalhousie Gazette, 1870-1873, Educational Review, 1887-1891; Journal of Education, 1891 to date; supt. of educn. for prov. of N. Scotia, since 1891; representative of N. Scotia at Imperial Education Conference, 1911 and 1923.
McKEAG, Capt. V. M.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, May, 1920; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927.
McKEAN, J. D.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917; ag. dist. commr., Kenya, 1926.
MACKAY, Moray Sutherland, D.F.C.—B. 1899; ed. Merchiston Castle Schol., Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry); R.F.C. and R.A.F., 1917-19; asst. conservator of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925.

McKECHNIE, Robert McKechnie, A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1875; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Univ. Coll. (Lond.); loco. dept., L.S.W.R., 1896; engnr. inspr., C.A., 1905; asst. engnr., 1919; dep. head, engnr. inspection dept., 1922.

MACKENZIE, Capt. Angus Durham, M.A. —B. 1885; ed. Aberdeen and Edinburgh Univs.; 2nd cls. supervisor, cust., Dec., 1913; seconded for mily. serv. with Seaforth Highrs., June, 1915; staff capt., W.A.F.F., 1917; ag. intelligence offr., Gold Coast, from Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1918; contr., enemy property, offr. in charge, treasy. and cust., Togoland, July, 1920; rep. of these depts. in final handing over of Lome and that portion of Togoland ceded to the French, 1st Oct., 1920; colir. of cust., Jan., 1921; ag. asst. compt. custs., Aug., 1922; contr., enemy property, Gold Coast and Br. Togoland, in 1922, 1923 and 1924; provl. colir., cust., Feb., 1924; ag. dep. contr., cust., on various occasions, 1924-26; ag. contr., cust., June to Sept., 1926; offi. mem., Accra town coun., 13th Apr. to 8th Oct., 1926; dep. contr., cust., June, 1928.

MACKENZIE, H. A.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Acad. and Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; clk., crown agts. office, 24th Apr., 1901; asst. treas., Uganda l'rot., 20th June, 1907; treasy. offr., Kampala, 1st Oct., 1907 to Feb., 1909, and Oct., 1909 to Apr., 1910; asst. dist. commr., Entebbe, Aug., 1911 to Apr., 1913, and May, 1914 to May, 1915; ag. prin. regis. of docs. and admirar.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1913; dist. mag., Entebbe, for various periods, 1912-15; asst. dist. commr., Kampala, Nov., 1913 to May, 1914; second in command, Uganda armed levies (afterwards Baganda Rifles), May, 1914 to May, 1915; senr. asst. dist. commr., Kampala, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916; ag. dist. commr., Bunyoro, Nov., 1917 to May, 1918; dist. commr., Bunyoro, July, 1918 to Aug., 1919.

MACKENZIE, Hon. Sir Thomas, G.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1856; ent. N.Z. parl., 1887; min. of industries and commerce and min. of agr., N.Z., 1910; prime min. and min. of lands, 1912; high commr. in London, 1912-20; mem. of comtee for stintl. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.L.C., 1921.

MACKENZIE-KENNEDY, Henry Charles Donald Cleveland.—B. 1889; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1911; probat.ioner, dist. admnstr., N. Rhodesia, 7th Dec., 1912; asst. native commr., June, 1915; native commr., July, 1918; ag. asst. mag., Apr.-June, 1923; seconded to dept. of native affrs., Apr., 1925; asst. mag., Apr., 1926; represented N. Rhodesia at Imp. educn. confere., 1927; prin. asst. ch. sec., July, 1927.

McKIBBON, Hon. Harrison Andrew, B.A., LL.B., K.C., D.C.L.—B. 1861; ed. at Fredericton coll. schl., and Mt. Allison univ.; called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1886; elec. to legia., N.B., 1890; re-elec., 1899, 1903, and 1908; solr.-gen., 1903; atty.-gen., 1908; judge of sup. ct., N.B., 1909; chief just., King's Bench, 1916; chmn., bd. of rly. commrs., 1924.

McKERRON, Patrick Alexander Bruce.—B. 1896; ed. Fettes Coll., Edin.; 2nd lieut. Gordon Highrs., 1914; capt., 1916; trans. I.A.R.O., 1917; capt., 20th Punjab, 1918; demob., Feb., 1919; cadet, S.S., Feb., 1920; attd., treas., Singapore; ag. asst. aspt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, Sept., 1921; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1921.

MACKIE, George Douglas, M.B.E. (1923).—Ag. 2nd olk., govt. office, St. Vincent, Dec., 1890, to Apl., 1891; 2nd olk. and cashier, treasy., Apl., 1891, to May, 1892, and from Jan., 1897 to Oct., 1898; clk., crown lands and survey office, May to Dec., 1892; audit elk., Jan., 1893, to Dec., 1896; 2nd. clk. and acctnt., treasy., Grenada, Nov., 1898; ch. elk., Jan., 1903; ch. sub-collr. and revenue offr., treasy., St. Lucia, Jan., 1911; ag. treas. on several occasions in 1911 to 1915; treas., Nov., 1915; ag. col. sec. in 1923, 1924 and 1925; admstd. govt., July to Sept., 1925.
MCKINLEY, JOHN, B.A.—Served with I.Y. in S. African campaign, 1900-1; acctnt., and statistician, educn. dept., O.R.C., 1 July, 1902; provincial acctnt., O.F.S. prov., 15th Aug., 1910.
MCKINNEY, E. H.—B. 1877; ent. Bahamas civ. ser., 1896; Out Island commnr. (2nd div.), 1909; commr. (1st div.), 1912.
MACKINNON, Hon. D.—Attorney-gen. and min. of hwys., Victoria, Dec., 1913; dir.-gen. of recruiting, Commonwealth of Australia, during the European war; commr. for C. of A. to U.S.A., 1923.

MACKINNON, WILLIAM LUNAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), 1911.—B. 1888; astt. auditor, G. Coast, Nov., 1912; ag. senr. astt. auditor, in 1918 and 1919; ag. astt. treas. (in addition to his own duties), May to Sept., 1919; ag. senr. astt. auditor, Oct., 1919.
MACKLIN, MAJOR RODRIGUE WILLIAM, M.C., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1891; serv. with R. Artillery in the Gt. War, 1914-18; travelling commr., Gambia, 6th Mar., 1920.

MOLAREN, HUGH, A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1878; European instr. tech. schl., Accra, G. Coast, June, 1909; astt. mast., training institution, Accra, July, 1910; ag. prin. ditto, on several occasions; prin., Accra Tech. Schl., July, 1919.

MACLAREN, JOHN SUTHERLAND, M.A. (hons.)—B. 1900; Fettes Coll. and Edin. Univ.; M.A. (hons. class.) 1921; Fettes exhib., Lamone schol., Ritchie schol., 1st equal Gray Essay Prize, 1922; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923; ag. 3rd and 2nd astt. to sec. for Chinese affs., 1924; passed in Cantonese, Dec., 1925; astt. treas. and astt. assessor, 1926; astt. estate duty commr., 1927.

MACLEAN, ADRIAN JOHN.—Ed. at Selwyn Coll., Camb., B.A., 1895; M.A., 1901; astt. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th May, 1901; collr., 1st Apr., 1906; recorder of titles, 1911; senr. commr., Kenya, May, 1920.

MCLEAN, F. H.—B. 1889; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to bd. of educn., May, 1908; transf. to C.O., Dec., 1911; on military serv., from 25th Apr., 1917, to 23rd Feb., 1919; cler.offr., higher grade, 24th Jan., 1922.
MACLEAN, G., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), M.R.E. (1928).—Temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Mar., 1918 to Mar., 1919; temp. capt., Mar., 1919 to June, 1920; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1921.

MACLEAN, Hon. John D., M.D.—B. 1875; ed. pub. schls., Prince of Wales Coll., and normal schl., P.E.I.; schl.-teacher for some time; removed to British Columbia, 1893, where engaged in teaching until 1901; graduated in medicine, McGill Univ., 1905; returned to B. Columbia, taking up practice in Greenwood; mayor of Greenwood for two years; elec. to legis. assem., 1916; prov. sec. and min. of educn. in Brewer cabinet, B. Columbia, 1916; min. of finance, educn. and industries in Oliver cabinet, 1924.

MCLEAN, JOHN DOUGLAS.—B. 1866; ent. civ. ser., Canada, 1876, as 3rd cl. clk.; promoted to junr. 2nd cl., 1878; senr. 2nd cl., 1882; 1st cl., 1887; ch. clk. and sec. of the dept. of Indian affs., 1897; astt. dep. supt. gen. and sec., 1908.

MCLEAN, SIMON JAMES, M.A., LL.B., Ph.D.—B. 1871; commr. of the Canadian govt. to report on rly. rate grievances, 1901; advr. to commr. of govt. to draft Railway Act, 1903; prof. of polit. econ., Toronto Univ., 1908-1909; apptd. a mem. of rly. commr., Canada, 1906; re-apptd., 8th Nov., 1918; astt. chief commr., 6th Aug., 1919.

MACLENNAN, Finlay Malcolm.—B. 1857; ed., Tarbutt Public Sch.; Sant Ste. Marie, Ont., High Sch.; ent. serv. of dept. of rlys. and canals, Can., as officerman, Sant Ste. Marie Canal, Sept., 1910; transferred, Port Nelson on Hudson Bay terminals work, as jun. engnr., 1912; astt. audr., dept. rlys. and canals, Ottawa, 1st Apr., 1915; audr., 1917; ch. audr., 1918; astt. comptr., 1920.
MOLEOD, Hon. Alexander Donald, M.P.—B. 1872; ed. privately; mem. N.Z. parl. since 1919; min. of lands, 1924; min. of industries and commerce, 1926.

MOLEOD, David William, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Cent. Training Coll. and Aberdeen Univ.; senr. astt. King's Coll., Seychelles, 1910; prin., King's Coll and inspr. schls., Seychelles, 1913; on active serv., 1914-19; senr. astt., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Feb., 1920; ag. prin., ditto, May, 1920; ag. head mast., Outram Rd. Schl., Singapore, Feb., 1921; ag. inspr., schls., Singapore, Mar., 1921; prin., King Edward VII Schl., Taiping, Mar., 1922.

MOLEOD, Hon. Sir Ezekiel, Kt. Bach. (1917), B.C.I. (Harv.), LL.D. (New Brunswick univ.).—B. 1840; ed. at high schl., King's co., New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1868; Q.C., 1882; atty.-gen. of N.B., 1882-1883; mem. of senate of Univ. of N.B., since 1869; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1891; judge, sup. ct. of N.B., and judge of N.B. v.-a. dist., 1896; chief just., N.B., 1914-1917.

MOLOUGHLIN, A. P.—Entd. civ. serv., O.R.C., 23rd Mch., 1900; ag. distributor of stamps, Transvaal, 21st Aug., 1900; distributor of stamps, 1901; ch. clk., treasy., 1st July, 1903; ch. clk., dept. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910; under sec. for finance, Union of S. Africa, 1st Aug., 1912; commr. for inland rev., Oct., 1916.

MACLURKIN, Alfred Robert.—M.B., Ch.B. Glasg., D.P.H., R.C.P. and S. Edin., R.F.P. and S. Glasg., D.T.M. Liverpool; ed. Allan Glen's, Glasgow, Univ. of Glasg., Univ. of Liverpool Schol. of Trop. Med.; R.M.O. Grey's Hosp., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1901; dist. surg., Ngutu, Zululand, 1902; A.M.O. Metrop. Asylm. Bd., 1906; astt. educ. med. offr., Surrey C.C., 1908; gov't. med. offr., Cook and N. Ields., 1911; astt. educ. med. offr., Derbyshire C.C., 1914; gov't. med. offr., Fiji, 1915; ag. med. supt., leper asylum, Makogai, 1916; dist. commr., dist. med. offr. and M.O.H., Bua Prov., 1917; D.M.O., Taviuni, 1919 and 1925; Savu Savu, 1920; Labasa, 1922.

MCMAHON, Cecil.—Knt. S. Rhodesia civ. serv., Nov., 1909; served S. African rebellion and German S.W. African campaign, 1914-15; served E. African campaign with 1st Rhodesian Regt., 1915-18; capt. and adjt., 8th S. African Inf. Regt.; "1914-15" Star and M.C.; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 5th Mar., 1918.

MCMILLAN, Sir Daniel Hunter, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1846; ed. Canada; served with Canadian Volunteers on Niagara frontier, 1864; during Fenian raid, 1866; Red River expdn., 1870; N.W. rebellion (medal), 1880; mem. for Winnipeg in Manitoba legislature, 1880; mem. of Manitoba govt., 1889; lt.-gov. of Manitoba, 6th Sept., 1900; re-apptd. for second term, 1906; retired 1911.
MCMILLAN, L. B.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schls., Charlottetown, Prince Edward Is.; messenger, P.E. Is. govt., 1890; clk., 1893; sec. for pub. wk., P.E. Is., 1900.

McMILLAN, HON. SIR ROBERT FURSE, K.C.M.G. (1925), KT. BACH (1916).—Queen's scholar, Westminster school; scholar, Trin. Hall, Camb.; senior in lawtropes, 1879; holder of law studentship at Trin. Hall; Inns of Court studentship and Inner Temple common law scholarship; called to the bar, 1881; joined western circuit and Devon and Exeter sessions; puisne judge, W. Australia, 1903; ch. justice, Dec., 1913.
McMURRAY, HON. EDWARD JAMES, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Manitoba Univ. and Man. Law Schl.; barrister; el. to H. of C., Can., 1921; solr.-gen. in King adminstr., 1923.

McNAB, HON. ARCHIBALD P.—B. 1864; ed. pub. and high schls., Vankleek Hill; elec. to prov. legis., Sask., 14th Aug., 1908; min. of mun. affairs, 10th Dec., 1908; re-elec. by acclamation at bye-elec., 24th Dec., 1908, and g.e., 1912, 1917, and 1921; min. of pub. wks., 1913.

MACNAUGHTEN, T. C., C.M.G. (1923), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1872; ed. Charterhouse and Oxford; scholar of Hartford Coll., 1892; 1st class classical moda., 1894; B.A., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., Local Govt. Bd., Feb., 1896; 2nd class clk., C.O., Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Earl of Selborne, Dec., 1899, to Earl of Onslow, Nov., 1900, to Duke of Marlborough, July, 1903; 1st cl. clk., 24th Mar., 1904; principal clk., 6th July, 1917; chmn. E.I.O., Oct., 1912; mem. of comttee. for stltmt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; vice-chmn., Oversea Stltmt. Comttee., 1919; attended internat. confere. on migration and immigration, Rome as tech. adviser to Viscount Ulswater, the Br. del., 1924; mem., inter-deptl. comttee. to consider effect on migration of schemes of social insurance, 1925-26; visited Canada with Earl of Clarendon, chmn. of the overseas settlement comttee., in connection with Br. settlement, 1926.
McNAMARA, GEORGE.—B. 1881; entd. N.Z. post and tel. dept., 1896; supt. of staff, 1916; 2nd ast. sec., 1920; 1st ast. sec., 1923; sec., 1926.

McNEICE, THOMAS PERCY FERGUS, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bradford Gram. Schl. and Keble Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1925; addn., Chinese sect., Kuala Lumpur, Jan., 1925; in Hong Kong studying Chinese, Aug., 1925; addn., Chinese prot., Singapore, Dec., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Mar., 1928.

McNEILL, JAMES.—B. 1869; ed. Belvedere Schl., Dublin, Blackrock Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; ent. Indian civ. ser., 1890; regist., co-op. socs., 1904; on sp. duty in Fiji and W. Indies in connection with Indian immigr.; comnrr., cent. div., Bombay presidency; addn. mem., Imp. leg. coun., India; chmn., Dublin county coun., 1922; mem., comttee. apptd. by the provisional govt. to draft constitution for Irish Free State, 1922; high comnrr., Irish Free State, 1922; gov. gen., 1923.

McNULTY, THOS. SYDNEY.—B. 1870; ed. at Marist Coll., Sydney; clk., acta. branch, wks. dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1896; ch. corres. clk., ditto., 1897; mem. mun. coun., Claremont, 1901-3; sec. to goldfields water supply adminstr., 1903; under-secr. for agric. and industries, 1909.

MOOWAN, ISLAY.—B. 1871; ed. C. of E. Grammar Schl., Melbourne, Victoria; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Fiji, Sept., 1892; stip. mag., ast. native comnrr., and tax inspr., Lau, May, 1896; stip. mag. and tax inspr., Navua, and comnrr. for Namasi, Feb., 1900; stip. mag., Lomaiviti and Tailevu; comnrr. and tax inspr., Lomaiviti; offr. in charge, Levuka gaol, Jan., 1904; inspr. gen. of constab. and prisons, and sheriff, Sept., 1909 M.L.C., 1909; seconded as ag. H.B.M.'s consul. and agent, Tonga, Apr., 1911 to Mar., 1911; ag. native comnrr., Oct., 1913; offr. comdmg. local forces, and lieut.-col., defence forces of Fiji, Aug., 1914; ment. by Army Coun. for services in 1914 in connection with defence of Fiji: British agt. and consul., Tonga, June, 1917, also judi. comnrr. and dep. comnrr., Tonga, June, 1917; ag. col. sec. and M.E.C. Fiji, May, 1921; gov.'s dep., Aug., 1921; resumed duty, Tonga, June, 1922; chmn., retrenchment comttee., pub. serv., Feb., 1922; sec., native affrs. and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Fiji, July, 1926; ag. col. sec., July, 1926, and Nov., 1927 to A.pr., 1928; gov.'s dep., July, Aug. and Oct., 1926.

MACPHAIL, JOHN ADDISON.—Ck., P.W.D., Transvaal, 1903; ch. clk. to govt. archt., July, 1904; ch. clk., P.W.D., Transvaal, Mar., 1907; under-secr. for pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1920; ag. sec. for pub. wks., Sept., 1924 to Jan., 1925.

MACPHERSON, BARBARA.—Ed. Durness H.G. Schl. and Skerry's Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after compet. exam. typist, Lond. tel. serv., 1912; transf. to C.O., May, 1918; shorthandwriter, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., Jan., 1923.

MACPHERSON, JOHN STUART.—B. 1898; ed. Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ. (M.A., 1921); 2nd lieut. A. & S. Highlanders, Jan., 1918; France, Apr., 1918-Jan., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ag. 4th ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1921; passed cadet, July, 1922; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Aug., 1922; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., May, 1923; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1923; offr., cls. V, Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Bentong, Oct., 1925; lieut., M.V.I., Dec., 1925; ag. capt., Dec., 1926.

MO'PHILLIPS, HON. ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1861; ed. St. Boniface and Manitoba Colls.; barrister; K.C., 1900; elected to leg. assem., B.C., 1898; atty.-gen., 1903; subsequently pres. of coun.; judge, ct. of appeal, B.C., 1913; served N.W. rebellion under Gen. Middleton, 1886.

MCQUADE, JOHN HEBBERT.—B. 1883; ed. privately; highest marks in U.K. in entrance exam. for cust. and excise, Mar., 1913; Imp. cust. and excise, July, 1913; served in Flanders with R. Arty. and Chapperton Down Arty. schl. with R. Engnr.; ast., cust., Kenya, 2nd July, 1920; collr., cust., 24th Aug., 1924.

MACQUARIE, EDMUND JEFFREY.—B. 1883; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana and Caius Coll., Cambridge; B.A., L.B.B., 1905; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1906; ag., stip. mag., B. Guiana, June to Nov., 1906, and July, 1907 to Mar., 1914; dormant comnrr. as stip. mag. and apptd. J.P. and comnrr. of oaths, Dec., 1907; ast. dist. comnrr., Gold Coast, 1915; crown counsel, 1916; ag. chief regis., Apr.-May, 1917; ag. senr. crown counsel, 1917; ag. pol. mag., Nov., 1917 to Apr., 1918; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1919; ag. ch. regis., Apr. to Oct., 1919; solr.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. in 1923, 1924 and 1927.
MACQUILLAN, CECIL JAMES, B.A., M.B., R.C.H. (T.C. Dublin).—M.O., R.A.F.(res); served U.K. and Irak, 1924-26; R.M.O., Kent and Canterbury hosps., 1926; medl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1927.
MACRAE, DONALD MACKENZIE, M.B., C.M., Glasgow Univ. (1896); M.D. (1920); F.R.G.S.—Surg. capt., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., 1903; surg. major, 1915; prin. med. offr., 1917; J.P., 1920.
MACRAE, DUNCAN MACKENZIE, M.A., Glasgow (1906); M.B., Ch.B. (1900); M.D. (1921).—Med. offr., Bechuana land Prot., 1919.
MACRAE, LACHLAN, M.A. (Aberdeen), M.A. (Cantab.).—B.B. 1886; dir., educn., Ceylon, Jan., 1922; M.I.C.

McROBERTS, CAPT. B. A. K.—Natal mounted pol., 1903-08; Natal rebellion, 1906; Usutu operations, 1907; inspr., veterinary pol., E.A.P., 1909; asst. inspr., pol., Mar., 1910; asst. supt., Sept., 1911; capt., Gordon Highlanders, Aug., 1915; France, 1916; called to bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1917; attd., Highland Light Infy., Mesopotamia, Mar., 1917; seconded, polit. dept., dist. mag. and dep. mily. gov., Basrah, 1917-18; mily. mission to Siberian gov., 1919; supt., pol., Kenya Colony, May, 1920; mag., Zanzibar Prot., Jan., 1921; ag. asst. judge, Mar. to Oct., 1922 and May to Dec., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1925; pol. mag., Sierra Leone, 28th Apr., 1926.

McSWEENEY, D. C. J.—B. 1903; ed., Christ's Coll., Cambridge (schol.); ist cl., English tripes, June, 1923; 1st cl., oriental languages tripes, June, 1925; apptd. after compet. exam. asst. prin., W.O., 17th Oct., 1925; transf'd to C.O., 11th Oct., 1926.

MOTURK, WILLIAM HENRY, F.S.L.—B. 1883; survr., asst., dept. lands and mines, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1901; land survr., 1904; 2nd cls. offr. and govt. survr., 1906; J.P., 1908; land offr., Pomeroon dist., 1910; drawing office aspt., Jan., 1919; ag. asst. commr., lands and mines, Apr. to Nov., 1920 and May to July, 1923; in char., dept., lands and mines, June to July, 1920; ch. clk., 1922; land offr. and survr., Gambia, Nov., 1924.

MACWILLIAM, DONALD KIRTON, M.B.E. (1926).—2nd clk., treasry., St. Kitts, 1949; rev. offr. and quarantine offr., Apr., 1894; rev. offr. and harb. mast., 1906; 1st rev. offr. and harb. mast., 1st Apr., 1915; mem., poor law bd., Mar., 1918; mem., quarantine bd., St. Kitts, 3rd May, 1921; sp. serv. in connection with small-pox epidemic and quarantine measures.

MADELEY, WALTER BAYLEY.—B. 1873; ed., Bombay High Schl.; apprenticed Woolwich Arsenal, England; came to S. Africa, 1903; represented Benoni in Union Parlmt. since 1910; associated with amalgamated engineering union; min. of posts and tels. and pub wks., Nov., 1925.
MADIGE, JOHN BRISTO CULLER, M.B. (Edin.), Ch.B.—B.1892; med. offr., Zanzibar, 4th Feb., 1926.

MAGENNIUS, WILLIAM BABINGTON.—B. 1869; ed., pub. schl., Uitenhage; clk., mag.'s office, Port Elizabeth, 1888; Uitenhage, 1895; Willowmore, 1893; asst. mag., Uitenhage, 1894; Willowmore, 1895; Beaufort West, 1896; Vryburg, 1897; Somerset East, 1899; dep. commdt., several places during Anglo-Boer War and dep. admstr., martial law; mag., Aberdeen, 1902; Namaqualand, 1905; commnr., relief distress, N.W. Districts 1908; commnr., Robben Is., lunatic and leper asylums, 1909; inspgr. mag. and inspr. prisons, 1912; sen. mag., East London, 1920; sp. grade mag., Kimberley, and regis. of deeds, 1923; sp. A mag., Cape Town, 1926.
MAGILL, CHARLES VICTOR.—Asst. dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Nov., 1919; dir., posts and tels., Somaliland, Apr., 1926.

MAGRATH, CHARLES ALEXANDER.—B. 1860; ed., priv. tuition; irrig. and development work in S. Alberta from 1878; mem. for Lethbridge in N.W.T. assm. for several years, and finally mem. of prov. cabinet; al. to H.C., Can., 1908, for Medicine Hat; def., 1911; fuel contr., Can., 1917-20; chmn., Internatl. Joint Comsn., Can. sect., 1921.
MAHABIR, F.—B. 1888; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Trinidad, 7th Apr., 1915.

MAHAFFY, ROBERT PENTLAND.—B. 1871; ed., Marlborough (foundation schol.) and King's Coll., Cambridge (open schol., 1st cls., in hist. tripes; Whewell schol., Prince Consort's prize-man; Worts travelling schol.); editor, Calendar of State Papers, 1896-1914; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1901; author, "Law relating to Motor Cars" (1910, 1912, 1922), "Life of the Emperor Francis Joseph" (1908, 1914), several vols. of Calendar of State Papers; temp. asst., Devon Regt., 1914; severely wounded, 1st July, 1916; sp. serv., Sudan, 1917-19; asst. legal adviser, Rhineland High Coman., 1919-20; legal adviser to gov. of Malta, 6th June, 1921, and to H.M. Forces at Malta, 2nd Nov., 1921.
MAHONY, DAVID BERNARD, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed., Blackrock Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1921.
MAINOT, ELLIOT FRANCIS.—B. 1891; ed., Ushaw Coll., Durham; served articles of clerkship in London and qualified as solr., 1914; crown solr., admstr. gen. and off. receiver, Trinidad, 1st Dec., 1922.

MAJOR, SIR CHARLES, KT. BACH. (1911).—Called to the Bar (Mid. Temp.), June, 1887; mem. of Leeward Is. bar, Sept., 1887; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, Oct., 1889; nominated mem. of legis. coun., Antigua, 1896 to 1899; nominated mem. of gen. legis. coun., Leewards, Nov., 1896, and pres. of same, Dec., 1896 to 1899; mem. fed. ex. coun., Leewards, Mar., 1897, to June, 1901; vice-pres. legis. coun., Antigua, May, 1897; ag. sol.-gen. and atty.-gen., Leewards, 1897, 1898, 1899; capt. commanding Antigua volunteer defence force on its establishment, 1897; J.P., 1897; ag. 2nd puisne judge, Leewards, June, 1899; atty.-gen. of Grenada, 1901; ch. just. of Fiji, and chief judicial commr. of Western Pacific, 1902; admstd. govt., Mar.-Oct., 1904, and in 1908, 1910, and 1912; chief just. B. Guiana, 1914; ret., 1926.
MALAN, HON. CHARL WYNAND.—B. 1883; ed., Victoria College, Stellenbosch; mem. for Humansdorp, Union H. of A. since 1915; min. of rlys. and harbrs. in Hertzog cabinet, 30th June, 1924.
MALAN, HON. D. F., M.A., D.D.—M.L.A., Calvinia Divn., Union of S. Africa; min. of int., pub. health and educn., 30th June, 1924.

MALAN, RT. HON. FRANCOIS STEPHANUS, P.C. (1920).—B. 1871; sd. in S. Africa and Camb.; B.A. Science, Cape univ.; LL.B., Camb.; advocate of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 1896; M.L.A. for Malmsbury, 1900; re-elected, Feb., 1904; min. of agric., Cape Colony, 1908; min. of educn., Union of South Africa, 1910; attended Imp. conf., 1911; min. of mines, industries and educn., 1912; min. for agr., 1920; min. of mines and industries, 1921-24; defeated, gen. elec., 1924.

MALAN, FRANCOIS STEPHANUS.—B. 1882; ed., Normal Coll., Capetown and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, B.A.; inspr. schls., Transvaal, 1916; prin., Normal Coll., Ermelo, Transvaal, 1919; sec., Transvaal educn. dept., 1924.

MALCOLM, HARDCOURT GLADSTONE, O.B.E. (1918): K.C. (1910).—Priv. sec. to Sir Henry Jackson when admstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1895; ditto to Sir W. F. Haynes-Smith, gov. of Bahamas, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; mem. of H. of A., Bahamas, 1900; deputy-speaker, 1901; re-elected, 1904 and 1911; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1905; Sept., 1906 to Feb., 1907; July to Nov., 1910; Apr. to Dec., 1911; May to July, and Aug. to Nov., 1913; ag. chief just., 1913 and 1927; speaker, H. of A., 1913; re-elected, 1913 and 1927; re-elected speaker, 1919; thanked by S. of S. for assistance to atty.-gen in drafting bills; thanked by legis. for obtaining copies of early records; Bahamas del. to Canada—W. Indies conferees at Ottawa, 1920 and 1925; Bahamas rep. during visit of Prince of Wales, and partly, ter-centenary celebrations at Bermuda, Oct., 1920; Bahamas del. to W. Indian conferees, London, 1926.
MALCOLM, JAMES.—B. 1880; ed. Upper Canada Coll.; el. to House of Commons, Canada, 1921; min. of trade and commerce, Dominion of Canada, 1926.

MALE, STANLEY FRANCIS.—B. 1897; ed. Wellington Schol., Somerset and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 2nd mod. language mast., Wellington Schol., Somerset, 1919-20; war serv., France, 1915-19; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; recorder of proceedings, educn. conferees, Tanganyika Territory, 1925.
MALONE, DENIS KERON.—B. 1903; ed. Cooper's Company Schol.; cler. offr., C.O., 29th Mar., 1921.

MANDERS, RICHARD HENRY DAVID, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1904; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1927; attd., secretariat, Feb., 1927; attd., Kandy kach., Oct., 1927; ag. office asst., Kurunegala kach., May, 1928.
MANGAN, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Served in Bechuanaaland Prot. pol., 1910-14; elk., 1914; ag. asst. res. mag., 1921; passed 2nd grade Secwana exam., 1917; ag. res. mag., 1922.

MANGIN, THORLICK RATTRAY ORDE.—B. 1896; ed. Marlborough Coll.; sub lieut., R.N.V.R. and attd. to Royal Naval Dvn., 1915; qualified, gunnery course, H.M.S. Excellent, 1916; apptd., to H.M.S. Repulse and served with Grand Fleet, 1916-1919; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1919.
MANNERS, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1884; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 22nd Mar., 1911; dist. commnr., 19th Feb., 1916.

MANNING, RICHARD JOSEPH.—B. 1883; ed. Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; sub-inspr. of police, B. Guiana, Oct., 1909; dist. inspr., Sept., 1912; ag. stip. mag., W. Coast, Demarara, June, 1919; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1920; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 9th Sept., 1920; res. mag., Jamaica, 20th July, 1925; pol. mag., Gold Coast, 9th Nov., 1927; higher certif. in Hindi, Mar., 1916; higher certif., Swahili, 1922; compiler, "Br. Guiana Police Manual," Doc., 1918.
MANNING, WILLIAM.—B. 1892; on active serv., Sept., 1914 to Feb., 1919; inspr., pol., Zanzibar, Mar., 1919; supdt., 1924; ag. asst., commdt., Oct., 1926 to June, 1926.

MANNING, BRIG.-GEN. SIR WM. HENRY, G.C.M.G. (1921); K.C.M.G. (1904); K.B.E. (1918); C.B. (1903).—B. 1863; ed. at Camb. and Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; lieut., 2nd batt., S. Wales Bord., 24th Foot, 1886; capt., 1897; brevet-major, 1898; brevet-lieut.-col., 1899; brevet-col., 1904; joined Indian army, 1888; 51st Sikhs, frontier force, 1891; 2nd Burmese war (wounded, medal and clasp); Samana and Hazara expedns., N.W. frontier of India, 1891 (clasp); C. Africa, 1893-4 (medal); C. Africa and Rhodesia, 1894-98 (clasp); Somaliland campaign (medal and 2 clasps); dep. comsnr. and cons.-gen. for B.C. Africa, 1897; acted as comsnr. and cons.-gen., Dec., 1897 to Dec., 1898, and also from July, 1900 to Apr., 1901; raised and commd. C. Africa regt.; commd. Somaliland field force, 1902-1903; commd. 1st brig., Somaliland field force, 1903-4; inspr.-gen., K.A.R., 1901; ag. gov. of Nyasaland Prot., 1907; gov., Somaliland Prot., 7th Jan., 1910; gov., Nyasaland Prot., 5th Nov., 1910; gov., Jamaica, Jan., 1913; gov., Ceylon, 31st May, 1918; assumed govt., 11th Sept., 1918; recd. Coronation Medal, 1911; ret., 1925.

MANSFIELD, CAPTAIN EDWARD THORNTON.—B. 1878; entered Gray's Inn, 1888; lieut., R. Lancashire Regt., 1901; served in S. African war (medal, four clasps); 2nd cls. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, 9th Dec., 1909; ag. adjutant, G. Coast Rly. and Mines Volunteers, 26th Nov., 1910 to 26th Feb., 1911; asst. dist. comsnr., Apr., 1913; seconded for serv. with Field Force Togoland, May, 1915 to Apr., 1916; dist. polit. offr., Msimoho, Togoland, June, 1918 to 30th Sept., 1920; dist. comsnr., 29th Apr., 1919; dist. polit. offr., Ho, Oct., 1920.

MARCH, HARRY THOMAS.—B.1881; ed. King's Coll., London; 2nd divn. elk., post office, 1900-07; acctnt., post office, Nigeria, 20th Apr., 1907 to 31st Jan., 1912; postmr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1st Feb., 1912.
MARCHANT, W. S.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Kenya, 1926.

MARCIIL, HON. CHARLES, LL.D., Ottawa Univ.—B. 1860; elec. to H. of C. of Canada for Bonaventure, 1900; dep. speaker of H. of C., 1905-1908; rd-elec. at gen. elec., 1908, 1911, 1917, and 1921; speaker of the H. of C., 1909; mem. of P.C. for Canada, 1911.

MARLBOROUGH, 9TH DUKE OF (created, 1702), CHARLES RICHARD JOHN SPENCER-CHURCHILL, P.C., K.G.—B. 1871; Chancellor of Primrose League, 1897 and 1898; served with yeomanry cavalry, S. Africa, 1900, and A.D.O. to Lieut.-Gen. Sir Ian Hamilton, K.C.B.; pay-master-general, 1899; under-secr. of state for the colonies, July, 1903, to Dec., 1905.

MARRIOTT, FREDERICK CLAUDE, M.A. (Cantab.), L.C.P.—B. 1875; 4th asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 26th May, 1905; inspr. of imigrts., 1st Oct., 1913; ag. prot. of imigrts., May, 1917; man. of lab. exchanges, 1919; served on wages comtees, and profiteering comtees, 1920; ag. dir. educn., May, 1920, to Jan., 1921 and from Dec., 1921 to Apr., 1922; asst. dir. of educn. and senr. inspr., schls., 1922; dir. of educn., Sierra Leone, May, 1923; dir., educn., Trinidad, 23rd Mar., 1926.
MARRS, ROBERT, M.A. (Oxon.), C.I.E.—B. 1884; prin., Ceylon Univ. Coll., Sept., 1921.

MARDEN, ERNEST, D.Sc., M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed., Queen Elizabeth Gram. Sch., Blackburn, and Univ. of Manchester; lect., Univ. of London, 1911; fellow, Univ. of Manchester, 1912; prof., Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z., 1915; asst. dir. of educn., N.Z., 1922; sen. dept. of scientific and indu. research, 1926; served in Great War.

MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD, C.B., C.M.G., M.V.O.—B. 1872; ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; scholar, Trin. Coll., 1891; istcl. classical tripus, 1893; sen. chancellor's medal for classics 1896; 1st cl. with distinction classical tripus, Part II., and B.A., 1896; M.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. olk., C.O., Sept. 29th, 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, July, 1900; to Mr. Lyttelton, Oct., 1903; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1905; priv. sec. to Mr. Winston Churchill, U.S. of S. for the Colonies, Jan., 1907; accompanied Mr. Churchill on his visit to E. Africa and Uganda, 1907-8; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Pres. of B. of T., 1908; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Home Sec., 1910; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as first Lord of the Admiralty, 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, June to Nov., 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Asquith, first lord of treasy., 16th Nov., 1915; returned to C.O. as 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as min. of munitions, July, 1917; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill, as sec. of state for war, Jan., 1919, as S. of S. for the Colonies, Feb., 1921 and as chancellor of exchequer, 7th Nov., 1924; priv. sec. to the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 23rd Jan., 1924; priv. sec. to Mr. Churchill as Chancellor of the Exchequer, Nov., 1925.

MARSHALL, CLAUDE H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Med. offr., Uganda Prot., Apl., 1908; famine relief duty, May-Aug., 1908; sleeping sickness work at Kyetume and Sesse camps, Sept., 1908, to July, 1909; med. offr., Toro and Mbarara, Sept., 1909, to May, 1910; med. offr., Anglo-German-Belgian Boundary Comm., Jan., 1911, to Sept., 1912.

MARSHALL, EDWARD FOSTER, B.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1883; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1912; attached to Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1913; Badulla kach., Mar., 1913; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., July, 1914; ditto, cent. prov., Aug., 1915; pol. mag., Matale, May, 1917; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1918; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Galle, Sept., 1923; pol. mag., Matara, Dec., 1923; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Avissawella, Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Avissawella, Jan., 1928.

MARSHALL, FREDERIC CHARLES.—B. 1880; ed., Repton and Camborne Sch. of Mines; stlitm. offr., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1901; inspr., mines, Mar., 1902; mag., Dec., 1902; asst. warden, mines, Pahang, Jan., 1904; dep. supt., census, Oct., 1910; mag., Raub, Mar., 1912; collr. Raub July, 1912; seconded for service in S. Nigeria, Nov., 1913 to Mar., 1914; warden, mines, Kedah, June, 1918; ag. conserv., forests, Nov., 1918; ag. advr. to land offr., Apr., 1919; warden, mines, F.M.S., warden, mines, Johore, Feb., 1921; commr. under land enact., Oct., 1922; ag. 1st mag., Johore Bharu, in addn., May, 1925.

MARSHALL, CAPT. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A. (Oxon.), Diploma of Forestry.—B. 1893; exhib., Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1911; hons. mods. (maths.), 2nd cls. hons., 1912; 2nd lieut. Worc. Regt., Aug., 1914; Gallipoli, 1915 (wounded); Mesopotamia, 1916 (wounded); lieut., 9th Batt. Worc. Regt., France, 1917-18 (wounded and prisoner); capt., 1st Batt. Worc. Regt., B.A. (Oxon.), 1919; diploma of forestry (with distinc- tion—placed head of the list), 1920; M.A., 1920; forestry comm., 1920-22; instr., forestry schl., Forest of Dean; asst. commr., national savings comtese, 1923; dep. conservator, forests, Trinidad and Tobago, 1923; conservator, forests, 1923; mem., bd. of agr., 1923.

MARSLAND, HERBERT, B.Sc. (Ag.)—B. 1899; ed., Ashton-under-Lyne Secondary Sch.; coll. of Agr., Holmes Chapel, 1917; N.D.A., Harris Inst., Preston, 1921; N.D.D., W. of Scotland Coll. of Dairying, 1921; B.Sc. Univ. of Edinburgh, 1924; Imp. Coll. Topl. Agr., Trinidad, 1924; inspr. agr., Sudan govt., 1925; cotton investigator, Tanganyika Territory, 1928.
MARSTON, S.—Treasy, asst., Kenya, Jan., 1917; senr. asst. treas., June, 1923; dep. treas., Uganda, 1926.

MARTIN, GASTON PACOBS DE, B.A., Trin. Coll., Dub.—B. 1874; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1904; ag. inspr., English schls., 1913-14; postal censor, 1914-16; head mast., Yaumati Schl., 1920; inspr., English schls. and sec., bd. of educn., 1924.

MARTIN, HON. J. E.—B. 1859; ed. pub. schls. Waterloo, P.Q., and McGill Univ.; B.C.L., and gold med., 1883; called to the bar of Prov. of Que., 1884; K.C., 1903; batonner of bar, Que., 1913-14; puisne judge, ot. of King's Bench, prov. of Que., Sept., 1918; mem. of faculty of law of McGill Univ., and lecturer on company law; ag. ch. just., superior ct., Quebec, 1922.

MARTIN, JOHN MILLER, B.A.—B. 1904; ed., Edinburgh Acad. and Oxford; class. schol., Corpus Christi Coll., 1923-27; 1st cls. class. mods., 1924; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1927; B.A., 1927; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. prin., Doms. Office, 2nd Nov., 1927.
MARTIN, LEWIS VERNON.—B. 1902; entd., C.O., 10th Sept., 1917; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.O., 17th Mar., 1920.
MARTIN, S. B.—Ed. Wesleyan Mission Schl., G. Coast; 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890; clk. to dir. of telegraphs and storekeeper, Aug., 1891; clk. and acctnt., July, 1893.
MARTINDALE, LEONARD.—B. 1884; ed., Tonbridge and Uppingham; on milly. serv., 1916-19; temp. olk., C.O., Apr., 1920 to June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., C.O., 21st June, 1922.

MARTINS, G. N.—Ed. C.M.S. Grammar Schl., Lagos; messenger, audit, and P.O., 1889; customs, outdoor offr., 1890 to 1893 (during which time attached to revenue office); ag. olk., P.O., 1893; 4th cls. of customs, 1893; 3rd cls. clk. and statistician, Mar., 1894; transf'd. to the revenue dept., 1900; deputy for cashier and ch. clk. on various occasions; 2nd cls. clk., customs, 1901; chief examining offr. of cust., Lagos, 1st Mar., 1905; ch. clk., cust., Nigeria, May, 1916; supervisor, cust., Apr., 1926; certif. of hon., June, 1928.

MARWICK, ALLAN GRAHAM, O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; served in Natal civ. serv., 1895 to 1899; served Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1901; sub. native commnr., Mbabane, Swaziland, Jan., 1903 to Mar., 1907; asst. commnr., ditto, 22nd Mar., 1907; asst. commnr., Hlatikulu, Swaziland, 31st Aug., 1916; mem., Swaziland-Portuguese bdry. comm., 1920.

MASON, GERALD BOYELL.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1890; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; certif., London Schol. of Trop. Med., 1909; D.P.H., Cambridge, 1917; jun. asst. med. offr., St. Marylebone infirmary, 1896; clin. asst., aurial and ophthalmic depts., London hosp., 1897; house physician, Brompton hosp., 1898; ag. med. offr., Three Counties asylum, 1899; civ. surg. in charge, Station hosp., Woking, 1902; med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. commnr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla and dep. judge of sup. ct., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905 to Sept., 1906; med. offr., No. 6 dist., Nevis, 1906; med. offr., dist. “D,” and mag., dist. “I” Dominica, 1907; med. offr., St. Vincent, 1909-12; temp. capt., R.A.M.C.; med. offr., in charge of troops, Cambridge, 1916-19; founder and hon. sec., W. Indian Club, 1897-8; founder and hon. sec., West India Red Cross Soc'y., 1921-1924; dir., med. and health dept., Bermuda, Aug., 1924.

MASON, H., A.M.Inst.T.—Actas. clk., 1st grade, E.A.P., May, 1912; asst. ch. acctnt., Uganda Ely., Nov., 1915; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika Territory rly., 10th Sept., 1920; ag. ch. acctnt. on various occasions 1920-21 and 1923-27.

MASETT, JAMES DAVID ARTHUR.—B. 1885; ed. privately and Harrison Coll., Barbados; post office dept., Barbados, Mar., 1904 to Apr., 1906; H.M. cust., May 1906; sworn weigher and gauger, 1908; sub. contr. of cust., Springlands, 1910-16; seconded for serv. in occupied territory, German E. Africa, 25th Nov., 1916; confirmed as supervisor of cust., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Oct., 1919; ag. asst. compt'r., cust., Feb. to June, 1923; ag. compt'r., cust., June to Oct., 1923; ag. asst. contr. cust. in 1923, 1924, 1925 and 1926; senr. supervisor, Apr., 1926; ag. dep. contr. cust. in 1926 and 1928.

MASSEY, HON. CHAS. VINCENT, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Toronto, Univ., of Toronto (B.A.), Balliol Coll., Oxon (B.A., M.A.); in charge, musketry training, M.D. No. 2, 1915-18; temp. lieut.-col., 1916; associate sec., war comttee. of cabinet, Jan., 1918; min. without portfolio, 5th Sept., 1925; Canadian rep. at Washington, 1926.
MASSEY, T. H.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Jan., 1913; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Oct., 1923.

MASTEN, HON. CORNELIUS ARTHUR.—B. 1857, Quebec; educ. parish dist. schl. La Colle; La Colle Academy; Victoria Univ., Coburg, Ont. (graduated with hons., May, 1879); mathematical master, high schl., Orillia, Ont., 1879, 1890; entered law soc. of Upper Canada, Oct., 1880; called to the bar, 1883; K.C. (Ont.), and bencher of the law soc. of Upper Canada, 1908; commr. to enquire into the Insurance situation in Ont. with a view to improvements in the law; pres., Alumni Assoc. of Toronto Univ.; chmn., exec. comttee. of the speaker's patriotic league; author of "Masten Company Law in Canada"; justice of the sup. ct. of Ontario, Nov., 1915.

MASTERTON-SMITH, SIR JAMES EDWARD, K.C.B. (1919), C.B. (1915).—B. 1878; ed. Harrow (schol.) and Hertford Coll., Oxford (schol.); ent. home civ. serv. (cla. L), admly., 1901; priv. sec. to 2nd sea lord, 1904-8; priv. sec. to perm't. sec., 1908-10; priv. sec. to successive 1st lords of the admly., 1910-17; ast. sec., min. of munitions, Sept., 1917 to Jan., 1919; ast. sec. (addnl.), W.O. and Air Min'y., 1919-20; joint perm't. sec., min'y. of labour, 1920; perm't. under-sea. of state for the colonies, 30th Aug., 1921; sec. to the Order of St. Michael and St. George; mem. of the off. side of the National Whitley Coun.; ret., 1925; Offr. of the Belgian Order of the Crown.

MASTERTON-SMITH, PERCIVAL FRANK, M.C.—B. 1895; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (hons. mod. hist.); B.A., 1921; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; 60th Rifles, 1916-19; M.C., 1917; capt., res. of Offrs., 60th Rifles; admin. offr., Nigeria, 26th Jan., 1922.
MATEER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. Foyle Coll. and Taunton Schol.; ast. treas., Uganda, 10th July, 1920.

MATHERS, FREDERICK FRANCIS, K.C., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. schls. in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Dalhousie law schl. and Harvard law schl.; called to the bar, 1892; dep. prov. sec. and clk. of exec. coun., N.S., 1902; also registr. of joint stock companies, 1909; now deputy atty.-gen.

MATHESON, CAPT. C. L. T., M.C.—Commissioned in Royal Engineers, 1909; ast. dir. of surv., G. Coast, 1914; Togoland campaign, 1914; Cameroonos, 1914-15; France with Field Coy. R.E., 1916-17; W.O., 1918; dep. dir. topographical surveys, G. Coast, 1920.

MATHEWS, ALBAN FRANK, F.S.A.A. (Incorporated Accountant).—B. 1891; ast. audr., external audit dept., S. Stiltns. and F.M.S., 23rd Aug., 1919; ag. senr. ast. audr., 11th Mar., 1920 to 31st Dec., 1921, 7th Sept., 1922 to 10th June, 1923 and from 2nd July, 1923; astd. audit office, Singapore, 1st July to 6th Sept., 1922.

MATHEWS, H. F.—Ed. Oxford High Schol. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; open math. exhib., 1905; 2nd cls. hons., math. moda., 1907; 3rd cls. hons., physics finals, 1909; certif. in cultural anthropology, Oxford Univ., 1912; certif. teacher, bd. of educn., 1910; ast. res., N. Nigeria, Aug., 1910.

MATHIESON, HON. JOHN ALEXANDER, K.C.—B. 1863; ed. Harrington Gram. Schol., Prince of Wales' Coll., Taught Schol., Desable Gram. Schol., P.E.I., read law with McLean and Macdonald; called to the P.E.I. bar, 1893; K.C., 1906; el. to legislature for King's county, 1900; re-el., 1904, 1908 and 1912; leader of the opposition, 1903; premier and atty.-gen., 2nd Dec., 1911; ch. just., P.E.I., 13th June, 1917.

MATTHEWS, ERNEST LEWIS, K.C., C.M.G. (1914).—Called to the bar, Inner Temple; law adviser to Transvaal govt., June, 1902; law adviser to govt. of Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; puisne judge, Natal prov. divn., 1926.

MATTHEWS, Sir John Bromhead, Kt. Bach. (1911).—B. 1864; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1890, N.E. circuit; ag. solr.-gen., S. Settlimts., Oct., 1902, to May, 1903; M.L.C., S. Settlimts., 1901 and 1904; pres. of Moslem charities comsn., S. Settlimts., 1903; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1907; M.L.C., Bahamas, 1907-1909; K.C., Bahamas, 1909; atty.-gen., S. Settlimts., 1909; ch. just., Bahamas, Jan., 1910; resig., 1911.

MATTHEWS, Leonard Scott.—Ist K.A.R., 1916-18; ag. senr. paymr., K.A.R., Apr., 1920; clearance offr., Zomba, K.A.R. war acota., Apr., 1921 to Mar., 1922; dep. treas., Nyasaland, Dec., 1924; ag. treas., Apr., 1925.

MATTHEWS, Leslie Gard.—Ed. Plymouth; Oxford Univ. O.T.C., 1915; O.A.S., France, 1916 and 1917, Lanc. Fusiliers; Mesopotamia, 1917-11, attd., Indian Army and Iraq Levies; Arab rebellion, Iraq, 1920 (med. and clasp); ast. treas., Somaliland, June, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1923; ast. comsnr., pol., Nigeria, 1926.

MATTHEWS, Marmaduke Humphrey.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; probationer, col. audit dept., Feb., 1914; ast. audr., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; temp. lieut., Nigeria Regt., 1914-15; audr., Brit. Honduras, Sept., 1920.

MAVROGORDATO, Arthur Stephen, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1886; ed. at Oakham Schl., Rutland; elk., pol. headqrs., Cyprus, Nov., 1903; in apr. of mil. pol., Cyprus, May, 1906; in charge of pol. detachment, Kyrenia, 1907-1909; gov. of prison and dep. coroner; passed govt. exams. in modern Greek and Turkish; in command of Papho dist. several occasions; detailed for special duty in Larnaca dist. in connection with Limassol riots, May-June, 1912; ast. comsnr. of police, Sierra Leone, June, 1913; J.P. and dep. coroner, 1913; ag. comsnr. of police, also sheriff, prison visiting justice, and govt. nominee on city coun. of Freetown, and performing duties of marshal of the Admiralty prize ct., May to Nov., 1915, Dec., 1916, to May, 1917, Feb. to Apl., 1918, and from Aug. to Dec., 1918; ag. harbour. and detaining offr. under mil. authorities, May, 1916, to May, 1917, and May, 1918, to Mar., 1919; ag. dist. comsnr. in headqrs. dist., S. Leone, Feb.-Mar., 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., police and prisons, Palestine, Dec., 1922; ag. inspr.-gen., pol. and prisons, 1st Apr., 1924; commdt., ditto, 1st Apr., 1925.

MAXWELL, Arthur Walter, M.I.R.S.E.—B. 1885; ed. St. Thomas' Charterhouse, Lon.; ch. tel. mech., N. Nigeria, 1910; ast. signal and tel. engr., F.M.S. rly., 1913; ast. tel. engr., 1917; dist. tel. engr., 1919; signal and tel. engr., Feb., 1925.

MAXWELL, Sir Frederic Mackenzie, Kt. Bach. (1911).—Called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1884; 1st cl. studentship, Inns of Ct., 1882; 1st cl. scholarship (Equity), Linc.'s Inn, 1884; M.A. (Oxon), 1891; 1st cl. jurisprudence, 1885; ag. atty.-gen., Br. Hond., Feb. to Dec., 1890, and May to July, 1896; atty.-gen., Br. Hond., July, 1896; K.C., Br. Hond., Apr., 1905; ag. chief just., Br. Hond., 21st June to 29th Dec., 1899, Mar. to Oct., 1902, Apr. to Nov., 1904, and Apr., 1906, to Jan., 1907; chief just., Br. Hond., Jan., 1907; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1912; lieut. commdg. mounted section of Br. Hond. vol. force, Feb., 1905; attended schl. of mounted infantry at Bulford Camp, and reed. certif. of qualification to command a company of M.I., Sept., 1905; qualified at schl. of musk., Hythe, Dec., 1905; capt., Br. Hond. vol. force, Jan., 1906; major, commdg. ditto, Apr., 1906; ret. from vols., Sept., 1908, with permission to retain rank and wear uniform of the force; chancellor of the diocese of Br. Hond., and Cent. America, 1896-1907; revised the Federal Acts of the Lee-ward Is., from 1871 to 1888.

MAXWELL, Col. Geoffrey Archibald Prentice, D.S.O., M.V.O., M.C., Officer Ordre de Leopold, Officer Legion d'Honneur, Belgian Croix de Guerre, French Croix de Guerre, M.Inst.T.—B. 1885; R.M.A., Woolwich, 1902; R.E., 1904; Nigeria Barokano rly., 1908-11; survey, E.A.P., 1912-14; European War, 1914-18 (ment. seven times in despatches); inter-allied comsnr. of control, rly.s., occupied territory, 1919; communications sec., sup. economic coun., 1919-20; gen. man., Tanganyika rly., 1920.

MAXWELL, Gerald Verner.—B. 1877; ed. Bedford Gram. Schl. (exhibitioner) and Peterhouse, Camb. (scholar); cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1898; ag. regier.-genl. and regier. of titles, Dec., 1898; stip. mag., Dec., 1900; ast. native comsnr., Jan., 1904; native lands comsnr., Jan., 1906; ag. ast. col. sec. and ag. ast. rec.-gen., Oct., 1909; ag. rec.-gen., Dec., 1909, to Nov., 1910; chmn., native lands comsnr., 1912; ag. sec. for native affairs (joint), 21st Mar., 1917; temporarily M.L.C., May, 1917; ch. native comsnr., Kenya Colony, May, 1921; mem. exec. and legis. councils.

MAXWELL, Sir James Crawford, K.B.E. (1925), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1869; ed. Dundee High Schl. and Edin. Univ.; M.A., 1889; M.B.C.M., 1893; M.D., 1896; ast. col. surg., S. Leone, 1897-1900; W. African med. and clasp, 1898-99; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1900; 1st cls. comsnr., Southern Provinces, Nigeria, Feb., 1914; col. sec., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1920; ag. gov., Sierra Leone, 9th Sept., 1921 to 3rd May, 1922; col. sec., Gold Coast, 4th Oct., 1922; ag. gov., Apr. to Aug., 1923, Mar. to Sept., 1924 and Sept. to Nov., 1925; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 31st Aug., 1927.

MAXWELL, John, C.M.G. (1921).—B. 1875; ed. at Dumfries and Glasgow; passed final exam. in law, Apr., 1899; ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 21st Mar., 1902; travelling comsnr., 28th Jan., 1905; prov. comsnr., 18th Mar., 1907; dep. ch. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1921; pres., Accra town coun., 1st Dec., 1921; sec., native affrs., 21st Mar., 1923; ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 22nd July, 1923; has acted on several occasions as solr.-gen., atty.-gen., col. sec. and gov's dep. and as ag. gov. from 6th July to 2nd Sept., 1926 and from 6th June to 19th July, 1927; has also been chmn. of numerous comens. including those on Takoradi harbr., munic. corporations and native civ. serv.; compiler, 1st and 2nd edns., Gold Coast Handbook.

MAXWELL, Thomas Doveton.—B. 1873; ed. Tiverton, Bundell scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1892; B.A. (honours, jurisprudence), 1896; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1903; ast. dist. comsnr., So. Nigeria, 1902; ag. dist. comsnr., Degema, 1903; ditto, Calabar, Mar., 1904; polit. and transport offr., Ibibio patrol, Nov.-Dec., 1904 (W. African medal); ag. ast. sec., Calabar, Jan., 1906; ag. pol. mag., Calabar, Apr., 1906; comsnr. of lands, S. Nigeria, Aug., 1906; crown solr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; pol. mag., June, 1910; legal adviser, Jan., 1914; temp. lieut., Nigeria regt., Nov., 1914; intell. offr., Cross River column, Kamerun Exped. Force, Nov., 1914 to June, 1915; 2nd puisne judge, E.A.P., Apr., 1917; 1st puisne judge, Oct., 1917; ag. chief just., E.A.P., on various occasions in 1919, 1920, 1923 and 1924.

MAXWELL, SIR WM. GEORGE, K.B.E. (1924); C.M.G. (1915); Royal Humane Society's med. (1882).—B. 1871; ed. at Clifton Coll.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp.; jun. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Mar., 1891; ast. mag., Kinta, Apr., 1892; ag. collr. and mag., Kuantan, Pahang, Aug., 1898; ag. supt. of Ulu Pahang, Aug., 1896; regar. of cts., Kinta, Perak, Feb., 1899; ag. ast. sec. to gov't., Perak, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. of land rev., Larut, regar. of titles, Perak, North, and warden of mines, Perak, North, Mar., 1901; ag. sen. mag., Selangor and Negeri Sembilan, Jan., 1903; ag. sen. mag., Perak, Aug., 1903; ag. sec. to res., Perak, Apr., 1904; ag. commnr. of the ct. of requests, Singapore, Nov., 1904; dist. offr., Dinding, Aug., 1908; dep. pub. pros., Singapore, 1906; sol.-gen., S. Stlitms., Aug., 1906; ag. atty-gen., S. Stlitms., Mar., 1908; British adviser to gov't. of Kedah, July, 1909; ag. col. sec., S. Stlitms., 1914; ag. sec. to high commnr., Malay States and Brunei, 1916; ag. col. sec., S. Stlitms., Jan., 1916; ag.
