"C," St. Kitts, in 1905, 1906 and 1907; clk. and inspr. wks., P.W.D., Jan., 1909; rev. offr., Dieppe Bay, Apr., 1910; govt. offr. and harbmr., Sandy Point, July, 1914; 3rd rev. offr., Apr., 1915; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbmr., 1916; 2nd rev. offr., Jan., 1917; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbmr., 1919; dep. harbmr., Apr., 1920; ag. cashier, 1920; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbmr., 1922; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force.

SLADE, WALTER, A.M. Inst. T.—B. 1877; L. and Y. rly., 1892; minor appts., S. Africa, S. America and India; E. African mil'y., rly., 1917; gen. foreman, Tanganyika rly., Apr., 1919; asst. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., Mar., 1921.
SLADE-HAWKINS, W.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

SLADER, C. H. YORKE.—Ent. Jamaica Govt. serv., 1883; 1st cls. clk., sup. ct., 1894; ag. regir., 1895-7; lieut., Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with Jubilee contingent (medal), 1897; capt., 1899; passed exam. equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., Clarendon, 1900; ag. comsnr. and judge of grand court, Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Island, 1909; J.P. and stip. mag., Kingston, Oct., 1909; res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910; res. mag., Clarendon, 1912; ditto, St. Ann, 1914; major, regt., 1914; res. mag., St. Elizabeth, 1922; ditto, St. Catherine, 1926; compiled 3rd edit. "Index to Laws of Jamaica," 1911; ed. 6th edit., "Sub-Officer's Guide," 1924.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra off. asst. govt. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war, Diyatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; olk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional dist. col. sec. and olk., legis. couns., Apl., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint comsnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1912; and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; on special duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. govr., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 4th Apr., 1922; gov. and comdr-in-chief, Gold Coast, 1927.

SLEEP, ARTHUR, B.Sc.—B. 1894; ed. Ulverstone Grm. Schl. and Manchester Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; asst. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; attd. dist. office, Lower Perak, Dec., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Brunei, Mar., 1925; offr., cl. IV, Mar., 1926; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Aug., 1926.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9) ; capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM.—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Krian, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1916; ag. asst. adviser, Endau, Johore, Sept., 1918; ditto Muar, Johore, June, 1920; ag. asst. dir., eduon., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1922; ag. asst. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1923; town-planning admstr., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1926; ag. finan. comsnr. and audnr., Johore, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1928.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra.; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; ag. asst. M.O.H., 1916; ditto, in addition to other duties, 1916; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examiner in Physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr., govt. civil hosp., 1923; med. offr., lunatic asylum, 1923; ag. port med. offr., 1924; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, med. offr. in charge pub. mortuary, Kowloon and Kowloon pub. hosp., 1925; med. offr. in charge, Victoria hosp. for women and children, 1927; ag. dep. dir., med. and sany. services, 1928.
SMART, L. M.—B. 1889; asst. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rly., 26th Mar., 1927.
SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PERCY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; asst. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921.

SMARTT, RT. HON. SIR THOS. WM. P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1911).—L.R.C.S., Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A., Cape Colony, since 1894; col. sec., 1898; comsrr. of pub. wks., 1900-02, and 1904-1908; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904, 1906 and 1906; attended Impl. confce., 1907; mem. of S. African Nat. Conventn. in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transkei," having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900; M.L.A. for Fort Beaufort; min. for agr., 1921-24.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., 1st Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comsrr., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. c.l.k., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. insp't., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., 19th May-31st Oct., 1924.

SMIT, JACOBUS STEPHANUS.—B. 1878; D.T.D. (Dekoraties Trouwe Dienst) Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; pvt. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1908; asst. mag., Transvaal, 1909; asst. mag. Union civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1915; el. mem. for Klipkendorp, Union of S. Africa legis. assm., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high comsrr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925.

SMITH, ALLAN F., I.S.O. (1906).—Clk. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; asst. col. sec., 1896; col. postmr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900 to July, 1901; recvr.-gen., 1908.

SMITH, LIEUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1880; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub. lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-15; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salam, Mar., 1919; harbmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, 27th Feb., 1926.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. asst., post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pensions comsrr., 1920; sec., deptl. salaries comttee., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comsrrs., July, 1923; transf'd to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; acctnt., sup. ct., Oct., 1926.
SMITH, CHARLES BERNARD, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. lept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; attached, Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1926.

SMITH, CHRISTOPHER PATRICK, B.A.—B. 1894; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin and National Univ. of Ireland; Inns of Court O.T.C., Oct., 1915; cadet, R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst, 1916; 2nd lieut., R. Irish Regt., addl. Machine Gun Corps, 1917; demob., 1919; dep. supt., mero. marine branch, B. of T. 1920; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; addl., Chinese prot., Perak, May, 1921; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Feb., 1924; passed cadet, Feb., 1924; ag. prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, May, 1924, ditto, Taiping, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Sept., 1925; addl., cust. dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1926; ag. 3rd mag., S'pore, May, 1926; dist. offr., Xmas Is., Aug., 1927.

SMITH, HON. DAVID STANLEY, LL.M.—B. 1888; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl., Wellington Coll. and Victoria Univ. Coll. (N.Z.); on serv. with N.Z. expd. force, 1917-18; barrister, solic. and notary pub. till apptd. judge, N.Z., apr. ct., 1928; examin. in law, univ. of N.Z., 1920-27; author of "Adoption of Children in N.Z." in Journal of Comparative Legislation, Oct., 1921.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam, ast. clk., Dec., 1913; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glasg.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., Dec., 1912.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; univ. prizeman in constit. law and hist.; nons. certifs. in civ. law, pub. internat. law and med. jurisprudence; ast. dist. comsrr., S. Nigeria, 29th Aug., 1908; ast. pol. mag., 23rd Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., 29th Jan., 1914; crown proscr., 10th Sept., 1914; ag. legal adviser, and mem., leg. coun. for various periods, 1914-19; crown counsel, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921, and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, 9th Aug., 1922; ag. puisne judge, G. Coast, 1st Apr. to 4th July, 1924; puisne judge, G. Coast, 12th June, 1925.

SMITH, FREDERIC GORDON.—B. 1886; ed. Broomgrove Schl.; solr., Jan., 1909; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, July, 1921; legal dept., N. Rhodesia, 1913; on active serv. in East Africa with N. R. Police, July, 1915 to July, 1918; held various appts. in legal dept., N. Rhodesia during B.S.A.Co.'s admnstr. including regisr., high ct. etc., and ast. legal adviser and pub. pros.; ast. atty.-gen., Oct., 1924; solr.-gen., Kenya, Sept., 1926; atty.-gen., N. Rhodesia, Apr., 1927.

SMITH, F. M. ULING.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; dir. of educn., Northern Provs., 24th Aug., 1919.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C. M.G. (1911).—B. 1858; entd. war office, 10th Apr., 1878; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; ast. to ch. sec., Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, Aug., 1886 to Feb., 1887; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1886 to Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec., and mem. of exec. coun., May to Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; comsrr., Papho, Nov., 1891; regisr.-gen. and offl. mem. of legis. coun., June, 1895; has served also as mem. of comttee. of management, Cyprus museum, 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comttee., 1886; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comttee., 1887; mem. of land registn. comsrr., 1891; pres. mun. coman., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. comsrr., 1896; mem. of mun. comsrr., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf, under conven. with Turkey, Mar., to Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; Br. del. of Evcaf, July, 1905; ag. recr.-gen., and ch. collr. of

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE ECHLIN.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. R. Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1895; hon. capt. in army, 1900; asst. inspr., Hausa force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub-inspr., Br. Guiana pol., Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, 1902-04; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, 1904-06; asst. dist. supt., pol., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1906; supt., pol., Apr., 1914; comdt. pol. and gov. of prisons, Zanzibar, May, 1920-25; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron, gov., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; 1914-15 Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory medals; King's pol. med., 1924.

SMITH, HARRY LESTER.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., O.R.C., May, 1903; clk., col. sec's. office, O.R.C., Aug., 1903; clk., gov's. office, O.R.C., 1907; clk., gov-gen'l's. office, Union of S.A., May, 1910; prin. clk., July, 1924; ch. clk., Nov., 1926; ag. sec. to gov-gen'l., Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1927; ch. clk., off. of high commr. for S.A., Jan., 1928.

SMITH, HERBERT DENSHAM.—B. 1882; entd. Impl. civ. serv. and apptd. to acct. gen'l's. dept., Adm'y., 1901; asst. acct., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1910; acct., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, 1914; transfd. to treas., Sierra Leone, as asst. treas., Jan., 1916; ag. senr. assts. treas. for various periods in 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. ch. acct., rly., May to Aug., 1922; ag. treas. and currency offr., 1919 and 1923; recr. gen. and currency offr., Gambia, Jan., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

SMITH, HERBERT FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1873; ed. at Malvern coll. (class. scholar); entd. Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1893; asst. cashier, 1900; head of gen. stores dept., August, 1904; sec., Dec., 1920 to Nov., 1921; mem., fair wages advisory comtee., 1921; sec., E. African Currency Bd., 1922; head of stores dept., 1922; head of shipping dept., 1924; head, finance dept., 1927.
SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON BRICE, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. at Pocklington Schl. and Queen's Coll. Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria Jan., 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., Oct., 1917; ag. mag. in 1921 and 1922.
SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub-insp., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES ALFRED, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1871; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902; asst. sec. to col. regns. comtee., 1907-8; staff clk., 1st grade, 1915; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922.

SMITH, JAMES DAVID MAXWELL, M.A., Aberdeen.—B. 1896; cadet, F.M.S., July, 1920; asst. collr., land rev., Lower Perak, Oct., 1920; offr. in charge, treas., Telok Anson, Oct., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1921; ag. asst. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Nov., 1921; ag. asst. dist. offr., Sitianwan, Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Nov., 1923; asst. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1925; ag. sec. res., Perak, in addn., Nov., 1925; ag. state treas., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cl. IV, Sept., 1926; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak in addn., Oct., 1926; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, Mar., 1927.

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman, survey offr., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; commr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904; survr. of crown lands, 1904; ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, to 16th Jan., 1912; supervisor, govt. cotton ginnery, 1st Oct., 1912; ag. supt. pub. wks., St. Vincent, 4th Oct., 1913; col. engnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supt., pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. New Vety. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reapp't., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921; May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. coun.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.

SMITH, JOHN NORL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radcliff Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as asst. res., N. Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910; att'd, Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, 24th Mar., 1915; station mag. N. Provs., 22nd Mar., 1919; dist. offr., 17th Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., 7th Sept., 1923.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub.-insp. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. regisy. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEE.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to commr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regir. of shipping) commr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regir. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. asst. commr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedbergh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, 20th Oct., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. asst. postmstr-gen., 1914; seconded for serv. as British postmaster at Shanghai, 1914; ag. postmstr.-gen., 1915; seconded for military service 1916-19; ag. deputy regir. and appraiser, 1919; ag. supt. Victoria Gaol, 1919; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 5th cls., 1920; qualified in Pekinese, 31st Mar., 1920; supt., impts. and expts., 1921; att'd., Geneva opium confce., 1923; head of sany. dept., 1924; 2nd pol. mag., Nov., 1924; head, sany. dept., Feb., 1925; P.M.G., Oct., 1928.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munic.E. (Lond.)—B. 1874; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keites and Fosbrooks, archs. and survrs., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as asst.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contrs., Lond.; asst. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engnr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engnr., Burbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.
SMITH, PETER HAMBLIN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1900; ed. Denstone coll. and Lincoln coll.; Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 5th Sept., 1923.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON.—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramagate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.

SMITH, REGINALD MONTAGU BOWSWORTH, C.B.E. (1929).—Sub.-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1896; inspr., 1905; asst. comsnr., 1909; govt. sec. 1920; ag. dep. res. comsnr. for various periods in 1921, 1923 and 1924.

SMITH, ROBERT, LL.B., Barrister and Solicitor.—B. 1885; ed. Pub. Schls., Nansaimo, B.C.; Osgoode Hall and Toronto Univ. (LL.B., 1912); called to bar Ontario, 1912; called to bar Br. Columbia, 1913; M.L.A., Vancouver, Br. Columbia.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, F.R.Aus., H.S.—B. 1885; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N.S.Wales, 13th June, 1912 to 29th Feb., 1920; asst. under sec., educn., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educn., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, SYDNEY RICHARD, O.B.E. (Mil.), 1919.—B. 1875; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Apr., 1900; dist. engrnr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1901; ag. ex. engrnr., grade III, Sept., 1902; ag. ex. engrnr., grade II, May, 1904; ex. engrnr., grade III, Perak, Dec., 1909; ex. engrnr., grade II, Jan., 1913; adjt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1915; ment. in desps., July, 1918; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1919; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, May, 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., June, 1922; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, Jan., 1923; state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1923; ag. asst. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, Apr., 1925; ag. state engrnr., Perak, Dec., 1926; state engrnr., Selangor, Dec., 1926; ditto, Perak, Mar., 1927.

SMITH, WALTER BUCHANAN, M.C. (1918).—Ed. Repton Schl.; cadet, Br. N. Borneo civ. serv., 1903; dist. offr., 1904; 1st cls. mag., 1907; asst. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1909; asst. comsnr., lands, 1912; ag. comsnr., lands, 1912 and 1914; attd., Nigeria Regt. for service in Cameroons, 1914-15 and in German E. Africa, 1916-18; 1st cls. dist. offr., 1918; res., 1921; ag. sec., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1922 and 1925; senr. res., 1926.

SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884; asst. sup. wks., P.W.D., S. Stiltsms., Jan., 1908; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engrnr., grade III, July, 1908; ag. state engrnr., Kedah, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. state engrnr., Kedah, Apr., 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM HARDEN, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (T.C.D.), L.M. (Rot.), D.P.H. (R.C.P. and S.I.), D.T.M. (Bengal).—ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; surg. sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1918; med. offr., Zanzibar, 27th Nov., 1925.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb. Rustat schol., 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls., class. tripes, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., 1st Dec., 1903; asst. audr., Nyassaland, 1st Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., 17th Aug., 1910; Seychelles, 23rd Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., 1st Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 5th Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., 29th Jan., 1915; wounded, 8th Mar., 1917; relinquished coman. owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., 29th Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, 29th Dec., 1923; audr., Kenya, 1927.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chrmn., cent. bd. of health and coroner, 1903; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMITH-DORRIEN, GEN. SIR HORACE LOCKWOOD, G.C.B. (1913), G.C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1886); grand offr., legion of honour; A.D.C. gen. to the King, 1910-14.—B. 1858; served in Zulu war, 1879; Egyptian war, 1882; Soudan campaign, 1885; Soudan field force, 1885-6, 4th cls. Medjidie; 4th cls. Osmanlieh, Chitral exped., 1895; N.W. Frontier, India, 1897-98; Tirah exped. force; Nile exped., 1898; S. African war, 1900-01; European war, 1914-15; adjt.-gen. in India, 1901-3; commdg. Quetta div. of army in India, 1903-7; commdg.-in-chief, Aldershot, 1907-12; commdg.-in-chief southern commd., 1912-14; commdg. 2nd army corps and 2nd British exped. force in France, 1914-15; commdg. E. African exped. force, 1915; gov., Gibraltar, 9th July, 1918; assumed govt., 7th Sept., 1918, ret., 1923.
SMITHERS, ROBT. FRANCIS.—Clk., atty. gens.-office, Apr., 1902; senr. clk., Jan., 1910; pub.-serv. inspr., Sept., 1921.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.C. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebben scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripes, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comm. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comttee. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confce. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; M.L.A. for Sanderton, 1924.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH., (1905).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1896; atty.-gen., 1896; ch.just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911.

SNELGAR, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1890; ed. Christ's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; clk., td. of educn., 1906; clk., C.A., 1910; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1912; ag. office supt., 1917 and 1919; land asst., 1919; sec., land tenure coman., 1920; warden of mines, 1920; regsr. of titles, etc., 1922; asst. land offr., 1922.

SOLOMON, MICHAEL CLAUDE.—Copyist, med. dept., Jamaica, Oct., 1885; 3rd cls. clk., 1888; 2nd cls. clk., 1891; 1st cls. clk. and sec., cent. bd. of health, 1908; ch. clk., 1920; sec. to coman. on reorganization of med. serv., 1920.

SOLOMON, NEVILLE STAFFORD VICTOR.—B. 1874; clk., G.P.O., Bahamas, 1891; tel. dept., 1892; treas. and cust., 1895; supervisor, cust., 2nd gds., Gold Coast, 1902; ag. ch. regsr., sup. ct., 1906; finan. asst., treas. and cust. dept., Nigeria, 1907; supervisor, 1st gds., cust. dpt., 1910; collr., 1917; adm'y. reporting offr., various ports, Nigeria, during great war, 1914-18; ret. on pension, 1922; astt. postmr. (temp.), Bahamas, Jan., 1924; resigned, Nov., 1925; recr. gen. and treas., and mem. exec. coun., Bahamas, 1928; has held various ag. appts.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1907), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Town and Cambridge Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1876; raised to the bench, Kimberley, 1887; afterwards transferred to Eastern Districts Court in same capacity; puisne judge sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appellate div. of sup. ct. of South Africa, May, 1910; ch. just., 1st Mar., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Kalutara kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo Kach., Dec., 1922; office astt. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office astt., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th astt. col. sec., Nov., 1926; 4th astt. col. sec., May, 1928.

SOMMERVILLE, JAMES, O.B.E.—Cltk., traffic dept., Cape, 20th Mar., 1897; clk., C. T. M. office, Cape, 1st Oct., 1898; oh. clk., admtr.'s office, Transvaal, 1st Dec., 1900; sec., refugees aid dept., 1st Nov., 1901; sec., Transvaal immigrn. office, 1st Aug., 1903; oh. clk., lands dept., 1st May, 1904; under sec. for lands, Union of Africa, 1st June, 1917; sec. for lands, 1st Apr., 1921.

SOFER, JOHN ROBERT PHILPOTT, B.A. (hons.), Cantab.—B. 1904; ed. Tombridge Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (schol.); headmaster, agril. prim. schl., Zanzibar, Sept., 1926; seconded for duty as agrl. offr., Nov., 1926; agrl. offr., Jan., 1928.

SOUTHBOURGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. W. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; astt. lawclk., B. of T., 1886-1888; astt. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec., rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1895; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to chmn. of select comms. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic comsn., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions comsn., 1906; mem. of comms. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal comn. on electoral reform, 1909; reg'lar of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development comsn., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHERN, WILFRID THOMAS, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct. 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1906; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1909; astt. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to govt., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. astt. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and chmn., Colombo port comsn., Dec., 1923; mem., leg. coun., 1924 and exec. coun., 1926; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926; O.A.G., Oct., 1927 and May, 1928.
SPARROW, WILLIAM LA BART.—B. 1904; pol. probationer, Nov., 1924; seconded to Weihiwei as junr. dist. offr., Jan., 1928.

SPEARMAN, BARUCH, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Camb.), D.T.M. and H., certif., London Schl. of Trop. Medicine (with distinction).—B. 1877; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1912; capt., Uganda med. serv., Sept., 1916; med. offr. of hth., Zanzibar, Nov., 1920; recd. thanks of agt. gov't., Uganda, for services in connection with outbreak of plague at Jinja, Nov., 1914; ment. in desps., 1919; M.O.H., Zanzibar, Sept., 1920; senr. med. offr. of health, Zanzibar, Jan., 1922; ag. D.M.S.S. on various occasions, 1922-23, 1925-26 and 1928; D.D.S.S., Jan., 1926.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNET, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibt., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripods, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripods, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.

SPENCE, ARNOTT VERE.—B. 1893; 3rd clk., treasy., St. Vincent, Nov., 1910; ag. rev. and excise offr., June, 1915 to May, 1916; 1st cls. rev. offr. and port offr., May, 1916; ag. warden, Leeward dist., June, 1917 to June, 1919; 2nd clk., govt. office, July, 1919; ag. ch. clk., govt. office, clk. ex. and leg. couns. and med. regis., Apr., 1923 to Sept., 1927; oh. clk. etc., Sept., 1927.

SPENCE, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (Syd.).—Ent. pub. wks. dept., N'S. Wales, 1897; admitted to bar, 1909; ch. scot., P.W.D. 1918; senr. inspr., pub. serv. bd., 1919-22; in charge, govt. printing office re-organisation, 1922-23; under sec., treasy. and dir. of finance, 1923.

SPENCER, CYRIL EDWARD.—B. 1873; entd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1898; also clk. to legia. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comnr. on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspr., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comdt., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in comd. of diva. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov't. of pris. and astt. to dist. comsnr., and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comsnr., Apr. to Dec., 1906; passed in mod. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transfd. to B. East Africa, as asst. dist. commr., Nov., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. commr., Mombasa, from July, 1907; dist. commr., K.A.P., Apr., 1912; senr. asst. sec., secretariat, Jan., 1919; clk. of councils, Jan., 1919; ag. asst. chief sec., Jan., 1919; comsnr. of prisons, Kenya, Oct., 1923.

SPICER, ROY GODFREY BULLEN, M.C. (1918).—B. 1889; ed. Coley Court and St. Paul's; probationary asst. supl. pol., Ceylon, Nov., 1909; asst. supt., 1911; supt., 1915; on war serv., 1915-19; wounded, Oct., 1918; returned to Ceylon pol., 1919; supt., grade I, 1923; comsnr. pol., Kenya, June, 1925.

SPILLER, JOHN WYATT, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schil. (Som.); served in engnr. dept., G.W.R., 1896 to 1910; joined engnr. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Miny. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Stltmts., 1920; ch. engnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., F.M.S. rly., 1926.

SPIO-GARBRAH, JOEIAH.—B. 1878; 2nd asst. mat., tech. schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 1st Jan., 1899; 1st asst. mast., 19th Nov., 1902; 3rd grade teacher, 1st Jan., 1907; prin. teacher, 3rd Nov., 1912; asst. headmast., 1st Apr. 1920; headmast., 1st Apr., 1922; insp., scrls., 1st Apr., 1923.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GAERTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. asst. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; asst. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engnr., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engnr., grade II, 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engnr., grade I, Ipoh, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engnr., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; ag. engnr. constrn., F.M.S. rly., Sept., 1925 to Feb., 1927; senr. dist. engnr., Johore Bahru, Mar., 1927.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. asst. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros., May, 1905; ag sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908; ag. regisr., supreme ct., Penang, Dec., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1909; solr.-gen., Apr., 1911; ag. judel. comsnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1912; solr.-gen., May and Oct., 1912; ag. temp. judge, sup. ct., July-Sept., 1912, and Nov., 1912; puisne judge, Dec., 1913; pres. toddy coman., Selangor, Oct., 1916; ag. ch. judl. comsnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. ch. just., S. Stltmts., Oct., 1920; ag. senr. puisne judge, Feb., Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just., S.S., 16th June, 1922; ag. ch. judl. comsnr., F.M.S., June, 1924.

STACE, WALTER TERNER.—B.A., Trin. coll., Dublin; B. 1886; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 29th Oct., 1910; attached to the Galle Kadchheri, 1st Dec., 1910; office asst. to gov't. agt., Galle, March, 1912; pol. mag., Chilaw, May, 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1915; priv. sec. to gov. and extra asst. col. sec., Sept., 1915; asst. censor, Dec., 1916; censor, May, 1918; dist. judge, Negombo, Apr., 1920; asst. stltmtn. offr., Jan., 1922; ag. stltmtn. offr., Mar.-Nov., 1926; mayor of Colombo, Oct., 1927 to Dec., 1928.

STANFORD, COL. THE HON. SIR WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, K.B.E. (1919), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1892), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1850; mag.'s clk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gocaika war, 1877-8 (medal and clasp); mem. native laws and customs comsn., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1 (med. and clasp); on special service to Pondoland, 1884; oh. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; comd. forces in E. Griqualand, Boer War, 1899-1902 (deeps., 2 meda. and 2 clasps); ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs coman., Sept., 1903, to Jan. 1906; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembuland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; mem. of senate, Union of S. Africa; dir., war recruiting, Union of S. Africa and commissioner for returned soldiers, 1918-19.
STANLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR LYULPH, K.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1875; ed. Oxford Univ. (B.A.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; M.P. for Eddisbury div. of Cheshire, 1906-10; gov. of Victoria, 1914-20.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov.-gen., 1913; resident comsnr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commdr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924; gov. of Ceylon, July, 1927.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921. —Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (med. and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjt., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expedit., 1901, (med. and clasp); ag. trav. comsnr., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. comsnr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. eng., Chatham, 1905; resig. coman., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. comsnr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, on various occasions, 1918-20 and 1922-23; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.

STANTON, AMBROSE THOMAS, C.M.G. (1929), M.D. (Toronto), F.R.C.P., (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. (Cambridge).—B. 1875; senr. house surg., regisr. and demonstr., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., 1905-07; bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1908; on sp. duty in Siam, Indo-China and Netherlands E. Indies, 1915-16; dir., govt. labs., F.M.S., 1920; ch. med. adviser to S. of S., 1926; Craggs' research prize, L.S.T.M., 1911; Bisset Hawkins med., Royal Coll. of Physicians, London, 1926; author of "Etiology of Beriberi," "Malayan Culicidae," "Melloidiosis," etc.
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transfd. to C.O., Mar., 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; milly. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922.

STARK, WALTER JOHN KIRKPATRICK.—B. 1887; ed. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and at Aberdeen Univ. (M.A.), 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, Mar., 1911; ditto, Ulu Langat, May, 1911; ditto, Klang, May, 1912; passed cadet, Aug., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of imgrts., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contr., lab., Dec., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar., 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1913; asst. contr., lab., Seremban, Apr., 1914; ag. mag., Seremban, in addn., Feb. to Mar., 1915; asst. contr., lab., Klang, Sept., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. dep. contr., lab., Sept., 1917 and June, 1918; dep. contr., lab., Klang (offr., cls. IV.), 1st Jan., 1919; emigrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Coconada, Sept., 1920; ag. emigrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Madras, 9th to 24th Oct., 1920; supt. emigrn. depot, Negapatam, 31st Mar., 1922; mag., Ipoh, 28th July, 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Dec., 1926; ditto, Klang, Oct., 1927; offr., cls. II., Nov., 1927; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Nov., 1927; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1928.

STARNES, CORTLANDT.—B. 1864; served in N.W. rebellion, 1885, as ag. adjutant, 65th Montreal; Inspr., R.N.W.M.P., 1st Mar., 1886; adjutant, 1891-97; supt., 1909; asst. comsnr., 1st Dec., 1919; comsnr., 1923.

STATEN, OSCAR WILLIAM—Asst. res. mag., Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1902; ag. res. mag. and res. mag., Barberton, 1902-11; ag. res. mag., Heidelberg, 1911-16; inspgr. mag., 1916-21; ch. pub. serv. inspr., 1921; sec., pub. wks., Jan., 1925.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, KT. BACH.—(1926).—Mem., N.Z. parl. since 1915; speaker since 1923.

STRAD, KINGSLEY WILLANS, O.B.E. (1928).—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; registr. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, 1st Jan., 1919 to 31st Mar., 1920; refugee comsnr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; registr. of patents, Aug., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Française, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. comsnr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; registr. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; registr. of companies, July, 1922; registr. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; registr. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir., cust. excise, and trade, Palestine, July, 1924; ag. registr.-gen., Aug. to Dec., 1924; registr., trade marks and patents, 1st Apr., 1928; ag. registr., companies and co-op. societies, Apr.-Dec., 1928.

STEAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; asst. gen publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of imgrm. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.

STEDMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY, O.B.E. (1929), M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Weymouth Coll. and trained at Royal Naval Depot, Portland; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1903; ag. exec. engrnr., Kisumu, Mombasa, 1907; in charge, Thika rly. survey and constr., 1910-11; ag. exec. engrnr., Nairobi and Naivasha, 1912-16; seconded to 3rd batt., K.A.R., 1915 ("1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meda.); exec. engrnr., P.W.D., 1916; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1925; Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, cls. III., March, 1928.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on milly. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July., 1922.
STEEL, CECIL RICHARD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.) L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. London S.H.T.M. (distinct.) D.T.M. & H. (Eng.)—Milly. serv., 1917-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1926.

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 26th Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, 7th July, 1899; mag. dist. F., Dominica, 16th Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and regir.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920.

STEEL-MAITLAND, SIR A. H. D. R., BART. (creat. 1917).—B. 1876; ed. Rugby (scholar and exhibitioner); Balliol Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); Eldon scholar, 1899; 1st cls. class. mods., 1897; 1st cls. class. schl., 1899; 1st cls. final law schl., 1900; sec., junr. treas. and pres., Oxford Univ. Soc'y; special comsnr. to royal coman. on Poor Laws, 1906-7; M.P. (C.) East Birmingham, since 1910; chrmn. of Unionist party organisation, 1911; parly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 31st May, 1915; ditto, 11th Dec., 1916; additional under-sec., F.O., and additional parly.-sec., B. of T., in charge of the work of the dept. of overseas trade, 1917-1919; min. of lab., 7th Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E. (1919), M.C., King's Police Med.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; comsnr., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in despatches; ch. comsnr. pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1869; ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Royal Univ. of Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1894; mag., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, 4th Jan., 1912, and mag. of the st. of H.H. the Sultan of Zanzibar, 2nd Feb., 1912; ag. asst. judge, 30th Aug., 1915 to 20th Apr., 1916 and from 26th Feb. to 5th Aug., 1918; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Feb., 1925; editor-in-ch. of "The Manual of Penal Law and Court-martial procedure" (4th edtn.); asst editor of 11th edtn. of "Addison on Contracts"; author of works on "Demurrage," "Freight," "Charter parties," etc.; contributor to the Encyclopaedia of the Laws of England, Journal of Comparative Legislation, etc.

STEPHENSON, LIEUT.-COL. ARTHUR C.M.G., (1918), D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1881; served S.A. War (Queen's med., 3 clasps); S. Rhodesia civ. serv., 1902-04; N.R. civ. serv., 1904-12; served in Great War, 1915-19; (deeps.). lieut.-col., Reg. Army R. of O. (R. Scots), 1916; commdt., N.R. Volunteer Forces, Aug., 1919; ch. intell. offr., N.R., June, 1920; ag. commdt., N.R.P., Oct., 1924; commdt., April, 1925; J.P.

STEPHENSON, JOHN EVERARD.—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914, to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, 2nd cls. elk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923.

STERLING, HERBERT HARRY, LL.B.—B. 1886; ed. Christchurch (N.Z.) Boys High Schl., Otago Univ. and Victoria Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. rly. dept., 1901; mem., rly. bd., 1924; ret. 1926; gen. man., rlys., 1928.

STEVENS, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Schl., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. hon., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; ast. mast., Achinota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, WILLIAM JOHN COTTON.—B. 1879; ed. Grey Inst., Port Elizabeth, St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown, and Univ. of Cape of Good Hope; survr., grade I, F.M.S., June, 1909; ag. ast. supt., rev. surveys, F.M.S., Aug., 1917; ag. ast. supt., survs., Kedah, Aug., 1918; ast. supt., rev. survs., F.M.S., 1919; ag. supt., survs., Kedah, Aug., 1920 and Feb., 1923; supt., rev. survs., F.M.S. and S.S., July, 1925; supt., survs., Kedah, Mar., 1926.

STEVENS, WILLIAM OSWALD.—B. 1891; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna kach., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addn. May, 1915; att., Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., S., Prov., Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avissawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Sept., 1927; ag. ast. govt. agt., Kegalle, Sept., 1928.

STEVENSON, CECIL YOUNG, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., F.B.Met.Soc.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; ast. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Ex. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Mons Star" ment. in desps.; exec. engr., German E. Africa, July, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. from Oct., 1923.

STEVENSON, DUNCAN.—B. 1899; ed. Lincoln Schl., Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1923; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1918; lieut., R.E., 1919; France, 1918-19; ast. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Jan., 1924; dep. conservator, forests, Apr., 1926; ag. conservator, forests, Apr. to Oct., 1927; senr. ast. conservator, forests, N. Rhodesia, 1929.
STEVENSON, NEIL STUART.—B. 1902; ed. Lincoln Schl. and Edin. Univ., B.Sc. (Forestry), 1924; ast. conservator, forests, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1924.

STEWARD, THOMAS ALFRED FRANK.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African war with Army Ser. Corps.; ch. elk. in staff office for payment of mil. receipts, O.R.C., 1902-1903; regis. of war claims, Kronstad and Heilbron districts, O.R.C., 1903-1904; elk., Swaziland adminstr., July, 1905; principal elk., Apr., 1910; also regis. and master of the special ct. of Swaziland and sheriff of Swaziland, Apr., 1907; passed Cape Univ. civ. ser. lower law exam., 1906; ag. ast. comntr., Peak dist., 1916-18; ast. comntr., Uumbo dist., 1919.
STEWART, AUGUSTUS GEORGE, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922; dir., govt. cottage hosps., Natal, 1922.

STEWART, CHARLES A.—Elec. to Alberta legis., 1909-21; min. of munic. affrs., 1913; min. of pub. wks., 1913; premier of Alberta and min. of rly. and telephones, 1917; ag. min. of immigrn. and colonization, 1921-23; min. of int. and mines and supt.-gen. of Indian affrs. in Dominion govt., 1921 and 1926.

STEWART, DONALD JAMES.—B. 1876; ed. privtly. and at Whitgift Schol.; mines dept., Transvaal, 1901; senr. elk., 1903; ag. dist. regis., 1906; prin. elk., 1908; ag. ast. regis., 1910; ast. regis., 1921; regis. and Rand townships regis., 1923; served in S. African War, 1899-1901, and the Great War.

STEWART, DOUGLAS ROY.—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; elk., native dept., Fiji, Apr., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and ag. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and gov't.'s comntr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. ast. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-1916; ag. elk. to exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to admnstr., June, 1912; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examrns., Fijian language, Dec., 1913; ast. native comntr. and gov't.'s comntr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; ast. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; licut., Fiji defence force, 21st July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov., June, 1918; ag. dist. comntr., Rewa and prov. comntr., Rewa and Naitasari, 1918; ast. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; prin. ast. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; ag. agt., gen. immigr. and editor "Rajdut" in 1922 and 1923; ag. col. sec., etc., 2nd Sept., 1922 and 18th Jan., 1924 to Apr., 1926; gov't. dep., June and Sept., 1924, Jan., May and Aug., 1925; dep. comntr., W. Pacific H.C. and ag. ast. high comntr. and gov't. dep., Sept.-Oct., 1925; gov't. dep., Jan., 1926; col. sec., Barbados, Aug., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns. and exec. comte, also ch., bd. of dir., sugar industry agrl. bank; ag. gov., Apr.-July, 1927.

STEWART, CAPT. JOHN LIVINGSTONE, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory meda.; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Gold Coast, 27th Dec., 1923.
STEWART, NEIL, M.M.—B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.); ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919; ast., 1927.
STEWART, HON. PRICCY GERALD.—B. 1885; el. mem., H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Wimmera, Dec., 1919; min. of pub. works and rlys., C. of A., 1923-24.

STEWART, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MACGREGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portsmth.; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, ROBT. PETER.—Exhbtrn., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; ag. ast. mast., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1892-93 and 1893-94; elk., immigrn. dept., 1894; seconded to govt. secretariat, 1897-98, and 1900-02; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, July, 1903; immigrn. agt., Dec., 1906; ag. sen. immigrn. agt., 1908, 1909, and 1913; senr. immigrn. agt., Feb., 1914; mem. bd. of examiners in Hindi, etc., 1914; rep. of Br. Guiana at educ. confere., Trinidad, 1921; chmn., poor law comsrs., 1920-22; dir. widows and orphans pension fund; mem., bd. of educ., colonisation and development bd., lab. exchange bureau and various comtees.; ag. comsrs., educ., Sept., 1926 to June, 1927; ag. immigrn. agt.-gen. on various occasions, 1916-26; mem., exec. coms. in 1924, 1925 and 1926.

STEWART, ROBERT STROTHERN, M.A., B.C.L., B.Litt., Durham Univ., Gladstone prizeman.—Admitted solr., sup. ct., 1905; 2nd lieut. the Tynemouth R.G.A (T.F.), 1913; A.D.C. to gen. offr. commdg. Tyne garrison, 1914-15; intelligence offr., Tyne garrison, 1915-17; ment. for valuable services; asst. competent mily. authority, Tyne garrison, 1917-19; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1919; major, R.A., T.F., R. of O., 1919-27; M.P. for Stockton-on-Tees, 1923-24; mag., county of Victoria, Trinidad, Apr., 1927.

STEWART, HON. WILLIAM DOWNIE, LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Otago High Schl. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. park., 1914; served in France with N.Z. Rifle Brigade; min. of internal affrs., 1921 to 1923; min. of cust., 1921; min. of industries and commerce, 1923-26; atty.-gen., Jan.-May, 1926; min. of finance and min., stamp duties, 1926.
STEYK, J. R.—B. 1876; prin. C.N.O. schl., Leeston, O.F.S., 1904-5; vice-prin., 1906-20; prin., 1920-22; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1st Apr., 1922.

STIEBEL, HERBERT CECIL, O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1876; Boer War, 1899-1902 (King's and Queen's meda.); Transvaal civ. serv., as sub-native comsrs., 1902; prot. of natives, 1907-16; E. Africa campaign, 1916; polit. dept., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1916; dist. polit. offr., 1917; 1st grade administrative offr., 1st Apr., 1920.

STIGAND, ALMAR GORDON.—Ed. Rugby; 2nd clk. to res. comsrs., for Bech. Prot. at Mafeking, 1898; 1st clk. and regiur. to res. comsrs., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899 to May, 1900; clk. of ct. and clk. to asst. comsrs., Gaberones, 1902; J.P., Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsrs., Jan. to Feb., 1914; 4th Dec., 1905 to 28th Feb., 1906, and 11th May, 1906 to 29th Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag. for Southern dist., Bechuanaland prot., 15th Jan., 1907; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; res. mag., N'gamiland, 1910; hon. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1914; awarded "1914-15" star for services as mily. intell. offr., German S.W. Africa Campaign; lieut. R.W. Kent Regt., 1916; served B.E.F., France; attd., intell. I.E.F., Italy, 1917; ment. in desps.; temp. capt., Jan., 1919; mil. control offr., Italy, to Nov., 1919; capt., R.W. Kent Regt. R. of O., Mar., 1923; awarded Murchison grant by R.G.S., May, 1923 for surveys and map of N'gamiland; res. mag., Kweneng dist., Jan., 1924.
STIRLING, SIR JOHN LANCELOT, K.C.M.G. (1909); KT. BACH. (1902), O.B.E. (1918), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. legis. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres. legis. coun., since 1901.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, C.B.E. (1925), M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibr., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; M.A. 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on sp. serv. to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to W.I. agric. confere., 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem., agric. sch. comsrs., 1909; mem., banana comsrs., 1910; on sp. serv. to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec., tobacco comsrs., 1911; comsrs. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir., agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem., coun. of govt., 1913; regiur., co-operative credit societies, July, 1913; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., 1914; mem. of irrigation comtees., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; dir., agr., regiur., co-op. societies and vice-pres., agril. soc., Ceylon, 1916; chmn., exec. comtees., Ceylon rubber research scheme, 1916; del. to Imp. entomological confere., London, 1920; mem., coun., Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921; M.L.C., 1921; del. to Imp. botanical confere., London, 1924; mem., bd. of management, tea research inst., 1925; visited tea dists., N. India with Ceylon delegation, 1926; asst. agril. adviser to S. of S., Mar., 1929; editor "Tropical Agriculturist" and author of several rept., etc., on trop. agr.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUDSON.—B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schl., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); war service, E. Africa, 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comsrs., Sierra Leone, 29th Apr., 1914; priv. secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. comsrs., 21st Aug., 1922.

STONE, ARNOLD ALFRED PRICE DUNBAR, O.B.E. (1929), F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy. E., A.M.I. Mech. E.—B. 1877; eng. draughtsman, Sierra Leone, 1912; ag. asst. D.P.W., Nov., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. col. eng., Gambia, Jan., 1920; dist. eng., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., May, to Oct., 1922 and May to Oct., 1924; asst. D.P.W., 1923; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1925; mem., leg. coun., 1925; ag. gen. man., rly. in July., 1927.
STONE, ROBERT GEORGE.—Asst. paymaster, 1st King's African rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906; asst. dist. comsrs., E.A.P., Apr., 1911; dist. comsrs., Apr., 1914.

STONE, THOMAS.—B. 1876; sub-dist. commandant, S. African constab., 1901-8; King's S. African medal with five clasps; King's police medal, 1909; asst. comsrs. of police, N. Nigeria, 1st Oct., 1908; inspr., prisons, S. Prova., 18th Oct., 1922; ag. dir., prisons, 25th May, 1923.

STONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), Rt. Hon. Sir John Lawrence Baird, Bart., P.C. (1922), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (1915), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvt. sec. to Sir U. Garstin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1900-02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, desps., C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-15 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 1910-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-25; parly. secm., Air Bd., 1916-18; parly. under sec., R.A.F., 1918; parly. under-sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of transport, 1922-24; 1st comsrs. of wks., 1924; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 10th Aug., 1925.

STORM, WILLIAM THORBURN.—B. 1881; Imp. post office, 1896-97; asst., Cape of Good Hope P.O., June, 1901; survr.'s staff, N.distr., Dec., 1901; engnr. in ch.'s staff, Nov., 1902; postmtr., Feb., 1904; postmtr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909-10; prov. postmtr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1912; survr., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; divnl. survr., 1916; senr. survr., Jan., 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G.; various occasions between Jan., 1918 and July 1920; P.M.G., Tanganyika Territory, 8th Oct., 1920; mem., legis. coun., July, 1926.
STORRS-FOX, D., LL.D.—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

STORRS, SIR RONALD, K.C.M.G. (1929), KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. class, tripods, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. McMahon and Sir R. Wingate; asst. polit. offr. to Anglo-French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (deeps.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comte., Palestine exploration fund; mily. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917-20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc'y, Jerusalem schl. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local govt. commsn., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc'y.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; comdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece; apptd. gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 16th Sept., 1926; assumed duty, 30th Nov., 1926.

STOUT, THE RT. HON. SIR ROBERT, P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. solr., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1875; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for immigr., 1878; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educn., 1884-87; fellow and ex-chancellor of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1899-1926; mem. of coun. of Victoria Coll.; M.L.C., 1926.

STOUTE, Cyril Eustace.—B. 1882; clk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade clk., and ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk., leg. coun., 9th Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., 14th July to 24th Aug., 1926 and 20th Apr. to 14th July, 1927.

STOYLE, Herbert Blin.—B. 1888; ed. Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Schl., Crediton, Kattersea Polytechnic and L. and S.W.R.; Stan Creek rly., Br. Honduras, 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., 1919-20; ag. dist. commnr., Stan Creek, 1920; J.P.; asst. loco. sup't., Tanganyika rly., 1921; dist. loco. supt., 1923.

STRACHAN, John, M.I.C.E., M.I.St.E.—B. 1877; ed. at Bath; ch. engr., lake development scheme, Colombo, Ceylon, Aug., 1911; min. of transport, 1919; asst. D.P.W., Ceylon, 1920; asst. D.P.W., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., in 1921 and 1923; D.P.W., F.M.S., chmn., elec. bd. and mem. rly. bd., Apr., 1923; D.P.W., Ceylon and mem., leg. coun., chmn., elec. bd., pub. wks. advisory bd., local govt. bd. and prison bd., pres. engnr. assn., 1923-26; gen. man. and ch. engr., F.M.S. rlys., chmn., rly.; bd., Jan., 1927; offl. mem., fed. coun., F.M.S., Oct., 1927.
STRACHAN, Peter Donald, M.D.—B. 1872; med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921-23; supt., Basutoland leper asylum, 1923.

STRACHEY, Sir Charles, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1886; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comte., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comte., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Conf., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtes. for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924; ret., Sept., 1927.

STRADBROKE, (3rd Earl of) (cr. 1821). GEORGE EDWARD JOHN MOWBRAY ROUS, Baron Rous (cr. 1796), Viscount Dunwich (cr. 1821), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 272nd brigade, R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 1920-26.

STRAHAN, Kenneth Cyprian.—B. 1880; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury and King's Coll., Cambridge; Ceylon govt. rly., 1908-15; R.A.S.C.M.T., 1915; capt., G.H.Q., Inspection Branch, 1917; ment. in despa.; ch. mech. engr., Tanganyika rlys., 1920.
STRAKER, Major G. H., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Asst. dist. commnr., Kenya, Oct., 1921.

STRATFORD, James.—B. 1869; ed. St. Aidan's Coll., Grahamstown, Cape Colony and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L., Oxon; mem., Inner Temple; called to the bar, Eng/and, 1898; Transvaal, 1902; practised, Johannesburg; K.C., 1912; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. division, 1st March, 1921; judge of appeal, 1927.

STRATHAIRN, Donald Reith.—B. 1884; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; passed law agent's exam.; asst. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, 1912; dist. commnr., 1918; senr. asst. col. sec., 1921; clk. of couns., 1922; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., for various periods, 1922-25; ag. sec. for native affairs., 1924.

STRATHAIRN, George Cecil, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.). D.P.H., certif. from London S.T.M.—Med. offr., Orange River Colony Burgher Camps, 1901-03; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1903; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1914; war serv. with rank of major, 1914-18; ch. med. offr., Fiji, 1920; mem., leg. couns., Fiji, 1920-22; senr. sanry. med. offr., Jamaica, 1922.
STREET, Sir Philip Whistleb., K.C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1863; puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907; ch. just., 1925.

STRICKLAND, 1st Baron (cr. 1928), Sir Gerald Strickland, Count della Catena, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V.; and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb.; honours law trips; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn Temp., and pres. cholera comm., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for services in negotiations with the Holy See; unofficial mem., ex. coun., and ag. asst. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres., comte. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comte. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and commodr.-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrns.; gov., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed govt., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engrns., N.S. Wales; held dormant commn. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov.-gen.; retired, 1917; prime min. of Malta, 1928.
STRINGER, HON. SIR THOMAS WALTER, KT. BACH. (1928).—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-27.

STRONG, ARTHUR NEBBITT.—B. 1890; M.A., Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 13th Dec., 1913; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Jan., 1914; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, Dec., 1914; addtl. commn. of requests, Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, May, 1915; on military duty, 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1919; land surveyor, Colombo cust., Nov., 1921; dep. food contr. in addn., July, 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1924; asst. govt. agt., Matara, Apr., 1925; temp. attd., C.O., Nov., 1927 to Apr., 1928; dep. collr., cust., May, 1928.
STRONG, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1912; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915.

STRONG, THORPHILUS BENJAMIN, M.A., B.Sc. (N.Z. Univ.)—B. 1871; ed. Waimate Dist. High Schl., Canterbury Coll. and Univ. of Otago; teacher in primary, secondary and tech. schls., N.Z., 1887-1904; inspr., schls., 1904-15; senr. inspr., 1915-19; ch. inspr., primary schls., 1920-26; asst. dir., educ., 1926; dir., educ., 1927.

STRONGE, HERBERT CECIL.—B. 1875; ed. Falmouth Schl., and Trin. Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty. gen., 2nd July to 4th Aug., 1914, 3rd Sept. to 3rd Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1917; recd. thanks of Tongan govt. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of comm., in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925.

STUART, HON. CHARLES ALLAN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1864; ed. High schl., Strathroy, Ontario, and Toronto Univ.; called to bar, Ontario, 1896; called to bar, N.W.T., 1898; elect. to first prov. legis., Alberta, 1905; judge, supreme ct., N.W.T., 1906; judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 1907; chan., univ. of Alberta, 1908.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. John's Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1920; mily. serv., 1914-19, Salonica, 1916-19; deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, St. Mary's, Hamdy Riso, 1920; chaplain and lect., Ridley Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain and librarian, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STUART, WALTER LESLIE.—3rd associate to judges, sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, S. Australia, 1898; called to the bar, S. Australia, 1900; 2nd associate and clk. of arraigns, Nov., 1905; judges' assoc., chief clk. of sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, Feb., 1908; also a solr. to lands titles office, 1911; master of sup. ct., regis. of probates, regis. in admiralty, regis. of companies, trades unions and industrial and provident societies, and dist. regis. of high ct. of Australia, Jan., 1913, after acting in these offices from 1911.

STUARTSON, COLLARD METHVEN.—Clt., cust., Fiji, Nov., 1912; clk., res. comrnrs.'s dept., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Coly., Nov., 1913; dep. comrnrs., W. Pacific, Oct., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Dec., 1918; dist. offr., July, 1926.

STUBBS, CAPT. EDWARD CARLETON.—B. 1881; ent. R.N., 1895; lieut. (navigating offr.), 1903; commdr., 1916; squadron navigating offr., First Battle Squadron, 1920-23; asst., King's harbrmr., Portsmouth, 1923-25; in command, H.M.S. "Agamemnon," 1925-27; ret. with rank of capt., 9th Mar., 1927; master attendant, Colombo, 12th Apr., 1927.

STUBBS, SIR REGINALD EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cl. clk., May, 1907; 1st cl. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Stiltna. F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comte., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; offr. administering the govt., Jan. to Oct., 1913; dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed govt., 30th Sept., 1919; Order of the Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls., 1921; hon. L.I.D., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. fellow, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt.-gen. and gov. in ch., Jamaica, 1926; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. ii., 2nd edition, 1906.
STUCKEY, REGINALD ROBERT.—A.I.A.; pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under treas., 1923.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, KT. BACH. (1914).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl.; Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1872; for some years was sen. inspr. and probability offr., Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straits, 1884-88; supt. and dir. meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observ., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc., Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comte., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20.
STURDY DONALD, B.A.—B. 1905; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; agrl. course, 1923-26; B.A., 1926; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1926.
STURGESS, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1871; M.R.C.S.; gov't. vet.surgeon, Ceylon, May, 1895.

STURROCK, ALFRED JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902 (hons. class.); cadet, F.M.S., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, May, 1903; passed cadet, Oct., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Nov., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Gopeng, Mar., 1910; seconded for service in Kelantan, Apr., 1910; judge, high ct., and ag. supt. of cust., May, 1910; dist. offr., Batu Mengkabang, Dec., 1910; ditto, Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1913; supernumy. mag., Ipoh, Mar., 1915; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Oct., 1915; supernumy. offr., cls. II., Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, July, 1921; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, July, 1922 and Dec., 1924; ag. Fed. examr. in Malay, Apr., 1922; chmn., rubber restric., comtee., Perak South in addn., Nov., 1922; offg. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1924; fed. examr. in Malay, Nov., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1927; offr., cls. IB, chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1927; ag. sec. agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1927; ag. Br advi., Trengganu, Sept., 1928.

STURROCK, GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll. and Heriot Watt Coll., Edin.; asst. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1907; ex. engnr., grade III, Jan., 1913; ex. engnr., Kuala Pilah, Jan., 1914; ditto, Jelebu, in addn., 1915; ditto, Klang, Aug., 1920; sen. ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. state engnr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1923; state engnr., Kedah, Oct., 1926; dep. col. engnr., S'pore, June, 1927; ag. col. engnr., Straits Settlements, Dec., 1927.

STURROCK, JOHN CHRISTIAN RAMSAY, C.M.G. (1927), M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1875; ed. Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; tutor to H.H. the Kabaka of Buganda, Mar., 1905; dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., Aug., 1914; prov. comsnr., June, 1921; ag. ch. sec., June, 1924; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1926.

STUTCHBURY, SAMUEL.—B. 1882; supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1909, and served in different depts. of the P.O.; ag. senr. supt. of mails, Singapore, Feb., 1913; supt. of mails, P.O., Penang, Jan., 1915; senr. supt. of mails, July, 1918; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Jan., 1918; supr., grade I., Jan., 1919; ag. acnt., G.P.O., May, 1919; supr., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, but contd. to act as acnt., Singapore, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. asst. P.M.G., 13th May, 1920; acntnt., G.P.O., but contd. to act as asst. P.M.G., 12th Sept., 1920; ag. P.M.G., 1st Apr., 1921; ag. asst. P.M.G., 25th July, 1921; resumed duties as acntnt., Singapore, 1st Nov., 1921.

SAURES, ALFRED RADIMOND.—B. 1874; 3rd cls. clk., regnr. gen.'s dept., Jamaica, 1891; 2nd cls. clk., 1894; supervisor, census, 1911; asst. regnr. gen. and asst. dep. keeper, records, 1913; supervisor, census, 1921; regnr. gen. and dep. keeper of records, 1922.

SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1897; on mil'y. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Cayton civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1921; attd., Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; extra office asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1925; ag. archi. comsnr., Jan., 1926; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Feb., 1927.

SUFFERN, T. H., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (R.U.I.), Cert. of London, S.T.M.—Apptd. to W.A.M.S., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; served in Cameroon E.F., Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1915; ment. in desps., May, 1916; tempy. capt. (general list), Oct., 1914 to July, 1915; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1920.

SULLIVAN, CHARLES ERIC EUGENE, B.A. (Oxford).—Asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 7th Dec., 1909; temp. capt., intell. dept., E. African Forces, 1916; ag. 1st asst. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1918; ag. asst. chief sec., July, 1918; ag. registrar-gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Sep., 1918; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1919; senr. asst. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1925; ag. prov. comsnr., 1926; ag. deputy chief sec., Aug., 1926.
SULLIVAN-TAILYOUR, EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1886; supt., pol., Kenya, June, 1923.

SUMMERS, WALTER LLOYD.—B. 1870; entd. crown lands off., S. Aust., July, 1887; clk., agri. off., May, 1892; chief clk., July, 1902; ag. sec. to min. of agri., Feb., 1910; sec. to ditto, July, 1911; also sec. to min. of industry and sec. to min. of marine, 1915.
SUMNER, JAMES A. W.—B. 1903; ent. L.P.S. (Inland Rev.), May, 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. offr., London telephone serv., Apr., 1921; transf., C.O., Oct., 1923.
SUMNER, WILSON GEORGE HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Chigwell Sch.; Colonial Fellow of Surveyors' Inst., 1916; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

SURRIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lancs. regt., 1914-17; asst. sec. ch. secs. office, Cyprus, 16th Mar., 1918; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local comdt., police, Apr., 1919; ag. comsnr., Larneac, June, 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. asst. sec., Oct., 1920; passed exam. in Turkish, Aug., 1922; ag. comsnr., Aug., 1922; ag. asst. sec., Sept., 1922; ag. comsnr., Dec., 1922 to Oct., 1923; comsnr., Jan., 1924.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoc., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-05; lectr., law, McGill Univ. since 1905; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1st Jan., 1920; edr. of practice rept., Queb., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages); pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francaise, Montreal.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc. (Durham).—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, ALEXANDER CHARLES, M.A., M.C.E. (Melbourne Univ.), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1869; asst. mine surv., gov't. mining engr.'s office, Transvaal, 1902; mine surv., Transvaal, 1903; mine surveyor, Union of South Africa, 1910.

SUTHERLAND, A. R., M.A., Aberdeen University.—Science mast., Glen Urquhart H. G. schl., 1903-5; asst. mast., Campbelltown Gram. schl., 1905-6; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-10; ag. asst. master, pol. schl., 1909 and 1912; headmaster, Victoria British schl., 1913; lieut., R.G.A., on active serv., 1914-19; asst. mast., cls. 1, 1st Jan., 1920; headmaster, Indian Schl., 1920.
SUTHERLAND, CHARLES MCHARDY HUNTER, B.Sc., B.Sc. (agri.).—Ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schl.; military serv., 1914-19; B.Sc., 1923; L.Sc. (agri.), 1928; dist. agrl. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1925.
SUTHERLAND-GREAME, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. Rlys., 1915-24; loco. supt., F.M.S. Rlys., 1924.

SWABEY, COMMODORE GEORGE THOMAS CARLISLE PARKER, D.S.O. (1916), R.N., H.M.S. "Dunedin."—B. 1881; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia," Dartmouth; flag comdr. to Adml. Sir Lewis Bayly, 1913-14; served ashore with army in Aegean, 1915-16; comdr., H.M.S. "Lord Nelson," 1916-18; operns. divn., admv. naval staff, 1918-19; in comd. "H.M.S. 'Revenge' 1919-21; dep. dir., naval ordnance, admv., 1921-23; capt., R.N. coll., Greenwich, 1924-26; commodore congdg. N.Z. divn. and naval adviser to N.Z. govt., 1926; chevalier, Legion of Honour, 1916.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.B. (mil.) (1904); grand offr. of order of the Crown of Italy (1905).—B. 1863; ed. at naval coll., Gosport, and abroad; R.M.C. Sandhurst, 1882; entd. Welsh regt., 1883; Bengal staff corps, 1884; adjut., 1888; capt., 1894; local lieut.-col., 1900; brevet lieut.-col., 1901; brevet-col., 1903; temp. brig-gen., 1903-1906; retired from army, 1906; served with Burma expedn., 1885-86 (medal with clasp); intell. offr., Hukong Valley reconnaissance; in command of advanced base exploration, Turong River and Sing Pho country, Nov., 1895, to Feb., 1896 (McGregor medal); exploration to Somaliland, Feb. to July, 1891 (thanked by resolution of Bombay govt., and appreciation by govt. of India); Gildessa affair on Abyssinian frontier, 1892 (commendation by resolution of Bombay govt.); engaged on delimitation of internat. frontier between French and British Somaliland, 1892; served for five years on intell. staff, India, as staff-capt. and D.A.Q.M.G.; apptd. 2nd-in-command, Uganda Rifles, on active serv. in Jubaland, E.
