Africa (medal with clasp); on active serv. in Uganda, 1899; selected to command Somaliland police force, 1900; raised and organised levies, and commanded expeditionary force in Somaliland, Nov., 1900; defeated and routed Mullah (ment. in desps., medal with clasp, brevet lt.-col., thanked by govt.); organised 6th Batt. Uganda Rifles, second expdn., 1901; again defeated Mullah (C.B., ment. in desps.); apptd. comsnr., consul-gen. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., and judge of its high court, Apr., 1902; on special duty in Egypt, 1904; arranged evacuation of Somaliland, and organised control of tribes; British deleg. to conference with Italian deleg. at Lyons in connection with agreement with Italy respecting Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, British Honduras, 1906-13; spec. duty to Canada, with reference to Indian labour (thanked by govt. of India) 1908; spec. duty to Mexico, 1909.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E.B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon.; exhibitioner, Worcesters Coll., Oxon., June, 1876; 2nd classical mods., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb. and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admstd. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admstd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1895, again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstd. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., 20th Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct. 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, 15th Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., 30th Jan., 1906; recd. coronation medal, 1911; gov., Fiji, and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 11th March, 1912; assumed govt., 25th July, 1912; ret., 1918; rep. of Fiji at Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924.

SWETTENHAM, SIR FRANK ATHLETAN, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.H. (1917), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Stltmtns, July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and comsnr., ct. of requests, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted to act temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. comsnr., Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; comsnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. comsnr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malay States, Jan., 1896; admstd. govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royal comsn. on Mauritius, 1909.

SWETTENHAM, SIR J. ALEXANDER, K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll., Camb.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service, 1868; ag. pol. mag., Harrispattu, Feb., 1870; pol. mag., Kayts, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrispattu; ag. asst. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; ag. landing and tide survr., Galle, June, 1872; asst. collr. of customs, Jaffna, Oct., 1872, to continue to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1873; 2nd ast. col. sec., and clk. of the legis. coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; audr.-gen., Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S.S., 1893; admstd. govt., June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1898, and from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov., Br. Guiana, 1901; gov., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904; resig., 1907.

SWETTENHAM, LIEUT.-COL. JAMES PARRY, M.S.V.R.—B. 1875; ed. Shrewsbury and Bedford Grammar Schls.; Boer War, 49th Imperial Yeomanry (Montgomeryshire) 1899-1900 (Queen's med. with 3 bars); asst. engrn., Gold Coast (Kumasi) Rly., 1901; asst. engrn., P.W.D., Selangor, F.M.S., 1902; exec. engrn., grade II, Pahang, F.M.S., 1907; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 1911; capt., M.S.V.R., 1912; hon. A.D.C. to gov., S.S. and high comsnr., F.M.S., 1912-19; exec. engrn., grade I, F.M.S., 1914; seconded for mil'y. serv. in the Great War; asst. to chf. engrn., 2nd Army Cent. Force; temp. capt., R.E., 1915; O.C., 201st Field Co., R.E.; temp. major, R.E.; invalided from R.E., 1916; "1914-15 Star," War and Victory meda.; commdt., M.S.V.R. 1919; state engrn., Negri Sembilan, 1919; asst. dir. of pub. wks., F.M.S., 1923; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., 1925; ag. gen. man., rly., F.M.S., Feb., 1926; on sp. duty, pub. wks. dep., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; M.I.C.E., 1927; Col., Aux. Forces Officers' Decorn. and Long Serv. Med., 1927; asst. D.P.W., F.M.S., Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W., Dec., 1927.

SWETTENHAM, ROGER FRANCIS ROSS.—B. 1883; ed. privately, Bedford and Aston Rowant; attlm. offr., Negri Sembilan, June, 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Coast, Negri Sembilan, July, 1908; ag. chmn. and sec., san. bd., Seremban, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1909; ag. sec., san. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Apr., 1910; aspt. chandu monopoly, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1910; supervisor of cust., Kuala Lumpur in addn., Apr., 1911; seconded for mily. serv. (R.G.A. Cadet Schl.) Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1918; supt., chandu monopoly, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; sec., standing advisory comttee. on use of opium in Malaya, June, 1924; dep. chmn. and sec., ditto, Nov., 1924; dep. commr., trade and cust. (chandu), F.M.S., May, 1925; ag. commr., trade and cust., F.M.S., in 1926; ag. dep. commr., trade and cust. (excise) in addn., Oct.-Dec., 1927.

SWIFT, JOSEPH ARTHUR, B.Sc., B.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Tasmania; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1904; ag. ex. engrnr., grade II, asst. to D.P.W., F.M.S., June, 1911; ex. engrnr., grade III, Selangor, Apr., 1913; ex. engrnr., grade II, F.M.S., June, 1914; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, Apr., 1923; ag. state engrnr., Pahang, Apr., 1924; ag. state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926; state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, Dec., 1926.

SWINBOURNE, MAJOR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1884; clk., audit dept., N.S.W. govt. rly., May, 1900; lieut., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; passed with honours in topography and range-finding, Sydney, 1908; clk., pay and qrtmr., with rank of sub-inspr., Fiji constab., 6th Oct., 1909; capt., Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910; cmdg. ffr., Fiji cadet corps, 13th Feb., 1911; mem. of. bd. of survey, Suva, 13th Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., 16th Apr., 1911; qualified rifle course, Hythe, May, 1914; qualified (distinguished) machine gun course, Hythe, June, 1914; attached 1st Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Mar.-June, 1914; accompanied Fijl 1st contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., 1st Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, 13th Sept., 1915; comdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, 30th Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., 6th Oct., 1916; mentioned by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active service, July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lancs. Regt., 30th Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L. Regt., 25th Jan., 1918.

SWINDELL, REV. FRANK GUTHRIE, M.A. (Oxon.).—Chap., Selangor, May, 1902; col. chap., Malacca, June, 1906; ag. col. chap., Singapore, Apr., 1907, to Jan., 1908; ag. col. chap., Penang, 20th Dec., 1912; col. chap., Singapore, 1st Nov., 1914; regsr., diocese of Singapore, 13th Feb., 1916; archdeacon of Singapore, 2nd Feb., 1916; commissary, diocese of Singapore, 29th Sept., 1918; resigned regsr. of diocese, 3rd Feb., 1920; dep. regsr., July, 1921.

SYDENHAM OF COMBE, LORD (1st BARON, 1913, U.K.).—Sir GEORGE SYDENHAM CLARKE, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1905), G.C.S.I. (1911), G.C.I.E. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expdn., 1886, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defence comttee., 1885-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; apptd. as one of a comttee. to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comttee., 1904; gov. of Bombay, 1907-1913.

SYDNEY, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. JOHN CHARLES WRIGHT, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of Kilworth-Beauchamp, 1886-1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., 1889-1893; vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1895; St. George's, Leeds, 1895-1904; can. res. of Manchester Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. George's, Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain to Bp. of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Manchester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop of Sydney, 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Australia, 1910; author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Life and Work."

SYERS, CECIL GEORGE LEWIS.—B. 1903; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Oxford; classical schol., Balliol Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls. classical mods., 1923; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1925; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 21st Oct., 1925.

SYKES, GEORGE ROGNYALD.—Cadet, S. Sttltns., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1914; attached censor's office, Singapore; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1916; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Apr., 1917; asst. registrar imports and exports (war trade dept.) Singapore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept., ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1920; supernm. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies and asst. prot. of Chinese, Malacca, June, 1921.

SYMES, LIEUT.-COL. SIR GEORGE STEWART, K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. Army, 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., 2 clasps); Aden, 1903-04 (desps., D.S.O.); attld., Egyptian Army, 1905 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue Nile expdn., 1908 (med.); promoted major, 1915 and brevet lt.-col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sirdar and gov. gen. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intelligence, 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gov. gen.'s coun. (Sudan), 1913-16; European War (3 times ment. in desps., brevet lt.-col.); attld., staff of high commr., Egypt, 1917-19; attld., miny. of int., Egypt, 1919-20; dist. gov. Palestine, Sept., 1920; ch. sec., 1925; admtd. gov. Palestine, July to Sept., 1925 and May to Sept., 1927; accredited rep. to mandates commn., June, 1926; res. and commdr.-in-ch., Aden, 1928.

SYMONT, SIR JOSIAH HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1846; mem. of H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1881-1887; atty.-gen., 1881; Q.C. 1881; representative to Aus. fed. conven., 1897-8; and chrmn. of its judiciary comttee.; pres. of fed. league of S. Aust.; pres. of Commonwealth league; senr. for S. Aust., Commonwealth parl., 1901-1913; atty.-gen., C. of A., 1904-5; has ret. from politics.
SYMONTETTE, W. J.—B. 1878; pub. schl. teacher, 18th Apr., 1898; 4th divn. commr., 11th June, 1921; ag. ch. clk., revr. gen. in

SYMONS, CLAUDE TREVINE.—B. 1879; B.A., Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, gov't training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. gov't analyst, June, 1912; ag. gov't analyst, in addn., Dec., 1916; gov't analyst, June, 1919; ag. reg., med. coll. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

TACHE, JOSEPH DE LABROQUIERE.—B. 1858; ed. St. Hyacinthe Sem. and Laval Univ.; notary public, 1881; priv. sec. to Hon. J. A. Chapleau, premier of Quebec, 1880-82; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. Angers, 1887-92; proprietor and editor of "Le Courier," St. Hyacinthe, 1902-14; mem. of prov. coun. of agric., 1890-91; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., bye-elec. and g.e., 1904; apptd. King's printer of Canada, Mar., 1914; jtn. libr. of parl't., June, 1920.

TAGART, EDWARD SAMUEL BOURN, C.B.E. (1927).—B. 1877; ed. Bath Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, (B.A.); barrister-at-law; asst. collr., N.E. Rhodesia, 11th Jan., 1901; asst. nat. commr., 1st Apr., 1901; nat. commr., 1st Sept., 1902; ag. mag., May, 1903; asst. mag., 15th May, 1905; has acted as mag. on various occasions between Aug., 1905 and Mar., 1913; asst. legal adviser, 1st Apr., 1913 to 16th June, 1915; mag., 18th June, 1915; ag. judge, high ct., 31st Mar. to 23rd June, 1922; sec. for native affrs., 10th Apr., 1924; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. sec., 1st June to 22nd Nov., 1925; J.P.

TAINTSH, LIEUT.-COL. JOSEPH RAMSAY, C.B.E. (1924).—V.D.—Offr., loco. dept., Indian rly.s., 1895-1917; 2nd lieut., Volunteer Force, India, 1898; major, 1915; major, Indian Defence Force, 1916; capt. (temp. major), I.A.R.O., 2nd Apr., 1917; ch. mech. engr., Basra Light Rly., 3rd June, 1917; ag. asst. dir., works, Iraq, 4th July, 1917 to 13th Aug., 1917; asst. dir. of works, in charge of Basra Light Rly., 27th Sept., 1917; trans. to Rly. branch, R.E., 4th Oct., 1917; lieut.-col., 15th June, 1918; loco. supt., Basra divn. of rly.s., Baghdad, June, 1919; demob. with rank of lieut.-col., 3rd Sept., 1919; loco. supt., combined system, Nov., 1919; ag. dir., rly.s., 11th Dec., 1921.

TAYLOR, EDWARD ARCHIBALD THORNTON, B.A.—B. 1886; ed. Dover and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A., 1907 (2nd cls. class. tripus); asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. commr., Oct., 1918; senr. asst. col. sec., 1921; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. for various periods, 1922-26; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1925; sec., bd. of examns., native languages, Apr., 1926.

TAYLOR, EDWIN.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; clk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treasy. asst., Uganda, Jan., 1909; asst. treasy., civil admin., German East Africa (occupied territory), Jan., 1917; treasy., Seychelles, May, 1919; dep. treasy., Uganda, Apr., 1923; ag. treasy., May 1923 to 1924; senr. asst. treasy., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1925; ag. treasy., Apr.-Aug., 1925.

TAYLOR, EPHRAIM GODMAN.—B. 1877; ed. Wesleyan (Boys) High Schl., Freetown; civ. serv. exam., 1895; tempy. outdoor offr., oust., Nov., 1894; 2nd cls. offr., Mar., 1896; compet. exam. in acctng., May, 1902; 2nd clk., acctng. branch, treasy., May, 1902; 1st clk., Dec., 1903; 2nd grade clk., Jan., 1913; 1st grade, Oct., 1913; ch. clk., Jan., 1915; ag. senr. asst. col. treasy., May to Sept., 1919; staff supt., treasy., Jan., 1921; African asst. treasy., Jan., 1923.

TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918. France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 1921; attd., dist. office, Raub, June, 1921; attd., legal adviser's office, Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1921; addnl. mag., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1922; attd., land office, Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1922; supervisor, census, Perak, Apr., 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Dec., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr. cls. V., May, 1924; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1924; asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, July, 1924; ditto, Rembau, Dec., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Tampin, in addn., Oct., 1926; offr. cls. IV., May, 1928.
TAYLOR, FRANCIS BRYANT OLUMIDE.—Ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. of Preceptors, 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Lagos, Feb., 1907.

TAYLOR, FRANK WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Newbury Grammar School and Oxford, Non-Coll.; 3rd cls. hon., med. hist., 1910; bd. of educ. certif. exam.; certif. in geog., 1911; asst. mast., Magdalen Coll. School, Brackley, 1911-14; jun. supt. of educ., N.P. Nigeria, June, 1914; seconded polit. dept., June to Nov., 1919; regraded sup't, educ., 1920; late capt., 4th Batt. Oxf. and Bucks. Lt. Infy. T.F.; served in the Northern Cameroons, Garua and Banjo, 1915-16, and attached to the 1st Training Centre, W.A.S.B., 1917-18; publications, "A Fulani Grammar" (1921), "Two Fulani Readers" (1922), "A Practical Hausa Grammar" (1923), "A Fulani-Hausa Phrase Book" (1926), "Empire Reading Charts" (1926), "A Fulani-Hausa Vocabulary" (1927), "Fulani Hausa Readings in the Native Scripts" (1927), "Genesis in Fulani" (1927), contributions to the African Soc'y., the Philological Soc'y. and "West Africa."

TAYLOR, HON. GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. London and Toronto; called to the bar, Ont., 1902; practised in London and Moosejaw, Sask.; K.C., Jan., 1913; pres., Sask. Bar Assoc., 1917; just., ct. of King's Bench, Sask., Mar., 1918.

TAYLOR, GEORGE WILSON.—B. 1896; ed. in Toronto; apptd. to excise branch, inland revenue dept., 1888; engaged in spec. organization work, Quebec, 1898-1909; asst. sec., inland rev. dept., 1909; sec., ditto, 1913; asst. deputy min. and sec., 1914; asst. comsnr. of customs and asst. deputy minister of inland rev., Oct., 1919; comsnr., excise, Apr., 1927.
TAYLOR, GODFREY JOHN CAMERON.—B. 1902; ed. St. Andrew's Schl., Eastbourne, Rugby Schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A. (honors), 1923; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1926.
TAYLOR, GREVILLE POWER.—B. 1899; ed. Elstow Schl., Bedford, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 4th March, 1925.

TAYLOR, J. A., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—Med. offr., Uganda, Oct., 1908; temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1917; asst. med. supt., cent. asylum, F.M.S., 1920; S.M.O., Uganda, Oct., 1920; asst. D.P.M.O., Uganda, Mar., 1921 to July, 1921 and July, 1922 to Mar., 1923; D.M.S.S., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923.

TAYLOR, JOHN, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1863; entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Sudan war, 1884-5 (medal and Khedive's bronze star); impl. prison serv., Lond., Dec., 1887, to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize pris., B. Honld., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898; offl. recr. in bank., and offl. adminstr., 23rd Oct., 1908 to 22nd May, 1909; ag. dist. comsnr. on numerous occasions, 1906 to 1914; dist. comsnr., Toledo dist., 18th May, 1914; J.P. for the colony, 1908.

TAYLOR, ROBERT STANLEY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.A., M.D., B.C., D.P.H. (Cantab.), Cert., S.T.M.—B. 1876; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; exhibr., Charing Cross Hosp., London; D.M.O., Cue, West Australia; M.O.H., Cue and M.O.H., Day Dawn, West Australia; M.O., Uganda, 5th Sept., 1914; active serv., E.A. campaign and temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1916; S.M.O., Somaliland, 24th May, 1922; P.M.O., Somaliland, July, 1925.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, C.B.E. (1928), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schl. and King's Coll., London; clk. in crown agt's. office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymstr., Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); asst. treas., Uganda, 1910; asst. treas., Somaliland, 6th Mar., 1914; treas., Somaliland, 11th July, 1915; dep. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 13th Feb., 1920; ag. treas., Dec., 1921 to May, 1922; treas., Tanganyika Territory, 18th May, 1922; ag. ch. sec., 12th Apr. to 16th June, 1927; gov.'s dep. 10th to 27th May, 1927.
TAYLOR, S. F., Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

TAYLOR, Sir W. T., K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; oh. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. recvr.-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1886; ag. recvr.-gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr.-gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1898; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apr., 1900, to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Settmts., June, 1901; admntd. govt. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apr., 1904, and Feb. to June, 1906; ag. recvr.-gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1904; confirmed, Jan., 1905; ret., 1910; in charge of F.M.S. development agency, London, Oct., 1910; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912.
TELFER, ELIZABETH WILSON.—B. 1895; headmistress, govt. girls' schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 1st Oct., 1924.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; asst. gov. chemist, Leeward Is.; 1903; ag. govt. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909; govt. chemist and supt. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; offl. mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibtn., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees, Antigus pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; reported on S. African sugar industry, 1917; organised govt. tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agrl. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prin., Mauritius Agrl. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar industry, 1926; organising vice-pres., Mauritius agrl. and indus. exhibtn., 1926; chmn., various... comtees.; dir., agr., Malaya, 1929; author of numerous papers and repts. on agrl. chem., cane sugar production, agrl. educn. and trop. agr.

TENISON, EDWARD HERON RYAN.—B. 1898; on mily. serv., Apr., 1917; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., Dec., 1921; office ast., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., June, 1925; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Nov., 1926; pol. mag., Matale, Dec., 1927.

TENNANT, MATTHEW PETTIGREW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Montrose Academy; ch. ast. engr., Jamaica govt. rly., 1905; ag. loco. supt., ditto, July, 1909 to Dec., 1910; ag. ch. engr., Mar.-Nov., 1913; senr. inspgr. engr., P.W.D., Jamaica, Apr., 1914; ag. ast. dir., P.W.D., Dec., 1918 to Sept., 1921; ag. dir. of pub. wks., July-Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Uganda, 28th July, 1924.

TENNENT, JAMES HALL.—B. 1889; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1911; sen. house surg., Edinburgh Royal Infirmary; house physician, Cumberland Infirmary, Carlisle; house surg. and ast. health off., Penang and Singapore, S. Settmts., 1912-1914; seconded for service in Kedah, 1914; resumed duty in S. Stltmts., 1918; ag. surg., native hosp., Singapore, and lectr. in clinical and operative surg. to the King Edward VII. med. schl.; ag. senr. surg., Penang, 1919.

TETTEY, VICTOR ADOLPHUS, B.A. (Durham).—B. 1891; 2nd cls. teacher, Accra Training Coll., Gold Coast, 22nd Jan., 1915; ag. housemast., 10th Mar., 1919 to 8th Oct., 1919; ditto, 11th Jan., 1920; 1st cls. teacher and ast. headmast., 1st Apr., 1923; inspector, schools, 9th July, 1926.

THAINE, ROBERT NEIMANN, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1875; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1898; off. ast. to govt. agt. S. Prov., Apr., 1899; Cent. Prov., Aug., 1900; comanr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, June, 1901; off. ast. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Dec., 1901; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, Mar., 1902; dist. judge, Badulla, May, 1902; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1904; landing surv. cust., Colombo, Sept., 1904; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1906; Puttalam, Aug., 1906; dist. judge, Jaffna, July, 1908; comanr. of requests, Colombo, Jan., 1911; ast. govt. agent, Mannar, Sept., 1911; ag. govt. agt., Province of Sabaragamawwa, Feb., 1913; pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1915; comanr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1917; censor, 18th Apr., 1917; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, May, 1918; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Apr., 1919; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, June, 1920; ag. prin. collr. of cust. and food contr., Nov., 1921; govt. agt., W. Prov., Dec., 1923; ag. col. sec., Oct.-Nov., 1925.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram. schl.; examr. of loan acts., audit dept., O.R.C., 1st Jan., 1904; examr. of acts., ditto, 1st July, 1904; provincial auditor, O.F.S. Prov., 16th Apr., 1912.

THEILER, SIR ARNOLD, K.C.M.G.—B. 1867; state dipl. as vety. surg., Switzerland, 1889; dr. med. vet., Berne, 1901; hon. assoc., R. Coll. Vety. Surgeons, 1908; C.M.G., 1907; K.C.M.G., 1914; D.Sc., Cape, 1911; D.Sc., Syracuse, 1923; D.Ph., Berne, 1923; D.V.Sc., S. Africa, 1925; came to S. Africs., 1892; dir., lymph farm, Johannesburg, 1893; vety. surg. to sanry. bd. and mine sanitation dept., Johannesburg, 1894-95; sp. mission to Rhodesia on outbreak of rinderpest, 1896; govt. vety. surg. to Transvaal Republic, 1896-1900; vety. surg. to Transvaal State Arty., 1898; dir., bacteriological lab. and vaccine inst., S.A. Repub., 1898; govt. vety. bacteriologist, 1900; dir., vety. research, Union of S. Africa, 1910; dir., Lamiekste research, 1919; dir., vety. educn. and research, 1920; dean of faculty of vety. sci. and prof. of infectious diseases, Union of S. Africa, 1920; sp. mission to Europe to represent Transvaal and Natal govt., at Baden-Baden, 1899; sp. mission to Rhodesia in connection with Rhodesian tick fever, 1902; repd. Transvaal govt. at 8th International Vety. Congress, Budapest, 1906, at 9th International Vety. Congress, The Hague, 1909, and at Pan African Vety. Congresses, Bloemfontein, 1902, Cape Town, 1905, Pretoria, 1909, and at World's Dairy Confce, Syracuse, 1923; ret.

THEODORE, HON. E. G.—B. 1884; M.L.A. for Woothakata, Queensland, Oct., 1909; M.L.A. for Chillogoe since 1912; dep. leader of the labour party, 1913; treas. and sec. for pub. wks. in Ryan miny., June, 1915; premier of Queensland, 1919-26; mem., house of reps., O'wealth of Australia, 1927.
THEUNISSEN, E. D.—Ast. supt. pol., Kenya, Nov., 1922.
THIBOU, CLEMENT GABRY.—Foreman of wks., P.W.D., Antigua, Mar., 1915; ast. town clk., St. Kitts, Apr., 1916; ag. supt. of pub. wks., Aug., 1916.
THOM, M. ST. C.—B. 1887; S. African constab., 1905-10; ast. supt. of police, E. Africa Prot., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, Jan., 1921.
THOMAS, A. MUSGRAVE, Barrister-at-Law.—Crown counsel, Kenya, Oct., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Nov., 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct., on various occasions, 1925-1928.

THOMAS, CHARLES CLIFFORD.—B. 1890; ed. Blundell's; 2nd lieut., The Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1909; India, 1914-15; Aden Field Force, Jan., 1915; Egyptian Expdy. Force, Jan., 1916-19; adjt., 1/4th D.C.L.I., 1917-19; ment. in despa.; Territorial Decoration; adjt., 4th D.C.L.I. (T.F.), 1919-24; pte. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and clk. to couns., 1926.

THOMAS, HUGH WHITELEGGE.—B. 1887; ed. Jesus Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; ast. dist. comanr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1913; ag. dist. comanr. at various times 1913-17; dist. comanr., June, 1919; ag. ast. coll. sec., Oct., 1919; ag. comanr., cent. prov., May to Aug., 1924; dep. prov. comanr., May, 1926; ag. comanr., W. prov., Apr., 1927 to Mar., 1928; dep. sec., for native affrs., Apr., 1928.

THOMAS, Rt. Hon. JAMES HENRY, P.C. (1917), M.P., J.P., LL.D., Cambridge.—B. 1875; served as cleaner, fireman and driver, G.W.R.; has served in turn as organiser, ast. sec., gen. sec. and pres., Nat. Union of Rlymen; gov. of Dulwich College; mem., Balfour mission to America, 1917; M.P. for Derby since 1910; J.P., Surrey, 1920; S. of S. for Cols., 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924; author of "When Labour Rules" and other works.

THOMAS, SAMUEL JOYCE.—Ed. King's Coll., London; Inglis schl. and early English Text Soc. prizeman; assoc. (with distinc.) King's Coll., 1897; B.A., Lond., 1897; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1898; enlisted, 1915; commis- sioned, R.A.S.C. (M.T.), and attd. to R.G.A. (145th Siege Battery), France, Flanders, Italy invalided, 1919; ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1919; ag. col. sec. and admstr., St. Vincent, Feb. to Dec., 1920, and on three subsequent occasions; thanked by S. of S. for index of laws, St. Vincent, 1921; mem., W. Indian ct. of appl., 1921-23; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, Mar., 1923; chmn., oil and water bd., 1923; ag. atty.-gen., 1923; ag. 1st puisne judge on various occasions, 1925-26 and in 1928.

THOMAS, THOMAS SHENTON WHITELEGGE, C.M.G. (1929), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., Hons. 1901; ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd ast. sec., Apr., 1911; sen. ast. sec., Apr., 1912; ag. ast. ch. sec., Aug.-Oct., 1913; Aug., 1916-Mar., 1917, and Apr., 1917-Jan., 1919; elec. to exec. and legis. couns., 1917-19; mem. E. Africa civ. serv. comm., 1918; ast. ch. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Uganda development comm., 1919; gov's. dep., Jan.-Feb., 1920; prtn. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.-Dec., 1921; dep. ch. sec., 30th Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., 8th May to 11th Nov., 1925 and from 19th Aug. to 10th Nov., 1926; gov's. dep., 20th to 30th May, 15th June to 13th July, 17th Aug. to 16th Sept., 1925 and from 31st Oct. to 10th Nov., 1926; col. sec., Gold Coast, 1927.
THOMPSON, AUBREY DENZIL FORBITH.—Ed. Weenen County Coll., Natal and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); lieut., R.F.A., 1917-19; cadet, Uganda Prot., 19th June, 1921; A.D.C., 18th July, 1923.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924.

THOMPSON, HENRY BURFORD.—B. 1880; cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Apr., 1906; acctnt., ditto, Apr., 1906; ag. 1st treasry. offr., Montserrat, Aug.-Nov., 1906; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Antigua Dec., 1906; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1907 to Mar., 1908; 3rd outdoor offr., treasry., Jan., 1908; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1911 to Mar., 1912, and from Apr. to Sept., 1912; student, Middle Temple, 1912; cashier, treasry., May, 1914; ag. 1st clk., registr.'s office, May, 1915; commr. to admstr. oaths, Antigua, July, 1916.

THOMPSON, HERBERT W.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., 4th Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from 13th Aug., 1917 to 9th Apr., 1919; apptd., after compet. exam., astl. clk., C.O., 18th June, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to 2nd div. clk., 24th Feb., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
THOMPSON, JOHN BAIRD, C.B.E. (1929), M.N.Z. Soc. C.I.—B. 1868; ed. secondary and private schls.; joined lands and survey dept., N.Z., 1891; under-seer. for lands, 1922.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B. 1892; matric., London Univ., 1912; diploma, English language and lit., London Univ., 1921; appt. after compet. exam., astl. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., apptd. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 24th Aug., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; astl. librarian, 10th Oct., 1925; ag. registr., col. laws and astl. librarian, 19th Nov., 1928.

THOMPSON, JOHN WILLIAM.—B. 1880; L. & N.W. rly., 1894; addl. to R. Engnr. for active serv. in Sudan and S. Africa, 1898-1902; I.M. rly., C.S.A. rly., and S. African rly., in Transvaal, 1902; Buenos Ayres Pacific rly., Argentine, 1907-9; G.I.P. rly., India, 1912; Indian Expdty. Force "B," German E. Africa, 1917-19; ment. in despatches; supt.in-ch. of salvage work, July, 1918; dist. loco. supt., Dec., 1918; ast. loco. supt., Tanganyika rly., Apr., 1919.

THOMPSTONE, CAPT. ERIC WESTBURY, M.O.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury Schl.; 2nd lieut., King's Shropshire Light Infy., 1915; served in France and Italy, 1916-19; wounded; silver med. for valour (Italy); adminstr. offr., N.P., Nigeria, July, 1919.

THOMSON, FRANK DAVID, B.A., C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Napier and Canterbury and Victoria Colls., N.Z.; cadet educn. dept., 1894; priv. sec. to various ministers since 1901; sec. to prime min., since 1912; clk. of exec. coun. and sec. of cabinet since 1919; perm. head, prime minister's dept., 1926; mem. of secretariat, Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; mem., Imp. Confce. secretariat, 1917, 1918, 1921, 1923 and 1926.

THOMSON, SIR GRAEME, K.C.M.G. (1928), K.C.B.—B. 1875; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; higher div. clk., Admiralty, 1900; astl. dir. of trans., 1914; dir. of trans., 1914; col. sec., Ceylon, Sept., 1919; offr., admstg. govt., 8th-18th Jan., 1920 and Mar.-Sept., 1920; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, B. Guiana, 1923; gov. and commander-in-chief, Nigeria, 1925.

THOMSON, HENRY WAGSTAFFE, B.A. (Oxon.), C.M.G., (1927).—B. 1874; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Selangor, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1897; passed cadet, Dec., 1898; ag. astl. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Feb., 1898; ditto Serendah, May, 1898; 2nd astl. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Dec., 1898; ag. astl. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1899; ag. astl. dist. offr., Serendah, Apr., 1900; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1900; seconded for service in Kelantan, May, 1903; resigned F.M.S. serv., and in the serv. of the Kelantan govt., May, 1906 to July, 1909; offr., cls. III., F.M.S., performing duties of astl. adviser, Kelantan, July, 1909; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Jan., 1910; collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and regiar. of titles, Selangor, Feb., 1914; ag. regiar., titles, Perak, Oct., 1914; ag. supt., convict estab., Taiping, in addn., Feb., 1915; supt., convict estab., Taiping, and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., Jan., 1917; ag. regiar., titles, Perak, in addn., May, 1918; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1918; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Feb., 1919; confirmed in appt., Oct., 1919; Br. res., Pahang, July, 1921; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Dec., 1925; Br. res., Selangor, Feb., 1926; ag. Br. res., Perak, Sept., 1926; Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1926 ag. ch. sec. to govt. F.M.S., May and Sept., 1927.

THOMSON, JAMES MOFFAT.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll.; joined N.E. Rhodesia admstrn., 13th June, 1903; transf'd to N.W.R., 1st Oct., 1905; ag. collr., 1st Apr., 1906; native commr., 1st Apr., 1907; ag. astl. mag., 3rd Sept., 1907 to 22nd Oct., 1911; astl. mag., N.R., 23rd Oct., 1911; has acted as mag. on various occasions between Sept., 1917 and Oct., 1923; ag. sec. for mines, 14th Aug. to 9th Dec., 1922; mag., 18th Oct., 1923; J.P.
THOMSON, J. H., M.B., Bsc., Surg.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913; med. offr., Uganda, Dec., 1923; medical offr., Tanganyika Territory, October, 1924.

THOMSON, REGINALD FRANCIS, F.R.A.I., F.R.G.S.—B. 1879; ch. inspr., native labourers for Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Dec., 1920; ag. temp. dist. offr., Shortland, and dep. commr. for W. Pacific in Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1925-27.

THORNE, WM. HOBART HOUGHTON.—B.1875; ed. at St. Paul's Sch., King's Coll., Camb., and Paris Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1900; Licencié en Droit de la Faculté de Paris, Nov., 1903; British representative on Internat. Claims Coman. at Casa Blanca, 7th May, 1908; employed at F.O. in investigation of Anglo-American pecuniary claims, 16th Sept., 1910, to 11th July, 1911; pres. of dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 20th July, 1911; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1912, and in Ottoman Turkish, June, 1916; judge of the mixed tribunal, Alexandria, 18th Sept., 1916; resig. 5th Mar., 1917; British commr. on the mixed indemnities coman., Athens, 5th Mar., 1917; re-apptd. a judge of the mixed tribunal, Alexandria, 1st Feb., 1919; is commr. of the Order of the Saviour.

THORNE, WILLIAM HUXTABLE.—B. 1882; ed. privately; solr., 1903; commanded 3rd Volr. Batt., Lincoln Regt., 1904; called to local bar of S.S., 1907 and of F.M.S., 1908; censor and mem., S.S. Defence Force, munici. commr., Penang, 1914; mem., leg. coun., S.S., 1920; capt., Penang, Volr. R. of O.; puisne judge, S.S. and judge, F.M.S., Aug., 1926.
THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd after compet. exam., asst. clk., sec.'s office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transfd. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, Lieut.-Col. (TEMP. COL.), Sir EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.F. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., Esq., of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; asst. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosps. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape, 1910; major, med. br., sec. I, Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in desps.); Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C.S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comtee. of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Roehampton, and mem., comtee. management, Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comtee. on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problems arising out of the war, 1916-20; asst. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for the Union, 1920.

THORNTON, Sir HENRY WORTH, K.B.E. (1919), M.I.C.E.—B. 1871; ed. St. Paul's Sch., Concord, New Hampshire, U.S.A., Univ. of Pennsylvania; ent. serv., Penna Rly., (lines west of Pittsburgh) in engnr. dept., 1894; gen. supt., Long Is. Rly., 1911-14; gen. man., G.E. Rly (Eng.), 1914; dep. dir., inland waterways and docks, 1917; asst. dir-gen., movements and rlys. with temp. rank of col., 1917; dep. dir-gen., movements and rlys. with rank of brig.-gen., 1918; inspr.-gen., transportation, with temp. rank of major-gen., 1919; Cross of Offr., Order of Leopold, 1919; French Legion of Honour, 1920; American D.S.M., 1919; chmn., bd. of dirs. and president, Canadian National Rly., 1922.

THORNTON, HUGH CHOLMONDELEY, C.M.G. (1920), C.V.O. (1920).—B.1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916 to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; apptd. crown agt. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, R.W.—B.1881; ed. Graaff Reinet and Eiensenburg Schl. of Agr., Cape Prov. (dipl. with hon.); served in Anglo-Boer War as Scout and intelligence offr.; joined dept. of agr., Cape Prov. field crops and experiment and research section, 1906; offr.-in-ch., agric. research branch, agric. dept., Cape prov., 1908; prtn., Grootfontein Schl. of Agr. and experiment station, 1910; sent by govt. to England, Europe and Australia to study agric. educn., experiment station organisation, etc., took charge of expedn. to W. Central Africa, in connection with ostrich industry of Union, 1912; active serv. in France, 1917; resumed charge of Grootfontein Schl. of Agr., 1919; dir., field and animal husbandry, Oct., 1923.

THOROGOOD, WILLIAM JESSE, M.C.—B. 1869; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; enlisted, K.R.R.C., Sept., 1915; 2nd lieut., Tank Corps, Jan., 1917; M.C., Sept., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd. to Chinese prot., Ipoh; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Mar., 1922; ast. contr. of lab., Klang, May, 1923; passed cadet, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; ast. contr. of lab., Negri Sembilan and Malacca, Aug., 1924; attd., land office, Batu Gejah, Jan., 1926; offr., dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampor, Feb., 1926; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1927; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1927; ag. dist. offr., K. Langat, Sept., 1927; ag. ast. contr. lab., Klang, Mar., 1928; offr., cls. IV., Apr., 1928.

THRELFALL, WM. HERBERT.—B. 1875; apptd., after open compet., exam., to controller's off., London postal service, Apr., 1898; supt. of registn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1905; ag. supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1906; supt. of regian., ditto, Sept., 1906; supt. of mails, Penang, Sept., 1907; supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, Jan., 1912; ag. ast. postm.-gen., Penang, July, 1914; ast. G.P.O., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. ast. P.M.G., Oct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., May, 1919; ag. ast. P.M.G., Singapore, May, 1919; ag. ast. P.M.G., Penang, July, 1919; ast. P.M.G., Penang, July, 1920; ast. P.M.G., Singapore, but continued to act as above, Sept., 1920.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postm. in 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1905, 1906 and 1907; postm. of Antigua, and federal postm. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor in 1908, 1913, 1914 and 1915; chmn., bd. of guardians in 1911, 1913 and 1915; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912; ag. ast. censor and ch. censor during war; dep. chmn., city comsrs., 4th Dec., 1916; ag. offr. in ch. Antigua—Montserrat flashlight serv., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; ag. comsrs., Virgin Is., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; warden and mag., Nevis, Oct., 1920; mem., exec. and leg. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. comsrs., Montserrat and mem., exec. and leg. couns., Leeward Is., May to Aug., 1927 and Jan. to Apr., 1928; rep. Dominica at gen. leg. coun., 1928.

TIBBITS, ELWOOD D'ARCY, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1899 to Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., July, 1901; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk. to exec. coun., Dominica, Aug., 1905 to Apr., 1906; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Sept., 1906; 2nd clk., clk. to exec. and legis. couns. and aspt. of govt. printing off., Antigua, Oct., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov., in 1903, 1904, 1911 and 1912; ag. ast. col. sec., in 1907, 1908, 1911 and 1912; ag. ch. clk. on abolition of off. of ast. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912; ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1912; ast. col. sec., Oct., 1920; ag. col. sec., in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; admst. govt. of Antigua on several occasions during that period; comsnr. for St. John, Nov., 1926; ag. comsnr., Montserrat, Jan., 1926.

TIBBITT, CAPT. ERIC MONTAGUE (R. of O.).—B. 1896; served in European War, 1914-18; priv. sec. to gov., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1920; ag. clk., exec. coun., Br. Honduras, Nov., 1922; ast. audr., Nigeria, Aug., 1924; audr., Br. Honduras, Feb., 1928; capt., Br. Honduras Defence Force, Apr., 1928.

TIGHRE, MACARTAN H., B.A., B.L.—(Called to Irish Bar, 1910); lieut., R. Dublin Fusiliers, France, 1916; capt., 1917, ment. in desps.; demob., 1920; crown pros., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. solr. gen., Jan. to July, 1923, Nov., 1923 to Sept., 1924 and June to Dec., 1925.

TILLEY, ALBERT B. 1878; impl. serv., 1896-02; post off., exchequer and audit depts.; transferred to Transvaal serv., Mar., 1902; clk., audit off., Transvaal, 1902; senr. clk., 1909; ditto, audit off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; prov. audr. and local govt. inspr., Transvaal Prov., May, 1915.

TIMMS, CHARLES GORDON, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S.Ed., Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1886; ed. R. Coll., Surgeons, Edin.; served with R.A.M.C., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; M.O., Uganda, 26th Jan., 1922; transf., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1922.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria; K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1st Dec., 1922.
TISDALE, E. G. S.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

TISDALE, HERBERT GEORGE, B.Sc., A.R.C.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schol. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schol.), 1904; eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; ast. engr., engrg. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engrg. contracts dept., 1926.

TITCHENER, HENRY IAN HERRICK.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; temp. ag. higher grade cler. offr., 13th July, 1928; ast. librarian, 14th Jan., 1929.
TODD, CEDRIC LINDELEY.—B. 1897; ed. Sherborne Schol.; served in European War, Western fronts, 1914-18 and Rhine Army, 1919-21; ast. audr., Nyasaland, Nov., 1921.

TODD, RONALD RUSKIN.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924; passed Cantonese, June, 1927; astl. secretariat for Chinese affrs., 1927.
TOLMIE, ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1884; ed. Kincaidine high schol., Toronto Univ.; apptd. to Canadian govt. exhibn. oomsnr., 1908; Canadian govt. exhibn. comsnr., Aug., 1919.

TOLMIE, HOW. SIMON FRASER.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria High Schol., Ontario, and Vet. Coll., Toronto; farmer and breeder of live stock; pres., Dominion Holstein Breeders, B. Columbia Vet. Assoc., B. Columbia Holstein Breeders; chf. inspr. of health of animals branch, B. Columbia; B. C. representative on live stock comm.; elec. to Canadian H. of C., g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. of agric. in fed. govt., Aug., 1919; resig. portfolio on defeat of admtn., in general election, Dec., 1921.
TOMKINSON, C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS FOWLER, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, and Leeds University; ast. engr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1911; served with R. Engnr., France, 1915-19; res. engr., constrn., Trinidad govt. rly., 1920; temp. D.P.W., Virgin Is., 1926; dist. engr., P.W.D., Br. Guiana, 1927; ch. engr., govt. rly., Trinidad, 1928.

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, KT. B.A.OH. (1926).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, May, 1907; ast. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, Jan., 1911; ag. judge, Feb. to Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admtn. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; prepared revised edn. of the laws of Zanzibar, 1922; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923; ret., 1928.

TOMSON, GRAHAM.—B. 1887; ed. Elmdon Hall and Dover Coll.; inspr., plantations, Zanzibar, Apr., 1921; agri. offr., 1924; ag. ast. dir., agr., May-Oct., 1924 and Jan.-April, 1926; game ranger, Tanganyika, Sept., 1926; ag. game warden, Jan. to July, 1927; man., plantations, Zanzibar, July, 1927.

TONGE, LIEUT. COMDR. C. G., R.N.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; naval transport offr., Aug. to Dec., 1914; lent for serv. in gunboats on Lake Nyasa, Jan., 1915; senr. naval offr., Lake Nyasa, and 1st offr., marine transport dept., June, 1919; Royal Naval Res. Offr.'s Decoration.
TONGUE, ERIC DAUNOY, B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhibr.); ast. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 8th October, 1913; dist. comsnr., 15th Aug., 1923.

TOPP, WM.—B. 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. Nigeria govt., Oct., 1908 to Sept., 1910; transf., to the Gambia, Sept., 1910; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1911; ch. clk., June, 1913; sp. cls. apptmt., Jan., 1920; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1924; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, July-Aug., 1925; J.P. and comsnr., ct. of requests, July, 1925.

TOPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Diocesan schol., Waterford, Ireland; served as book keeper and acctnt. with W. Australian land co. (Great Southern rly.), 1887 to 1897; on taking over of line entd. govt. ser. as sub-acctnt., rly. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; ast. acctnt., 1st July, 1900; ag. ch. acctnt., 1st July, 1903; aud.-gen. for State of W. Aust., Feb., 1904; also dep. aud.-gen. for Commonwealth, Feb., 1904, to Jan., 1906.
TORY, JAMES CRANSWICK.—B. 1862; ed. common schol., Guysboro Acad., Wesleyan Theological Coll., Montreal; Nova Scotia legislature, 1911; re-el., 1916; lieut.-gov., Nova Scotia, 1st Oct., 1926.

TOTHILL, Francis James.—Extra asst. to postuntr.-gen., Ceylon, 28th Oct., 1898; asst. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; asst. sec., cent. irrigtn. bd., 15th Jan., 1900; sec., ditto, and office asst. to dir. of irrigtn., 15th May, 1900; asst. acctnt., gen. treasry., Jan., 1907; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., gen. treasry., Apr., 1911 to Mar., 1912, and Nov., 1915 to Mar., 1916; ag. paymr., Ceylon volr. force, Aug., 1914; ag. staff offr. in add., Apr., 1915; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., gen. treasry., Nov., 1915; 1st asst. acctnt., Apr., 1916; ag. finan. asst. and acctnt., Apr. to Nov., 1921; finan. asst. and acctnt., July, 1927.

TOUSSAINT, James Reginald.—B. 1879; ag. off. asst., Matara Kach, Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist Temporalities ord., comsnr., and extra off. asst. to dir. of educn., Nov., 1918; ag. off. asst. to col. auditor, Apr., 1919; paptd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., local divn., July, 1919; ag. asst. col. audnr., Mar., 1920; ag. sec. to food contr., July, 1921; addnl. asst. comsnr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; promoted cls. IV, Mar., 1926.

OWNER, H. V., Assoc. Mem., Inst. Civ. Engrns.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stiltns., Mar., 1901; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1904; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, Apr. to Sept., 1902, and from Nov., 1904, to Mar., 1908; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1909; ag. dep. col. engrnr., Penang, Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., Singapore, Mar., 1915; on sp. duty in Malacca on road reconstruction, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engrnr. and survr.-gen., Malacca, Feb., 1919; exec. engrnr., grade I., Malacca, Dec., 1919; dep. col. engrnr., S. Stiltns., Feb., 1921; ag. col. engrnr., Mar., 1921.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, 1926.

TRAFFORD, Joseph Louis.—B. 1888; ed. Beaumont Coll.; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., June, 1915, and from July, 1916 to July, 1917; asst. dist. comsnr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr. for various periods, 1915-20; ag. asst. col. sec., June to Aug., 1920; dist. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1921; asst. col. sec., 11th Oct., 1922.

TRATMAN, David Wm.—Ed. at Clifton Coll., schlr. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1904; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. land offr. in New Territories, and regar. of land et., 1907; mem. of land et., 1907; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., 1909; 2nd asst. regar.-gen., 1909; ag. supt. of imports and exports, 1910; 1st asst. regar.-gen., 1911; ag dist. offr., N.T., 1911; ag. head of sanitary dept., 1912; asst. dist. offr., N.T., (South), 1912; ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; ag. dist. offr., N.T., 1915; ag. head of sanitary dept., 1915; head of sanitary dept., 1917; ag. supt. of imports and exports, 1917; local priority authority, 1918; ag. col. treas., 1920 and 1922; dist. offr., N. Territory, 1920; ag. col. treas., 1924; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., 1925; 1st cls. cadet offr., ag. offl. recr. and ag. asst. col. sec., and clk. of councils, 1926.

TRAVERS, Michael F.—B. 1897; ed. St. Mary's and O'Connell's Schls., Dublin; boy clk., Irish land comsnr., Dublin, 8th May, 1913; asst. clk., board of educn., 22nd Apr., 1915; war serv., R. Irish Rege., 19th Apr., 1916 to 5th Dec., 1916; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1921; transf. to C.O., 7th Apr., 1926.

TREE, Francis Thomas, B.A., Dublin.—B. 1887; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Jan., 1911; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, June, 1911; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., sp. res., Nov., 1915; lieut., Sept., 1916; attd. R.E. for research in poison gas, Mar., 1918; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, May, 1920; ag. asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Johore, Oct., 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; ag. asst. advr., Muar, May, 1924.

TREMELTT, Frederick Thomas George, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1881; ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; 2nd lieut., Jamaica Militia, 1902; lieut., 1903; attached Lance Fusiliers, 1903; sub-inspr. constab., Jamaica, 1903; ag. inspr., ditto, 1908-1911; studied finger-print system, Scotland Yard, 1908; J.P., 1909; inspr. of constab., Jamaica, 1912; supt., Spanish Town fire brigade, 1911-1912; passed mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1912; transf. to Mauritius pol., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, 1916; capt. and adjt., Mauritius volr. force, 1916; capt. in army, 1917; raised and comd. Mauritius labour batt., 1917; comd. Mauritius volr. force, Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, May, 1917, and Mar. to Dec., 1919; major, 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., supt., prisons and C.C. Mauritius Vol. Force, Feb. to Sept., 1922; asst. comsnr., pol. and prisons, Uganda, Nov., 1922.

TREMELTT, Capt. John Daniel Schweder, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Hartow Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; war serv., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; M.C., 1917; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923.
TRENCH, Arthur Donald Le Poer, O.A.C. (dep.).—Senr. coffee offr., agrl. dept., Kenya, Oct., 1916.

TREWIN, Capt. Algernon Burney.—B. 1884; ed. the Royal Naval Schl. and Sandhurst; ent. Army (Essex Regt.), Jan., 1905; instr. of signalling, 1908; seconded, K.A.R., Uganda, 1908; coy. comdr., 1911; seconded, Zanzibar armed constab., 1912; ent. Uganda adminstr., July, 1914; adjt., Uganda vols., Aug., 1914; comsnr., gen. res. of offrs., 1919; dist. comsnr., Uganda, Aug., 1923.

TROWBRIDGE, Edmund.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schl., Toronto, Ontario; asst. clk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coalcomsnr., 1907; sec., Alberta pork comsnr., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and regiar. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

TRUMP, Philip, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. Exeter Schl. and King's Coll., Lond., civ. engnr.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Aug., 1910; ex-engnr., Grade III, 1919; ex-engnr., malaria advisy. bd., July, 1921; ex-engnr., wks. and bldgs., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1923; ag. senr. ex-engnr., Johore, May, 1924; ditto, Coast, Pahang, Oct., 1926; senr. ex-engnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1927; ag. state engrnr., Trengganu, Apr., 1927.
TRUPHET, John Sydney.—B. 1883; ed. Roan Schl. and King's Coll., London; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1901; asst. head, gen. stores dept., 1919; dep. head, shipping dept., 1927.

TRUTER, Sir Thedorus Gustaff, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George; Normal Coll., Capetown; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; entd. Cape civ. ser., 1st Dec., 1892; chief examr. of accts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (medal and four clasps); asst. mag., Pretoria, 1st Apr., 1901; mem., spec. criminal ct.; mag., Ermelo, 1st Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, 1st Apr., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, 1st July, 1910; oomsnr., S. African pol., 15th Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa.

TUBOKU-METZGER, ALBERT EMERIO, B.A.—Ed. in C.M.S. Gram. schl. and Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone; gained Fourah Bay Coll. foundation scholarship, Jan., 1877; grad. in Arts, June, 1880; first cls. hon. in theology, Durham Univ., Dec., 1881; jun. cls. tutor, Fourah Bay Coll., 1881; entd. S. Leone civ. serv. as extra clk., secretariat and treasy., 1st Mar., 1885; gov'ts. office, 1886; col. treasy., 1887; served as finan. clk. in the Yonni expdn. under Sir Samuel Rowe, 1887; ch. clk., registr.-gen.'s dept., 1890; registr. of births and deaths, Freetown dist., 1890; police clk. and clk. of cls. of requests, Freetown, Apr., 1896; ag. registr.-gen., 1904 to 1905, and 1907; asst. dist. commr., 1st May, 1908; appld. Crown prosecutor in the Sept. sessions of the sup. ct. at Sherbro, Oct., 1908; is a J.P. for the col.; asst. dist. commr., N. Sherbro dist., 1914; Karene dist., 1916; mem., lunatic asylum visiting comte., 1924; el. rural mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1924.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, F.I.A.A., certified acctnt.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-8; entd. engineering dept., office of crown agts., for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf'd to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Sept., 1913; asst. audtr., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; despatched Pretoria, on mily. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1914 (ment. in desps.); lieut., G. Coast Volks., Apl., 1915; hon. temp. capt., Nyasaland Field Force, 1919; asst. auditor, Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; ag. auditor, Apl., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dep. treas., June, 1921; dep. treas., Dec., 1921; ag. treas., Apr., 1923; treas., 14th Jan., 1924.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Institn. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys' schl., St. Helena, 1904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec. and treas., govt. lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911; govt. rep. on Hussey Charity bd. of educn., 1911; J.P. and coroner and mem., bd. of health, 1912; lieut., St. Helena Rifles, Sept., 1914 to Nov., 1916; lieut., R.A.S.C., Apl., 1917 to Nov., 1919; inspr. schls., Leeward Is., Feb., 1920; chmn., gen. purposes comte., Antigua bd. of educn.; chmn., St. Kitts bd. of educn., 1922.

TURBETT, IVAN JOILLE TUFNELL.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1915; ag. crown sol., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-23 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926 ag. atty.-gen. in 1926, 1927 and 1928.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legis., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ot. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNBULL, AUBREY MARRIOTT DALWAY.—Ed. Bath Coll.; clk., treasy., B.C. Africa, Jan., 1903; editor, B. C. A. Gazette, Nov., 1903, to Sept., 1906; 3rd ols. res., May, 1906; ag. registr. of high ct., offl. recvr. and liquidator, May to Dec., 1908; ag. res. Milane dist., July, 1909, to Mar., 1910, and May to Nov., 1912; dist. mag., July, 1906; ag. supt. of native affairs and comml. intell. offr., May, 1910, to Mar., 1911; attached to deputy gov'ts. office for special duty, Sept., 1911, to Apl., 1912; ag. sec. to the administration, Nyasaland Prot., Dec., 1912; asst. ch. sec., Mar., 1914; mem. of native rising comm., 1915; dist. pol. offr. in occupied territory in German E. Africa, July, 1916; adminis. officer, 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st April, 1920; hon. major, Nyasaland Field Force.

TURNBULL, DAVID OLIVER, D.S.O.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; diploma, R.C.V.S., Dec., 1903; lieut., A.V.D., Sept., 1904 to July, 1908 and Nov., 1914 to Nov., 1915; capt., Nov., 1915; major, Apr., 1916 (A.D.V.S., 27th divn.); lieut.-col., 1918 (A.D.V.S., 16th Corps); despa., 1916, 1917, 1918; French Croix de Guerre, Mar., 1917; D.S.O., June, 1917; N. Rhodesia vety. dept., Sept., 1920; ag. ch. vety. offr., N. Rhodesia on two occasions in 1927.

TURNER, EDWARD PHILLIPS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1865; ed. Hutchins Schi., Hobart (Tas.) and pvt. tutors; survey work in N.Z. and Tasmania; entd. N.Z. lands and survey dept., 1894; insp. of scenic reserves, 1907; sec. royal comm. on forestry, 1913; mem., royal comm. on scenery of Wanganui river, 1914; sec. of forestry, 1919; dir. forestry, 1928.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 5 clasps); depot sergt.-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offir. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919.
TURNER, CAPT. H. W.—Port offr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1915.

TURNER, JAMES POSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; commr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1906; 1st clk., registr.'s off., Antigua, 3rd May, 1906; commr. to admstr. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. to trustees, sup. ct. library, June, 1906; clk. to registr. of jt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; ag. registr. and prov.-marshal, Dominica, Oct., 1910, to Feb., 1912; ditto, Antigua, and ch. registr., Leeward Is., 17th Feb. to 31st March, 1912, 26th Apl. to 20th Nov., 1914, and from 4th May to 5th Nov., 1915; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B" Antigua, Apl., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; registr. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief registr., 1st Sept., 1917.

TURNER, Lewis James Barnetson.—B. 1885; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1908; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1911; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; asst. comsnr. of excise, Nov., 1913; asst. comsnr., S. Div., Jan., 1914; ditto, N.W. Div., Aug., 1916; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1918; asst. comsnr. of excise, N.W. divn., and asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1919; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1919; supt. of census, July, 1920; dir. of statistics in admn., Sept., 1920; designation changed to dir., statistics and office systems, Oct., 1926.

TURTON, Neville.—B. 1889; ed. at Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.), 1911; M.A., 1915; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; ass. audr., S. Leone, 1912; ag. audr. on four occasions; volunteer, R.G.A., Sierra Leone; asst. to the atty.-gen., Uganda, 1921; 1st cls. mag. and judge in admn.; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, June, 1924; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; chmn., income tax comsnr., 1924; mem., educn. bd., 1925; ag. col. sec., 1925 and 1926; ag. ch. just., 1925, 1926, 1927 and 1928; admstd. govt., Sept., 1926; solr.-gen., Uganda, Jan., 1928.
TUTE, Richard Clifford.—B. 1874; ed. Royal Schl. of St. Peter, York; ent. I.C.S., 1898; served in Gt. War; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple), 1923; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1925.

TWELLS, John.—Ed. at Jesus Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., 1907; examr., E. and A. Dept., July, 1909; asst. auditor, S. Leone, Nov., 1909; asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., July, 1912; senr. asst. mast., educn. dept., Kenya, 1919.

TWEMLOW, George Brownbill.—B. 1897; ed. at Rossall Schl.; mily. serv., 1916-20; demob. with rank of capt.; re-enlisted, 1920-23; joined botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1923; asst. supt., botanical and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1927.

TWOSE, Lawrence Rendle.—B. 1891; clk. to mag., dist. A, 1915; sec. to fire brig. comsnr., 1916; lieut., H.M. forces, 1916-19; ag. A.D.C. to gov., Leeward Is., on occasion of visit of Prince of Wales, 1920; clk. to comsnr., clk., couns., dep. coroner, educn. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1920; J.P., chmn., prison justices, 1924; mag., coroner, regar.-gen., sup. ct., 1924; M.E.C. and M.I.C., 1925.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E. A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Sc. (Eng.) with 1st cls. hons., civil engnr., Liverpool Univ., 1909; 2nd lieut., R.E. (sp. res.) 1910; asst. engnr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1911; exec. engnr., 1917; capt., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., Gambi Dec., 1920.
TYNDALL, Eustace John.—B. 1895; ed. Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 9th July, 1924.

TYRRELL, Francis Graeme, C.M.G. (1928).—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admstg. the govt., Apr., 1902; off. asst. to govt. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1904; seconded supt. of pol., N.W. prov., Aug., 1905, cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909; dep. collr. of customs, Nov., 1911; on mil. duty, Jan., 1915; ag. govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1919; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1921; prin. collr., cust., Apr., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. contr., rev., 3rd Mar., 1927; contr., rev., 6th June, 1927; ag. col. sec., 29th May to 19th Aug., 1927.

TYSER, Sir C. R., Kt. Bach. (1909).—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; asst. ag. judic. comsnr. and leg. advsr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leewards, 1896-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1895; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 1906; ret., 1919; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evkaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejellej, 1901.

UNIACKE, Capt. Andrew Gore, D.S.O.—Served with N.W. mtd. pol., Canada (including Yukon expdn.), 1893-1898; served in S. African war, with Canadian forces and M.I., 1899-1902 (2 medals, 8 clasps, D.S.O.); dist. supt., N. Nigeria pol., 1903; served with expdn. against Okpoto, 1903-4 (A.G.S. medal and clasp); served with Nigeria Regt., 1916-18; inspr.-gen., N. Provs., Nigeria pol., 1919, King's pol. med., 1929.

UNWIN, Arthur Harold, D.Oc., M.E.F.A., F.R.G.S., M.C.S.F.E.—Forestry branch, dept. of interior, Canada, 1903-4; ass't conservator and senr. conservator of forests, Nigeria, 1904-17; office of woods, timber supply dept., England, 1917-19; prin. forest offr., Cyprus, 1921.
USHER, Capt. C. G., M.C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920; ag. sec., secretariat, 1927.

USHER, Walter Whitbread.—B. 1886; ed. at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; clk., Swaziland, 1910; registr. and master, sp. ct. of Swaziland, and Sheriff, 1916; dep. asst. comsnr., Mbabane, 1919; served in Anglo-Boer War, 1901, S.W. campaign, 1914-15 and Flanders, 1916-18.

VALINTINE, Thomas Habcourt Ambrose, C.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (R.C.S. and R.C.P., Lond.)—B. 1865; ed. Marlborough Coll. and St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; dist. health offr., N.Z., 1901; asst. ch. health offr., 1902; inspr.-gen. of hosps., 1907; ch. health offr., 1909; dir., mily. hosps., 1915-19; dir.-gen. of pub. health, 1921.

VAN DER MRULEN, Sir Frederick A., Kt. Bach. (1919); O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1875; ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Keble Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons. in mod. hist.), 1896; M.A., 1900; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1900; Westm. circuit; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 1907; solr.-gen., 1908; ag. atty.-gen. and also ag. puisne and circuit judge on various occasions, 1908-1911; ch. mag., Gambia, 1913; puisne judge, Nigeria, Oct., 1919.

VAN DER RIET, Frederick John Wrendly.—B. 1868; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A. (hons lit.), 1887; Cape civ. serv., 1887; LL.B., priv. study, 1894; admitted to practice as advoc., sup. ct., 1894; Transvaal, 1897; K.C., 1912; mem., Union parl., Dec., 1912 to Feb., 1923; judge, sup. ct., assigned to Eastern districts' local divn., Feb., 1923.

VAN DER SPUY, Adrian Johannes, M.B., Ch.B. (Glasgow), D.P.H. (Cape Town).—Asst. health offr. for Union of S. Africa, and D.D.M.S., Union Defence Forces; lt.-col., S.A.M.C.; late H.S. and H.P. Western Infirmary, Glasgow; capt. S.A.M.C. (overseas); O.C. mily. hosp., Wynberg, and Roberts Heights.
VAN DER STERR, WILLEM CORNELIS.—Dir., secondary triangulation, Union of S. Africa, 25th Aug., 1919; dir., trigonometrical survey, Union of S. Africa, 1st Oct., 1920.

VAN DE VELDE, MARCEL ARTHUR MAURICE.—Ed. at Mt. St. Mary's Coll., Derbyshire; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 29th May, 1900; tempy. ast. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda Rly., 9th Mar., 1903; ast. audr., Uganda Prot., June, 1903; ast. audr., E. Africa Prot. and Uganda Rly., 1904; local audr., Uganda Prot., 16th Nov., 1909; audr., Uganda Prot., 1st May, 1910; audr., Kenya, Oct., 1923.
VAN HEERDEN, SENATOR THE HON. H. C., M.L.A.—Formerly chmn. of comtes.; min. of agric., Union of S. Africa 1913-19; pres. of the senate since 1921.
VAN LANGENBERG, VINCENT, V.D., M.B.—B. 1870; port surg., Galle, May, 1898; ditto, Colombo, 1909; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Dec., 1922; dep. dir., med. and sanitary services, Nov., 1927.

VAN REENEN, REENEN JACOB, B.A., C.E., Assoc. M. Amer. Soc. C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. at S.A. Coll., Cape Town; at graduation obtained Victoria scholarship, proceeded to Lehigh Univ.; ast. engrn., irrigtn. dept., Cape Colony, Sept., 1909; ditto, Union of S. Africa; supt. of roads and local wks., O.F.S. Prov., May, 1912; mem. of Union Drought Investigation Comsn., 1920-24; chmn., Draught Investigation Comsn. of S.W. Africa, 1923-24; irrig. finances comsn., 1924-25; chmn., permanent drought comsn., 1926.

VAN RYNEVELD, COL. SIR PIERRE HELPERUS ANDRIAS, K.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918), M.C. (1916), B.A., B.Sc.—B. 1891; ed. Grey Coll., Grey Univ. Coll., Bloemfontein, Imp. Coll., Univ. of London; 2nd lieut., 7th Loyal N. Lancs. Regt., Aug., 1914; lieut., R.F.C., Apr., 1915; detachment C.O., Senussi campaign, Egypt, 1915-16; flight comdr., Palestine, 1916; ditto, Salonika, 1916; squad. comdr., London night defences, 1916-17; squad. comdr., No. 17 squad., B.E.F., 1917; severely wounded; wing comdr., XI. (Army) Wing, 2nd Army, 1917-19; ditto, Army wing, Rhine army of occupation, 1919; ment. in despatches six times, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med., Order of the Nile, 4th clas., Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur, France, Croix d'Officier, Ordre Leopold avec Croix de Guerre, Belgium; S. African liaison offr., Air Miny., 1919-21; lieut.-col. (temp. col.), Permanent Force Union Defence Forces, 1st Feb., 1920; director of air services, U.D.F., 1st Feb., 1920.
VAN SCHALKWIJK, JAN ZACHARIAS.—B.A.; inspr. of schls., O.F.S. Prov., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1917.

VAN SCHALKWIJK, L. M. A. N., B.A. (Cape), Ph.D. (Amst.).—B. 1888; ed. Normal and S. African Coll., Cape Town, also in Holland and Germany; teacher, Cape Prov., seven years; organising inspr., Union dept. of educn., 1921.

VAN VELDEN, DIRK ELIZA.—B. 1869; ed. at Gymnasium and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., auditor-gen.'s off., 1896; ast. sec., second volksraad, S. African Repub., 1896; sec. to ditto, 1899; sec. to Transvaal govt., Sept., 1900, to 1902; clk. ast. to legis. coun., Transvaal, 1907; clk. of the prov. coun., Transvaal, and clk. of the exec. comte., prov. sec., Transvaal Prov., 1918; author of "Peace Negotiations between Boer and Briton in South Africa."
VAN ZEYLEN, FREDERICK CORNELIS.—B. 1885; mech. and elec. engr., draughtsmen, harbour engrn.'s office, Nassau, Bahamas, 1923; ast. civ. engrn., 1925; dep. civ. engrn., D.P.W., May, 1928.

VAN ZIJL, HENDRIK STEFANUS, L.L.B. (Camb.), B.A., L.L.B. (Cape).—B. 1876; ed., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; advoc., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1903; M.P., Cape of Good Hope, 1906-10; party draftsman, Union of S. Africa, 1910-15; K.C., 1919; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920; chmn., delimitation comsn., 19th July, 1927.
VAN ZIJL, L. J., B.A.—B. 1887; ed., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; vice-prin., Boshof, 1910; prin., Smithfield, 1914; prin., Ladybrand, 1919; inspr. of schls., Orange Free State, 1921.

VAREY, JAMES ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1879; apprentice, city engrn.'s office, Leeds, 1896; ast. engrn., ditto, 1900; ditto, waterworks engrn.'s office, Leeds, 1905; irrigtn. engrn., irrigtn. dept., Ceylon, 1907; irrigtn. engrn., P.W.D., B. Guiana, 1913; dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon, Feb., 1920.

VASKESS, HENRY HARRISON.—B. 1891; clk., Western Pacific high comsn., 1st Apr., 1911; on mil'y. serv., 9th June, 1915; clk. in ch., accts., W. Pacific, 5th Sept., 1919; ch. clk. and acctnt., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. sec. to W. Pacific high comsn. for short periods in 1920, 1922, 1925 and 1927.
VASSALL, HENRY HOLLAND.—B. 1887; ed., Bedford Grammar Schol. and Keble Coll., Oxford; ast. res., Nyasaland, 1911; res., 1919; admstive offr., 1st grade, Dec., 1923.

VASSALLO, EDWIN P., L. S. & A.—B. 1891; ed., Flore's Coll. and Malta Univ.; grad. as land survr. and architect, 1914; elec. for 7th divn. in leg. assem., Oct., 1921; re-el., June, 1924 and Aug., 1927; min. for pub. wks., 16th Aug., 1927; edr. "The Malta Times and Progress" until Aug., 1927.

VASSALLO, S., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Ed. Malta Lyceum and Malta Univ.; M.D., 1913; govt. exhibn. for science, 1906-1907; ditto for medicine, 1909-1913; post-graduate work at Univ. Coll. Hosp. and London Hosp.; clinical ast., St. John's Hosp., W., and Royal Ear Hosp.; supernumerary med. offr., Leeward Is., 1914; D.T.M. and H. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M. (Duncan med.); temp. comsn., R.A.M.C. (ment. in despatches); med. offr., Uganda, 1919; surg. speo., Zanzibar Prot., 1925.

VATHIANATHAN, KANTHIAR.—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., June, 1923; ast., Colombo kach., July, 1923; ditto Ratnapura kach., May, 1924; ag. office ast., ditto, Sept., 1924; extra office ast., ditto, Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1925; ditto, Avissawella, February, 1926; office ast., Galle kach., Nov., 1926.

VAUGHAN, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1892; ed., Kastbourne Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, B.A. (hist. tripes), 1914; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-21; M.C., Arras, 1917; ast. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to Br. res. and clk. of the coun., July, 1924; ag. regis., high ct., 1927.

VELLACOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOOT, M.B. Lond. (hons. in for. and obst. med.), 1896; B.S., 1896; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; F.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901.—House surg., res. obstetrician and clin. ast., Guy's hosp.; ast. curator of museum, St. George's hosp.; civ. surg., S. African field force. 1901 to 1902; capt., S.A. Constaby., 6th June, 1902; transf'd. to O.R.C. govt. serv. as res. med. offr.; National hosp., Bloemfontein, 1st Dec., 1905; fellow of R. Inst. of pub. health; deleg. from O.R.C. to 2nd Internat. Congress on School Hygiene.
VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC.—Cadet, S. Sttlm., Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Dingdingo, Feb., 1922.
VENIOT, PETER JOHN.—Mem. New Brunswick legislature, 1894-1900; re-elec., 1917 and min. of pub. wks.; premier, New Brunswick, 1923-26; postmr.-gen. of Canada, 1926.

VENN, HARRY NORMAN.—West Australian service, 1895-1900; S. African war (2 medals, 5 clasps); Imperial army pay dept. in Australia, 1902-03; ch. clk. and acctnt., dist. hosps. dept., Transvaal, Aug., 1903; pub. health dept., Transvaal, 1904; acctnt., col. sec.'s dept., 1905; acctnt., dept. of interior, May, 1910; ag. under-sec. for interior, Union of S. Africa, 1915; under-sec. for the Interior, Dec., 1919; commnr. for immigrn. and Asiatic affrs., and prot., Indian immigrn., Natal, May, 1927.

VENNING, Guy, M.B.E. (1929).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf'd. to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; conf. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; staff offr., 9th Jan., 1928.

VENNING, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1876; ed. Worthing Coll.; Matabele Rebellion, 1896; apptd. to dist. admintr., N.W. Rhodesia, June, 1907; asst. native comantr., Apr., 1908; native comantr., Apr., 1908; asst. mag., N.R., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., Barotse dist., Mar., 1915-Mar., 1916; has acted as mag. on various occasions between Apr. 1919 and Aug. 1924; mag., Aug., 1924; J.P.

VENOUR, LOUIS STEPHEN DOUGLAS HAMILTON.—B. 1886; ed. ptly.; capt., 5th batt., Middx. Regt.; asst. comantr., pol., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. comantr., pol., Aug., 1915; ag. staff instr., in 1916 and 1919; comantr., pol., Jan., 1917; King's Pol. Med., 1922; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., pol., in 1926, 1927 and 1928; ag. comantr., pol. C.I.D., June to Nov., 1928.

VERKER, STANDISH HENRY PRENDERGAST.—B. 1878; served in I.Y. in S. African War, 1900-01 (ment. in despatches), awarded medal ("For distinguished conduct in the field"); served under F.O. as British vice-consul, and frequently as acting consul, Cherbourg, Jan., 1902 to Sept., 1905; assisted partly comittee on vagrancy, Nov., 1905, to Mar., 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Sept., 1906.

VERITY, JOHN.—B. 1892; barrister-at-law, copyist, treasy. and cust., Br. Honduras, 1908; 3rd cls. clk., regist. gen.'s office, 1910; 2nd cls. clk., atty. gen's office, 1912; resig., 1913; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1918; dep. clk., etc., St. Catharine, Jamaica, 1918; clk., etc., Trelawny, 1920; ag. res. mag. for various periods, 1922-26; res. mag. for various periods 1922-26; res. mag., Hanover, 1926; ag. judge, Kingston et al., 1927.
VERNEY, FRANK ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1874; vet. offr., Natal, 1901-08; prin. vet. offr., Basutoland, 1908.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES, C.B. (1924).—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oxford; schol. of Balliol, 1895-9; 1st cls. mods., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cls. lit., hum., 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., olk. inseec.'s dept., inland rev., Nov., 1899; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 13th Aug., 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot, comantr., 1903; mem. managing comittee of E.I.O.; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Elgin., Jan., 1906; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 16th Apr., 1908; read. S. African Union medal, 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Denman, gov. gen. of Australia, 1911-12; 1st cls. clk., 9th Nov., 1912; visited and reported on British Solomon Islands, Nov.-Dec., 1911; represented H.M.G. on Anglo-French New Hebrides conferece, May-Aug., 1914; transf'd. to treasy., Oct., 1914; lent to Min. of Munitions, June, 1915; asst. gen. sec., 1916; visited France and Belgium on sp. serv., Feb., 1917; asst. sec., Irish Convention, June, 1917 to Mar., 1918; ret'd. to treasy., May, 1918; dep. acct-gen., B. of E., Apr., 1920; lent to C.O., Middle East dept. as asst. sec., finan. and economic, Apr., 1921; visited Egypt, Palestine and Trans-Jordania on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv. at Lausanne for Turkish Peace Treaty, Nov. to Dec., 1922 and June, 1923; transf'd. to permanent staff of C.O. as asst. sec., Jan., 1924; seconded for service as finan. adviser to govt. of Iraq, 1925; returned to C.O., 1st Oct., 1928; mem. Palestine Cur. Board, 1928.

VERRY, CECIL TYRRELL.—B. 1885; ed. Malvern; clk., treas., Nyassaland, 1909; transf'd. dist. admintr., 1900; res., 3rd grade, 1910; ag. A.D.C. to gov., 1915-18; temp. lieut., K.A.R., 1916; res., 1918; admintr. offr., 1st grade, July, 1923; ag. prov. comantr., Aug., 1923.
VERSCHAFFELT, PAUL DESIRÉ NESTOR, L.L.B., F.I.A.N.Z.—B. 1887; grad. in law, Victoria Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. pub. serv., 1904; asst. pub. serv. comantr., 1921; pub. serv. comantr., 1923.

VIADER, RÉNE.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll., Mauritius; ent. civ. serv., Mauritius, Nov. 1899; regisr., col. sec's office, May, 1919; finan. asst., P.W.D., Mar., 1923; ch. clk., col. sec's office, Apr., 1925; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 1926-26.

VICTORIA, RIGHT REV. GERARD HEATH LANDER, Lord Bishop of Victoria, Hong Kong. B. 1861; ed. at Trin. Coll. and Ridley Hall, Camb., B.A., 1884; M.A., 1888; D.D., 1907; consec. at Lambeth Parish Church, 29th June, 1907; curate of St. Bride's, Liverpool, 1884 to 1888; vicar of St. Benedict's, Liverpool, 1888 to 1896; of Litherland, 1896 to 1905; of St. Cyprian's, Liverpool, 1905 to 1907; chrmn., Litherland schld., 1898 to 1903; chaplain to Liverpool dist. lay readers, 1892 to 1905.
VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. dist. comantr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912; 1st grade admtrive. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924.

VILJOEN, WILLEM JACOBUS, M.A., L.N.C., Phil. D.—B. 1869; ed. at Normal Coll., Cape-town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and S. African Coll., Capetown; apptd. to staff, Normal Training Coll., Capetown, 1889; after graduation studied at Univ. of Amsterdam, Leyden, Strasbourg, and in 1894 took degree of Phil. D. (in modern languages and comparative philology); prof., mod. languages and hist., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1895-1903; prof. of Dutch (lang. and lit.) ditto, now Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1903-10; elec. mem. of coun. of C. of G.H. Univ., 1897; mem., Maatschappij der Nederlandse, Leiden, 1900; rep. Cape Univ. at quincentenary of Leipzig Univ., 1899; dir., educn., O.F.S., 1910-18; mem. and chmn., of Grey Univ. Coll. coun., Bloemfontein, 1911-18; rep. Union of S. Africa at Impl. Educn. Conf., in London, 1911 and 1923; first vice-chmn., Univ. of S. Africa, 1918; supt. gen. of educn., Cape Prov., 1918; mem., coun., Huguenot Univ. Coll., Wellington, 1919; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1923; author of several standard wks. on Dutch language and simplified orthography.
