Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; ag. inspr. of schls., Perak; passed in Malay, 1904; in law, Oct., 1904; ag. 2nd aast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1904; ag. aast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. 2nd aast. dist. offr., Tapah, Oct., 1909; ag. aast. dist. offr., Matang, and insp. of fisheries, Jan., 1908; class V., Jan., 1910; ag. aast. dist. offr., Gopeng, Apr., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Sept., 1912; sec. to comte. for Malay studies in addition, Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Dec., 1913; federal examiner in Malay in addition, Dec., 1913; promoted to class III., aast. dir. of education, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sanitary bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addition; cls. II., Nov., 1919; ag. dir. educn., S. Stiltna., and F.M.S., Feb., 1920; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stiltna., and F.M.S., June, 1920; ag. chmn., comte. for Malay studies, F.M.S., 1920; seconded to act as prin. of Raffles' Coll., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., May, 1923; offr. as sec. to high comsrr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addition, Mar., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. I., July, 1924; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Sawa and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melayu.
WINSTON, V. A.—B. 1903; ag. 3rd cls., post office, Dominica, 1920; asst. cls., P.W.D., Sept., 1920; jnr. audit. cls., Sept., 1924; ag. audit cls., Jan., Apr., 1927.

WISE, KENNICK STANTON.—B. 1881; ed Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C. Lond. Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and regis.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, 4th Aug., 1919.

WISEMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schol. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cls. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909; apptd. after compet. exam., cls., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. cls., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comte., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917, to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comte., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comte., May, 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. cls., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.K.E.—B. 1880; dir., rlyw. and elec. dept., Zanzibar, 12th Dec., 1911; dir., wireless telegraph, 1st Jan., 1914; 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WITTEENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resign. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resign. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901; M.L.C.

WOODEHOUSE, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSCELIN HENRAGE, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsrr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; comd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, P. P. J., C.I.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., regen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offr., 1900; ast. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1906; pol. mag., N.T. (Islands), Sept., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem. of bd. of examiners, 1907; ag. dep. supt. of pol. and fire brig., 1907, 1908 to 1909, 1911 to 1912; census offr., 1911; dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1912; aide-de-camp to gov., 1918 to 1919; ag. capt. supt. of pol., on various occasions, 1918-25.

WODEMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office ast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; ast. commr. of excise attached to the office of the excise commr., Apr., 1913; ast. commr. of excise, Cent. Div., Jan., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ag. ast. govt. agt., Matale, Aug., 1915; attached to chief censor's office, Sept., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matare, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. commr., excise, Nov., 1926; ag. commr., excise, Sept., 1927.

WOLFE, EDWARD DUDLEY CORSCADEN, C.M.G. (1928).—Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. lang., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. lang., tripes. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S. 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., P.W.D. enquiry commr., 1901; registr., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. ast. registr.-gen., 1902; registr., land ct., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and ast. supt. of pol., 1903-5; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907; ag. registr.-gen., 1907; ast. registr.-gen., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; capt. supt. of pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; attended internat. pol. confce., New York, 1925.

WOLFE, HENRY, M.Sc. (Agric.) Cornell Univ., U.S.A.—B. 1897; tech. ast. dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17.

WOLFFER, PHINEAS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Academy; clk., land dept., R.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land ast., 1919; registr. of titles, documents and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, JAMES FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut. Royal Berks. Regt.; lieut., S. African Constab.; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's and King's medals); capt., K.A.R.; served in E. Africa, 1914-1918 ("1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals); commanded a column in operations against Aulihan (Somali) tribe, Jubaland, 1917-18 (M.C., African Gen. Serv. medal); ag. ast. commr., pol., Kenya, 1927.

WOOD, A. E. Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; 2nd lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, 1908; ag. ast. registr.-gen., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. ast. registr.-gen., Apr.-May, 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., N.T., 1910 to 1911; 2nd ast. registr.-gen., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, 1911 to 1912; passed in Hakka, June, 1912; title of ast. registr.-gen. altered to 2nd ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. 1st ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. chief ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1914; ag. dist. offr., 1915; ag. chief ast. to S.C.A., 1916, and 1918 to 1919; passed in Pekingese, June, 1916; sent to Japan to study Japanese, 1916; passed in Japanese, May, 1918; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo, 1919 to 1920; ag. ch. ast. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo in addn., 1922; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1924; ag. dir., educn., Dec., 1925; dir., educn., Jan., 1926.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge schl., Sid. Suss. Coll. Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; ast. conservator, Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conservator, 1918; conservator of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925.

WOOD, HARRY EDWIN.—B. 1881; ed. Manchester Grammar Schl. and Owens Coll. Manchester. (B.Sc., 1902, M.Sc., 1905); ast. lect., dept. of physics, Manchester Univ., 1905; ch. ast., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1906; ch. ast., Union observatory, Johannesburg, 1912; served, German E. Africa campaign, 1916; Union astronomer, Jan., 1928.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripes. 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; called to the bar, 1919; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; registr. of land ct., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1903-4; ast. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. ast. reg.-gen., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1908; dep. registr. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., 1911 to 1912; ag. 1st pol. mag., on several occasions; 1st pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920-22 and in 1925; chmn., Sui An piracy comm., 1922; puisne judge, 1925; ag. ch. just., 1927.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queen's coll., Camb.; 1st ela. math. tripes., pt. I; 2nd els. math. tripes., pt. 2; B.A., 1912; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Aug., 1914; ag. dist. commr., 1914-20; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1921; ag. clk. of couns., Feb., 1922.
WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MEVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commdr., 1923; ast. treas., Kenya, 1923.

WOOD, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1896; ed. Abingdon schl.; served with Royal Berkshire Regt., 1915-20; France, 1916-19; Egypt, 1919-20; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Apr., 1918; ment. in desp., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 16th Apr., 1921; attd., dist. office, Port Dickson, Oct., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1923; ditto, Kuantan, Mar., 1924; ag. supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1925; dist. offr., Pt. Dickson, Apr., 1927; offr. cls. IV, Apr., 1928.
WOODFORD, HON. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Harbor Main, Newfoundland, 1889; chmn., bd. of works, 1897; min. of public works, 1898, 1909, and 1918; M.E.C., 1918.

WOODHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylors Sch.; astt. transport offr., with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, Nov., 1914; ment. in despatches for service in German E. Africa, Nov., 1916; astt. dist. commnr., Uganda, Dec., 1916; 2nd grade adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1920; dist. offr., June, 1926.

WOODS, A. E., R.D., R.N.R.—B. 1874; extra master's certif., 1897; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1903; lieut., 1906; lieut.-commdr., 1914; commr., 1919 (retd.); 1st and gunnery offr. H.M.S. "Naid" and "Oropesa," 1914-15; commanded H.M.S.Y. "St. George" sp. serv., Mediterranean, 1915; commanded destroyer divn., extended defence Channel patrol, 1917; ch. exam. offr., Gibraltar, 1918; mast. of "Lord Milner," Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1919; mast. of "Azania," Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1925.

WOODS, PERCY SINCLAIR COMYN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Michael's Sch., Lyme Regis, and Durham Univ.; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Somerset House, Jan., 1903; astt. auditor, Lagos and S. Nigeria, Apr., 1905; dep. ch. acctnt., G. Coast rly., Jan., 1909; ag. ch. acctnt., Mar. to Sept., 1909; promoted rly. acctnt. on abolition of office of ch. acctnt., Jan., 1910; col. treas., Feb., 1922.

WOODS, WILFRID WENTWORTH, C.M.G. (1926)—B. 1876; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schl., mod. hist., 1901; olk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Sept., 1901; astt. auditor, G. Coast, Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1904; astt. col. auditor, Ceylon, Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at the War Office, Dec., 1917 to 1919; ag. col. treas., May to Nov., 1920; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., Sept. to Oct., 1926.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Kandy Arch., June, 1921; ag. office astt. to gov't.; agt. N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. O.O., Feb. to June, 1926; addnl. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd astt. col. sec., Oct., 1926; astt. gov't. astt. N'Elia, May, 1927; 2nd astt. col. sec. and olk., leg. coun., Sept., 1928.

WORKMAN, CHARLES RUFUS MARSHALL, C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1874; ed. Christ Ch., Oxford, M.A.; called to bar, Inner Temple; pol. offr., Ocean Is., 1909; dep. commnr., Western Pacific, 1911; admtr., Nauru, 1914; ag. res. commnr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1916 to Feb., 1917; ag. res. commnr., Solomon Is., June, 1917; res. commnr., Apr., 1919; judi. commnr., Feb., 1920; col. sec., Gambia, Sept., 1921; ag. gov't. on various occasions, 1921-28.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; astt. contr. of lab., Parker, July, 1917; astt. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; astt. dir. of educon. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; astt. dir., educon. (Chinese), S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1924; ag. prot. Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb., 1925 and Nov., 1926; astt. dir., educon. (Chinese), F.M.S. and S.S., Jan., 1927; ag. dir., educon., F.M.S. and S.S., Apr., July, 1927; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang in addn., Feb., 1928.

WORLEDGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1896; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in despatches; Ireland, 1919; astt. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; astt. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. astt. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. astt. audr., Nov., 1925.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. Oxon.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 12th Nov., 1910; attd. Colombo kach., Dec., 1910; attd., col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office astt. to gov't. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; astt. land settm. offr., Jan., 1912; office astt. to gov't. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; astt. censor, Dec., 1914; on mily. duty, Feb., 1915; agt. astt. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1919; astt. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; agt. gov't. agt., Prov. of Uva, Aug. to Oct., 1922; astt. stimt. offr., Jan., 1924; astt. gov't. agt., Hambantota, May, 1924; ag. gov't. agt., Batticaloa, May, 1928.

WORTHINGTON, ARTHUR FURLEY.—B. 1874; ed. at Tonbridge and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1910; cadet, Perak, Nov., 1897; passed cadet, Feb., 1900; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, June, 1900; astt. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Feb., 1902; offr., cls. VI., Jan., 1903; ag. ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, June, 1905; offr., cls. V., Sept., 1904; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1908; offg. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1910; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, July, 1917, Lower Perak, Feb., 1919; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Mar., 1920; offg. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan. to June, 1921; Br. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Mar.-Aug., 1922; appt. of Br. adviser, Kelantan raised to "staff," July, 1924; on sp. duty, England, Apr., 1924; Br. adviser, Kelantan, May, 1925; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Dec., 1925; Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1926.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCELYN, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, gov't. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. soc'y., 1906; jnr. astt. chem., Jamaica, 1902; astt. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmstr., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; commnr. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food commnr., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comtees, on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyasaland, Dec., 1920; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."
WRATISLAW, JOHN CYRIL.—B. 1894; ed. Oundle Sch.; astt. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, 14th Jan., 1924.

WRIGHT, ANDREW BARKWORTH, M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918).—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; astt. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 3rd June, 1922; astt. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; ag. priv. sec. to high commnr., Apr. to June and Sept. to Oct., 1924; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., June to Sept., 1924; ag. ch. astt. sec., reg'r. of co-op. societies and reg'r. of co-op. credit societies, Apr. to Oct. 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1924; ditto, Turkish, Apr. 1925; ag. ch. asst. sec. for various periods, 1926-28; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G July-Oct., 1926; ag. col. sec., in 1927 and 1928.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR ALBAN, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. St. Edmund's Schol., Canterbury; open exhibnr., St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1906; lieut., 3rd Bn, Bedfordshire Regt. (S.R.), 1909; 2nd cls. hons. schl., mod. hist., Oxford, 1910; B.A., 1910; cadet, Fiji, 1912; ag. sub-inspr., constab., 1912; ag. clk., ex. coun. and 1st cls. clk., col. sec's. office, 1913; passed cadet, 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Rewa and prov. comsnr., Naitasiri, 1915; 2nd grade dist. comsnr., 1915; dist. comsnr., Nadi, 1915; dist. comsnr., Colo East, 1916; on mily. duty, 1917; 2nd lieut., 5th Bn. Rifle Brigade, 1917; on active serv., France, 1917; dist. comsnr., Macuata, 1920; ditto (1st grade), Nadroga and prov. comsnr., Nadroga and Colo West, 1922; ditto, Colo North, 1923; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1927; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1928.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR EDGAR.—B. 1880; Fellow of the Surveyors' Institution; ed. at Tiffin's Endowed Schol., Kingston-on-Thames; asst. engrnt., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 5th May, 1903; exec. engrnt., 1911; ag. assessor in addition, 1915; exec. engrnt., 1st grade, 1917; seconded to Weihaiwei on special serv., 1918 and 1920; ag. asst. D.P.W., 1923.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD—M.A., F.I.C., F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripod), 1902; M.A., 1916; asst. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. govt. analyst and prof. of chemistry, Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, H. PULLEW.—Served in S. Africa war in I.Y. and S.A.C., 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 3 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); staff offr. to O.C., Riet River div., O.R.C.; dist. comsnr., Edenburg and other districts, O.R.C., 1903-1908; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 15th October, 1908; district comsnr., June, 1914.

WRIGHT, HERBERT ARTHUR.—B. 1882; instr., govt. technical schol., Accra, G. Coast, 10th Apr., 1909; principal, ditto, 28th Sept., 1911; attached to Togoland field force, 16th Aug. to 8th Sept., 1914; in joint charge of detention camp for German prisoners of war, 8th Dec., 1914 to 13th Jan., 1915; asst. censor, 2nd Oct., 1914 to 2nd Feb., 1915, and 2nd Oct., 1915 to 25th Feb., 1916; prov. inspr. of schls., 25th July, 1917; ag. senr. inspr. of schls., 29th July to 19th Sept., 1917; asst. dir., educn., 1st April, 1926.

WRIGHT, JAMES ROGERS.—B. 1875; ed. at C.M.S Grammar Schol., Sierra Leone; 2nd clk., queen's advco. office, June, 1895; transfd., gov's office, July, 1895; asst. mast., circuit ct., Oct., 1903; pol. clk. and clk., ct. of request, Nov., 1908; J.P. for Freetown pol. dist., July, 1909; dep. coroner, Freetown pol. dist., Aug., 1909 and in Apr., 1928; ch. clk. master's office, Jan., 1914; comsnr. of oaths and affidavits, sup. ct., Nov., 1915; dep. mast. and regiar., sup. ct. and regiar., prize ct., May, 1920; comsnr., ct. of requests, Freetown pol. dist., Apr., 1924; mast. and regiar., sup. ct., regiar., trade marks, curator of intestate estates and adminstr., civ. servants' estates, May, 1924.
WRIGHT, HON. ROBERT ALEXANDER.—Mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1908-11 and since 1914; min. of educn., 1926.

WRIGHT, VICTOR WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; served with R. Naval Divn. in Gallipoli and France; wounded, 1914-16; lieut., R.E. and general staff, Macedonia, Caucasus, Trans-Caspia, Constantinople, 1916-20; mast. technical schol., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Schol., stud. engnr. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E. (S.R.), 1909; engnr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi riv. and water wks., 1911-14; R.K., 1914-22; served Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E., Londonderry and Kninskille Divn., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. pub. wks., Antigua, July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, Aug., 1922; asst., D.P.W., B. Guiana, Dec., 1926; ag. D.P.W., Apr. to Sept., 1927.
WROUGHTON, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1877; med. offr., Basutoland, 1908.

WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, 18th Oct., 1909; asst. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, 27th Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., 8th May, 1923.

WYATT, ALEXANDER HENRY LOUIS.—Ed. Wellington; served in Impl. Yeomanry, S. African War, 1900-01; Queen's Med., 4 clasps; W.O., 1901-04; asst. res., Nyasaland, 1904-14; seconded for serv. with 1st K.A.R., 1914-17; ment. in desps. and awarded M.C.; served in Nyasaland rising, 1915; A.G.S.M. and clasp; dist. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1917; senr. comsnr., Nov., 1922; prov. comsnr., Mar., 1926.

WYNNE-JONKS, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1896; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; ag. dist. offr., South, June, 1922; attd. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923; ditto, South, Dec., 1926; attd., col. secretariat, July, 1923.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins., from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Bobt. Borden; mast. deputy minister of railways and canals, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MERVYN ST. JOHN, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; regnr.-gen. Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. magis., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1923 to Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Kingston et., Nov., 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1925; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1928.

YOUULL, JOSEPH.—Seconded from Impl. serv. to B. E. Africa and Uganda Prots. as 1st grade dist. survr., 1st Jan., 1913 to 17th Dec., 1915; seconded to G. Coast, 31st May, 1916; offr.-in-charge, post and telegraph dept., Togoland, 22nd May, 1918 to 23rd Jan., 1919, and from 12th Aug., 1919.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED KARNEY, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1890; ag. reg'nr.-gen., 1896-7; dist. comntr., Orange Walk, 1898; reg'nr.-gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admnstr. govt., Apr., to Nov., 1911, and from 16th to 27th Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1915 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; chief just., Fiji, 1923.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON; G C.M.G. (1916); K.C.M.G. (1908); K.B.E. (1918), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdtn., mil. pol. Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; asst. comntr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; comntr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admnstr. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1896; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec., S. Stilmta., June, 1906; admnstr. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Stilmta., 8th Aug., 1911; assumed govt., 2nd Sept., 1911; also high comntr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. 25th Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; comntr. of crown lands and immigrtn., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comtes. for stintm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. at Derby Schl. and Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; res. mag., 1917; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Aug., 1921.

YOUNG, MAJOR HUBERT WINTHROP, C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Milly. Acad., Woolwich; comand., R. Garrison Arty, 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir. local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejaz opera- tions, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Nahda); pres., local resources bd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; seconded to F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; seconded to C.O., Middle East Dep. as asst. sec., Mar., 1921 to Dec., 1926; accompanied Mr. Churchill to Cairo confce., Apr., 1921; visited Palestine and Iraq on ap. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty., Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gibraltar, Feb., 1927.

YOUNG, JAMES.—B. 1869; ed. Ayr Acad.; served in Bechuanaland Border Pol., 1890-93; served in Bechuanaland Field Force, Dec., 1896 to Oct., 1897; clk., office of commissary of ordinance C.C., Nov., 1897; Bechuanaland Re- bellion, 1897, (med. and clasp); Anglo-Boer War, 1890-1900, (Queen's med. and 2 clasps); served in Bechuanaland Rifles, 1899-1900; acct., mily. adminstr., W. Transvaal, Dec., 1900; clk., mag.'s office, Zeerust, June, 1901; A.R.M., Standerton, Sept., 1902; detached A.R.M. Ventersdorp, Sept., 1903; relieving mag. Trans- vaal, Nov., 1903; senr. A.R.M. (detached) Krugerdorp, May, 1904; ag. 2nd civ. mag., Johannesburg, Sept., 1905; ag. 3rd ditto, Sept., 1906; A.R.M. (detached), Boksburg, Dec., 1906; A.R.M. (detached), Krugerdorp, Apr., 1907; R.M., Germiston, July, 1909; inpg. mag., Transvaal, Griqualand West and Bechuanaland, Nov., 1915; civ. mag., Johannesburg, Nov., 1916; mag., Pieterburg, Nov., 1922; mag., Johannesburg, Feb., 1925.
YOUNG, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—Mem. N.Z. parliament since 1911; min. of health, 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univs.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; asst. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, MARK AITCHISON.—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. ast. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. ast. govt. agt., Mullativu, Jan., 1913; 4th ast. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd ast. sec. col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd ast. col. sec., July, 1920; ast. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. ast. col. sec. and clk., exec. coun., Mar., 1923; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 1928.

YOUNG, MAJOR-GEN. ROBERT, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1915).—Volunteer serv. in N.Z.; left N.Z. with main body, N.Z. Exp. Force with rank of major, 1914; temp. lieut.-col., May, 1915; O.C. (temp.) 1st batt., Auckland Regt., May-Nov., 1915; lieut.-col., Nov., 1915; in command, 1st batt., Canterbury Regt., Nov., 1915 to Aug., 1917; comdr. (temp.), 2nd N.Z. Infy. Brig., Dec., 1916 to Jan. 1917; brig.- gen. (temp.), Aug., 1917; in command, 1st N.Z. infy. brig., Dec., 1917 to Feb., 1918; in command, 2nd N.Z. infy. brig., Feb., 1918 to Apr., 1919; comdr., "A" group, N.Z. Exped. Force, Sling Camp, Apr. to July, 1919; col., N.Z. staff corps, Oct., 1919; O.C., Canterbury dist., Oct., 1919 to Jan., 1921; O.C., Southern Command, Feb., 1921 to Dec., 1925; col.-comdt., 1921; major-gen. and comdt., N.Z. mil'y. forces, Dec., 1925; chevalier of Legion of Honour; five times ment. in desps.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.CH. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhibir., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. astt. children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Ealing hosp., 1922-23; East Africs med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923; ag. R.S.O., Zanzibar, July, 1927.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to govrs. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. astt. govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agt. N.W. dist., 1895; astt. col. sec., Mauritius, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; comsmr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admstr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govr., Leeward Is., 1909; adminstr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. govr., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govr., Falkland Is., 28th Dec., 1914; assumed govt., 15th May, 1915; ret., 1920.

ZAMMIT, HON. PROFESSOR THEMISTOCLES, C.M.G. (1911), M.D. (hon.) D.Litt. (Oxon).—B. 1864; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1882; grad. in medicine, 1889; govt. analyst, 1891-1920; curator, Valletta Museum, 1903; prof. of chemistry, Malta Univ., 1905-20; mem. Mediterranean fever comsn., 1904-07; rector, Malta Univ., July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. coun., 1920-21; hon. fellow, Royal Anthropological Inst., 1919; local sec. (Malta) of Soc'y. of Antiquaries, 1921; medallist (Mary Kingsley), Liverpool Schl. of Trop. Med.; delegate of Malta govt. at Imp. Educn. Conf., London, June to July, 1923.
ZIAI, MUSTAFA FUAD.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1922; inspr., Cyprus mil'y. pol., Nov., 1906; judge, dist. ct., Apr., 1913; puisne judge, Oct., 1927.
